description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The school's mission is to enable every student to fully unfold their potential through diverse, learning-promoting offers tailored to individual needs, guiding them successfully to school completion.[1][4] Core values emphasize individuality, self-reliance, and community, with an educational approach centered on individualized, cross-disciplinary learning in Lernbüros (learning offices) at students' own pace, supported by learning paths, logbooks, and regular development discussions.[1][3] It promotes self-organization, responsibility, motivation, and future readiness via digital platforms, multiprofessional support (e.g., school social work), local partnerships for practical projects and career orientation, and a future rhythmized full-day schedule balancing learning, practice, and recovery.[1][3]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
The school opened for the 2025/26 school year as part of Berlin's school construction offensive, starting at a transitional site (Erich-Kästner-Str. 52, 12619 Berlin, shared with Gymnasium Kaulsdorf) with five 7th-grade classes, expanding to a six-form Integrated Secondary School (ISS) for grades 7-10 (600 students total).[1][4][5][7] The permanent modern Compartment school building at Garzauer Str. 30, 12683 Berlin (Biesdorf, Marzahn-Hellersdorf district), is under construction by HOWOGE, featuring flexible spaces, digital equipment, open teaching areas, specialized rooms for sciences, art, music, sports, a library, a three-field sports hall, youth multi-purpose room, aula, terrace, and barrier-free access for inclusive full-day learning; move-in is planned for summer 2026.[2][3][4][5][7] It serves Biesdorf and Kaulsdorf communities, cooperating with local partners like pad e.V. (full-day offers), wetek (career orientation), and Studienkreis (learning support).[3]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Currently offers grade 7 (2025/26), expanding to grades 7-10 as an ISS, which provides all secondary qualifications up to Abitur (after 13 years, unlike Gymnasium's 12), including MSA and pathways to BBR via dual learning in "Wirtschaft, Arbeit, Technik" (WAT) focusing on work and career preparation.[3][6][7] Features self-organized learning in Lernbüros, transparent grading with central exams in math, German, English, second foreign language (French or Spanish), Wahlpflichtbereich (elective profiles in digital, languages, sports), and gymnasiale Oberstufe planned.[3][6][7] As a full-day Ganztagsschule, it integrates structured full-day programs until 16:00 with education, care, and open/ bound offers.[6]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Emphasizes personalized, networked learning with modern digital platforms, flexible rooms, and innovative formats.[1][4] Full-day rhythm includes recovery, deepening, and extracurriculars via partners; practical projects, career orientation, and Erasmus+ EU program for international exchanges, culture, and language experiences.[1][3][6] Sports in three-field hall and outdoor areas; arts/music in specialized rooms; active school culture with student/parent involvement.[2][3]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific awards, rankings, or outcome data (e.g., exam results) mentioned, as the school is newly opened in 2025/26 and still building its community and programs.[1][4][7] Designated an Erasmus+ School for international projects.[1]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a public tuition-free school, enrollment follows Berlin public secondary school processes (e.g., via Oberschulbroschüre events); central info at digital Open Day on 28.02.2026.[1][8] Currently starting with grade 7; contact Sekretariat for details (sekretariat@10k15.schule.berlin.de, +49 30 303233135).[1][4]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The school's mission emphasizes collaborative shaping of a positive future ("Gemeinsam eine gute Zukunft gestalten") through an innovative, student-centered approach in a new facility designed for active participation.[2][Main Page] It promotes unfolding individual potentials and strengths, with students actively shaping their school environment ("Gestaltet eure Schule aktiv mit! Entfaltet eure Potentiale und Stärken und nehmt eure Zukunft selbst in die Hand").[2] Core values include modern technical equipment, diverse and flexible instruction across subjects and grades, and a supportive structure via **Compartments**—eight small schools within the larger one, featuring connected classrooms, division rooms, and a central **Forum** for meeting, working, and relaxing.[2][1]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded formally on August 1, 2024 (school number 11K16), the school opened in September 2025 as a newly constructed **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** with gymnasial upper secondary level, replacing the former Stauffenberg-Oberschule site (demolished in 2002).[1][5][6] Built over 2.5 years as part of Berlin's school construction offensive on a 9,800 m² site at the corner of Am Breiten Luch, Wartenberger Straße, and Falkenberger Chaussee, it is a five-story, barrier-free building meeting standards for inclusion, all-day operations, and sustainability, with capacity for 725 students.[3][7][10] Facilities include 34 classrooms, modern technical equipment, two sports halls with six sections, outdoor areas, an inner courtyard, and **Check-In** time from 7:15-8:45 AM for relaxed preparation.[2][3][Main Page] Located in Berlin-Lichtenberg (Neu-Hohenschönhausen), it serves 234 students and 48 teachers, with school social work by SPIK e.V. supporting counseling, conflict resolution, leisure activities, and social learning.[1][2]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As an **ISS with gymnasial Oberstufe**, it currently serves grades 7-8 in 2025/26, expanding to grades 7-13, including Sekundarstufe I (5 classes per grade) and II (3 classes per grade), leading to pathways like Abitur.[1][6][7] The compartment-based structure enables varied, cross-grade, and interdisciplinary instruction in a full-day program (07:00-15:00, extendable).[2] Daily schedule: Check-In (8:15-8:45), Block 1 (8:45-10:15), Compartment break (10:15-10:30), Block 2 (10:30-12:00), Lunch (12:00-12:45), Block 3 (12:45-14:15), Compartment break (14:15-14:30), Block 4 (14:30-16:00).[Curriculum Page] Profiles include berufsorientiertes (career-oriented) and gesundheitsbetontes (health-focused).[4] No specific languages or special programs detailed beyond digital age focus and social learning.[2][1]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
The compartment model fosters community living and learning within small groups, with forums for relaxation and collaboration, plus early arrival for stress-free starts and peer/teacher exchanges.[2][Main Page] School social work offers leisure in breaks, team-building, conflict mediation, and support for student representation (GSV).[1] Full-day operations include outdoor areas and sports facilities for physical activity; a video showcases the setup.[2][3] Student involvement in school design emphasizes agency in a "Digital Age." No explicit extracurriculars, sports teams, arts, or exchanges mentioned.[2]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No awards, rankings, certifications, or outcome data (e.g., exam results) are mentioned, as the school is new and in build-up phase; perspectives focus on future gymnasial upper secondary development.[6] Capacity and modern design highlight infrastructure achievements via Berlin's school offensive.[3][7]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a tuition-free public school (**Öffentlich**), enrollment follows Berlin public secondary school processes, with no fees or special requirements noted.[School Information][6] Open house: January 10, 2026, 10:00-13:00.[2][6] Contact for inquiries: Phone 030/26393590, email 11K16@11k16.schule.berlin.de.[2]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The school's motto is **"Alle. Gemeinsam. Stark."** (All together. Strong.), emphasizing community and collective strength.[1] It operates as a primary school (Grundschule) and a special education support center (Förderzentrum), fostering and challenging approximately 570 local children in a calm and motivating school climate.[1] The educational approach is sport- and culture-focused, with a voluntary all-day program (offener Ganztag) led by partner ALBA BERLIN, integrating sports, culture, and daily school life through coaches and educators.[1]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
No specific founding history is mentioned in the content. The school serves children from the immediate neighborhood at its location on Pankstraße 70 along the Panke river (Panke-Ufer) in Berlin-Mitte district.[1] It maintains long-term partnerships in sports, museum education, music and theater pedagogy, and local community (Kiez) collaborations.[1] The website is undergoing restructuring to align with an updated school program, with some content temporarily unavailable.[1]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a **Grundschule** (primary school, typically grades 1-4 or 1-6 in Germany) combined with a **Förderzentrum** (special education center focused on "Autism" support), it provides inclusive education for children with special needs alongside regular primary students.[1][3] The daily schedule includes 7 lessons from 08:00 to 14:10, with breaks for breakfast, recesses, and extended all-day options.[1] No details on languages, Abitur, MSA, or BBR pathways are provided, consistent with its primary-level focus. The all-day program emphasizes sports and culture via ALBA BERLIN.[1]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students participate in a sport- and culture-oriented voluntary all-day program run by ALBA BERLIN, featuring coaches and educators for sports and cultural activities.[1] Extracurriculars include basketball teams competing successfully in the primary school league, music clubs (Musik-AGs) with performances (e.g., December 2025), and school delegations supporting professional teams.[1] Partnerships support sports and movement, museum pedagogy, music and theater, and local Kiez initiatives.[1] A school film showcases the community.[1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Recent successes include basketball teams performing well in the Grundschulliga and a school delegation motivating ALBA BERLIN's women's team to victory (January 2026 events).[1] No awards, rankings, or certifications are explicitly mentioned.[1]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a tuition-free public school (Öffentlich), enrollment follows Berlin's standard primary school registration process, with information available for parents on applications, instruction, school careers, and the parent association.[1] Contact key personnel for questions; special focus on autism support.[1][3]

description:
# Albrecht-Haushofer-Schule School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

The Albrecht-Haushofer-Schule's mission centers on creating **a school community where learning is experienced naturally and integrated into daily life**[5]. The school emphasizes that when positive experiences dominate school life, its offerings, projects, and concepts strengthen student connection to the school and foster social competencies[5].

The school's core principles include **gemeinsames Lernen** (collaborative learning), **soziales Lernen** (social learning), **handlungsorientiertes Lernen** (action-oriented learning), and **nachhaltiges Lernen** (sustainable learning)[2]. The school promotes a positive attitude toward learning through **varied teaching methods, interactive whiteboards, project work, offerings for high-achieving students, targeted support measures, and career preparation**[3].

The school values **respectful, friendly interaction** as a prerequisite for successful learning, emphasizing that all participants are responsible for a harmonious school environment[3].

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Founding and Development**

The Albrecht-Haushofer-Schule was **founded in 1952 in the Heiligensee district** as a three-track secondary school (Haupt- and Realschule)[2]. Beginning in the 2010/11 school year, it was gradually transformed into an **Integrated Secondary School (Integrierte Sekundarschule) without a gymnasium upper level, with part-time all-day programming**[2].

**Location and Community Character**

The school is located in **Kurzebracker Weg 40-46, 13503 Berlin-Heiligensee** in the Reinickendorf district[1]. It operates a **second campus at Stolpmünder Weg 45** for the career guidance team[5]. The school benefits from an **idyllic, green setting with an intimate atmosphere where everyone knows everyone**, creating a family-like environment that positively impacts instruction and school life[7]. Students describe themselves as **"Haushofers"** after a brief adjustment period[7].

**Facilities and Equipment**

The school provides **two computer rooms, interactive whiteboards, three workshops for metalwork, woodwork, and sewing, a teaching kitchen, and a large grass sports field**[1].

**Enrollment Statistics**

Student enrollment has grown significantly: from 337 students in 2011/12 to **543 students in 2023/24**, with teaching staff increasing from 28 to 51 teachers over the same period[4].

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**School Type and Structure**

The school is an **Integrated Secondary School (ISS) without a gymnasium upper level**, offering **maximum 26 students per class, no probationary period, and no grade retention**[6]. It operates with **part-time all-day programming**[1].

**Languages**

Students study **English as the first foreign language and French as the second foreign language**[1].

**Academic Specializations**

The school emphasizes **natural sciences and career orientation** as core strengths[1][4]. Special programs include:

- **Laptop classes** available in every grade level for students with learning differences like dyslexia (LRS)[6]
- **Small group learning in chemistry**[1]
- **External differentiation** in core subjects (English and Mathematics grades 7-10, German from grade 8 onward) with basic and advanced courses[6]

**Graduation Pathways**

The integrated secondary school enables all school-leaving qualifications[6]:

- **Berufsbildungsreife** (formerly Hauptschulabschluss/basic vocational qualification)
- **Erweiterte Berufsbildungsreife** (formerly extended Hauptschulabschluss)
- **Mittlerer Schulabschluss** (intermediate school-leaving certificate)
- **Abitur** (in cooperation with vocational colleges OSZ Emil Fischer, Ernst Litfass, and Georg Schlesinger)[6]

**Elective Courses**

Students choose from five wahlpflicht (required elective) courses including Sport + Fitness, EDV und Informatik (IT and Computer Science), and Deutsch als Zweitsprache (German as a Second Language)[6].

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**Career Orientation and Dual Learning**

The school provides comprehensive career preparation through **dual learning and professional training**[6]:

- **Grade 7**: Business site visits (Betriebserkundungen)
- **Grade 8**: Practical experience network (Netzwerk Berufspraxis)
- **Grade 9**: BVBO (Berlin Program for In-Depth Career Orientation) plus internships
- **Grades 9-10**: Practical learning sites including student enterprises and career passport work[6]

Additional career support includes **application training, business internships, and student companies**[1].

**Extracurricular Activities**

The school offers a **new Taekwondo AG** (working group)[5]. The website emphasizes that **"Sport an der AHS: Alle machen mit!"** (Everyone participates in sports)[5].

**Academic Support**

The school provides **targeted support measures** for students requiring additional help[3].

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

The search results do not contain specific information about awards, rankings, certifications, or standardized test outcomes. The school's school program document is available for download but specific achievement data is not detailed in the provided sources.

## 6. ADMISSIONS

**Public School Status**

As a **public school (öffentlich), the Albrecht-Haushofer-Schule is tuition-free**[1]. The school is open to students from surrounding primary schools as well as applicants from other districts[7].

**Enrollment Process**

The school welcomes prospective families to schedule campus tours. Interested families can arrange visits by calling **030 431 90 72**[5].

**Special Considerations**

The school is particularly known for its **laptop classes, which are specifically designed to support students with learning differences such as dyslexia (LRS)**. Multiple parent testimonials highlight this as a key reason for choosing the school[5].

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The school's **Leitbild** (mission statement), confirmed by the school conference on July 8, 2015, and updated in the Schulordnung effective April 17, 2024, emphasizes an inclusive environment where everyone is welcome and treated respectfully[1][2][3][4]. It views **diversity as a chance and enrichment**, enforces rules against exclusion, violence, racism, discrimination, sexual violence, and mobbing, promotes individual responsibility, transparent democratic decision-making, individualized learning to maximize potentials, and perspectives for self-determined lives[1][2][3][4]. The school positions itself as a **lernende Institution** (learning institution) offering co-shaping opportunities to all involved, with the motto "Wir sind Graefe!"[1][2][3][4][7].

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Built in **1888-1890** by architects Hermann Blankenstein and Karl Frobenius, the **denkmalgeschützte** (listed monument) school buildings feature richly articulated brick facades; during WWII (1940-1945), it served as an outpost for the Am Urban hospital housing forced laborers[8]. Located in Berlin's **Friedrichshain-Kreuzberg** district at Graefestraße 85-88, it serves over **450 students**, ~66 teachers, interns, 4 school social workers, 2 workshop pedagogues, 7 special educators, and full-day staff[1][6][8]. Facilities include **workshops** (wood, textile, bicycle, IT, language), teaching kitchen, bakery, lab, sports hall, and full-day offerings with sports focus in cooperation with ALBA Berlin[6][7].

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As an **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** with **gymnasialer Oberstufe** (grades 7-13), it offers pathways including **Abitur** and is a **Staatliche Europa-Schule (SESB)** with partner language **Spanish** alongside German[1][6][7]. Emphasizes **duales Lernen** (dual learning) linking theory to practical, work-market-relevant activities; praxis-oriented approach with differentiation, language education, and individual support[1][5][6][7].

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Full-day program (**Ganztag**) features **sports and movement** emphasis with ALBA Berlin cooperation, **music focus**, workshops for hands-on learning (e.g., woodworking, textiles, biking, cooking), and supportive services like school social work and supplementary learning[1][6][7]. School rules promote punctuality, respect, safety, and responsibility on campus during school hours[3][4].

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific awards, rankings, or certifications are detailed in the content; participation in the "School Turnaround!" project (kickoff September 24, 2014) highlights efforts in school development and networking[2]. The school aims to prepare students for optimal qualifications and post-school life through individual promotion[4].

### 6. ADMISSIONS
Public tuition-free school with open enrollment; current news highlights **infos for 7th grade registration** and a parent workshop report from November 22, 2025[scraped content]. Welcoming packet ("Graefe-ABC") greets new students into the community[9].

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Alexander-Puschkin-Schule emphasizes a respectful, tolerant school climate fostering mutual respect and anti-racism, as evidenced by its "Schule ohne Rassismus - Schule mit Courage" title and "Wohlfühlschule" award.[1][5] It promotes broad general knowledge, individualized preparation for school qualifications, social competencies, performance willingness, and responsible action through modern teaching methods like block scheduling, project-based learning, group work, and media integration.[3][5] The philosophy prioritizes anti-discrimination, equality regardless of origin, religion, or sexuality, and community engagement.[4]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Located in Berlin-Lichtenberg (Friedrichsfelde district) at Massower Str. 37, 10315 Berlin, the school serves a close-knit community as one of the smaller integrated secondary schools with around 16 classes, enabling personalized interactions where "everyone knows everyone."[3][2] Enrollment has remained stable at 430-490 students with 35-49 teachers from 2011/12 to 2023/24.[2] Facilities include over 120 networked computers, 30 student laptops, three IT rooms, internet-connected classrooms with projectors and video tech, six smartboards, a language lab, and a student club.[1] It operates partial all-day programs (Teilgebundener Ganztagsbetrieb).[1][3]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As an **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** (integrated secondary school without upper gymnasial level, school number 11K01), it offers grades 7-10 with block scheduling since 2011/12, focusing on 3-4 subjects per day in 90-minute blocks to enhance concentration, reduce homework, and lighten materials.[7][3] Profiles include **media-focused** and **language-focused** programs with languages: English, French, Latin, Russian, Spanish.[1][2] It supports all secondary qualifications (e.g., MSA, preparation for Abitur via second educational path, BBR vocational paths), differentiated learning in math, English, German, biology, chemistry, physics (grades 7-10), remedial support for reading/writing weaknesses and dyscalculia, and modern elements like individual learning, language/cultural/political education.[1][3]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in extensive extracurriculars via working groups (Arbeitsgemeinschaften): languages (English exam prep, vacation Spanish, Hungarian, Italian), arts (drumming, student band, creative design, manga, origami, paper design, guitar, keyboard), sports (soccer, cheerleading, line dance, basketball, yoga), clubs (internet café, aquaristics, school paramedics, healthy cooking, fashion, photo/video), and career orientation.[1] Community projects include annual "We Run United" charity runs against discrimination (e.g., 2025 for Kinderhospiz Berliner Herz), social initiatives like packing food/hygiene kits for homeless, Christmas singing/letters for elderly homes, clothing drives, and partnerships with local organizations (Bahnhofsmission, Stadtmission, Caritas).[4][6] Block model encourages independent project work; partial all-day with lunch.[1][7]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Holds titles **"Schule ohne Rassismus - Schule mit Courage"** (20+ years of anti-racism commitment) and **"Wohlfühlschule"**.[1][4][5] Block scheduling has led to successes in competency development; prepares nearly all students well for qualifications and transitions to vocational training, jobs, or studies.[3] Stable enrollment and growing staff reflect community trust; active in inspections and quality reports.[2][7]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **public tuition-free school (Öffentlich)** in Berlin-Lichtenberg, enrollment follows standard Berlin public school processes via eGovernment/Schulanmeldung, typically based on district residence without fees or tuition.[1][3]

description:
# Alfred-Nobel-Schule School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

The Alfred-Nobel-Schule is committed to preparing all students for successful transitions to vocational training or the academic upper secondary level (gymnasiale Oberstufe).[1] The school emphasizes a **bilingual and bicultural approach**, with teachers from both Italy and Germany collaborating to bring the cultures of both countries closer together while preserving cultural identity.[1] The school's educational philosophy centers on an **pronounced cultural profile** with significant engagement for students, positioning itself as a Europa-Schule (European School) in the green Britz district of Neukölln.[1]

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

The Alfred-Nobel-Schule was established in its current form as an Integrated Secondary School (ISS) through the merger of the Anna-Siemsen-Schule (Hauptschule) and the Alfred-Nobel-Realschule during Berlin's school structure reform.[3] The school operates across **two locations**: the main campus at Britzer Damm 164-170 and a second campus at Parchimer Allee 111, both in Berlin-Neukölln.[4][5]

**Facilities include:**[2][5]
- Wood and textile workshops
- Teaching kitchens
- Electronics laboratory
- Theater stage in the auditorium
- Computer rooms and internet café
- School garden
- Cafeteria
- A unique flight simulator operated by the student company "Nobel Air"

The school is conveniently located with good public transportation access in the green Britz district.[1]

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**Grade Levels:** Classes 7-10[1]

**Languages Offered:**[2][5]
- First foreign language: English
- Second foreign language: French or Italian

**Bilingual Program (SESB):**[1][2]
The school is part of the Staatliche Europaschule Berlin (SESB) with a German-Italian track. In grades 7-10, there is one SESB class per grade level alongside regular classes. Italian is the partner language, with the following subjects taught in Italian: Biology, Visual Arts, History, Geography, and Ethics.[1][2]

**Graduation Pathways:**[1][2]
By the end of grade 10, students can achieve all Berlin school certificates:
- Berufsbildungsreife (basic vocational qualification)
- Erweiterte Berufsbildungsreife (extended vocational qualification)
- Mittlerer Schulabschluss (intermediate school certificate)

Students who obtain the Mittlerer Schulabschluss with qualification for the academic upper secondary level can pursue the Abitur at the neighboring Albert-Einstein-Gymnasium in the European classes. The bilingual Abitur is also recognized as an Abitur in Italy.[1]

**Special Programs:**[2][5]
- Partial all-day program (Teilgebundener Ganztagsbetrieb)
- Theater-focused profile
- Intensive career orientation through projects, internships, and a practical class
- Support courses in German, Mathematics, and English for achieving the Mittlerer Schulabschluss
- Learning groups for newcomers without German language skills
- School station (Schulstation) for student support
- Reference school in the "Cultural Agents for Creative Schools" program

**Differentiation:**[5]
Inner and outer differentiation in Mathematics and English from grade 7 onwards, with special support offerings for MSA preparation.

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**Extracurricular Activities:**[5]
- Theater
- Football
- Acrobatics
- Volleyball
- Swimming
- Self-defense
- Robotics
- School band
- Student company: Cafeteria "Annas Café"

**School Events and Experiences:**[2]
- Class trips and seminar trips as regular offerings
- Summer festival
- Participation in sports competitions
- Cultural project weeks in January and June

**Career Orientation:**[3][5]
The school participates in Berlin's program for in-depth career orientation (BvBO) and cooperates with various educational providers. The school received a **quality seal for excellent career orientation** due to outstanding achievements in this area.[7] Career support includes the Berufswahlpass (career choice passport) sponsored by Deutsche Bahn AG and S-Bahn Berlin GmbH.

**Community Partnerships:**[2]
The school collaborates with organizations including Schlaufuchs, VESQ, TÜV Rheinland, Young Arts Neukölln, Jugendclub Feuerwache, and maintains a partner school in Italy.

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

The Alfred-Nobel-Schule has received recognition for its **quality seal for excellent career orientation** based on outstanding performance in career guidance and student preparation for vocational pathways.[7] The school is a **reference school** in the "Cultural Agents for Creative Schools" program, demonstrating excellence in cultural education.[2]

**Student Population Statistics (2023/24):**[4]
- 539 students
- 56 teachers
- Belastungsstufe (stress level): 5

## 6. ADMISSIONS

**Open House:** Saturday, February 21, 2026, 11:00-13:00[7]

**Registration Period:** March 5-12 for the next school year[7]

As a **public school** (öffentlich), the Alfred-Nobel-Schule is tuition-free. Students in grades 9-10 of ISS schools without their own upper secondary level have the right to a place at a Neukölln ISS with a gymnasiale Oberstufe if they meet the necessary prerequisites.[6]

All information and enrollment materials are available on the school's website.

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Benjamin-Franklin-Schule emphasizes fostering democratic and tolerant thinking, individual development based on students' personalities, origins, and talents, and promoting violence-free, open, and respectful social learning.[2] It supports personalized education through differentiated offerings to help students realize their potential, alongside self-responsible learning, teamwork, linguistic competencies, and critical media use.[2] The school collaborates with parents, students, social educators, and teachers to achieve these goals, with an annual Schulprogramm outlining development plans oriented to its Leitbild (guiding principles).[1]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded as a Mittelschule in 1919 in a building originally constructed in 1913 (initially used as a barracks during World War I), the school was officially recognized as a six-year Mittelschule on March 31, 1924, and transitioned to an Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS) starting in the 2011/12 school year.[1][2][4][8] Located in Berlin-Reinickendorf (Ortsteil Wittenau/Borsigwalde) at Sommerfelder Str. 5-7, 13509 Berlin, the protected historic building (Denkmalschutz № 09012380) features specialized facilities including 22 classrooms, 7 practice rooms, 4 science demonstration rooms, 2 language labs, a typing room, cooking kitchen, workrooms, music room, art room, and photo lab (as of expansions completed in 1978).[2][4][7] The community engages in local clean-up (Kehrenbürger-Tag), democracy demos, and parent networking via Elterncafé.[1]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As an **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS) without gymnasiale Oberstufe**, it serves grades 7-10, with entry typically after 6 years of Grundschule.[1][5] Foreign languages include **English and French**; it features a **music profile**.[1] Possible qualifications: Berufsbildungsreife (BBR) after grade 9, erweiterte BBR (eBBR) or Mittlerer Schulabschluss (MSA, with or without Gymnasialempfehlung for upper secondary transition) after grade 10; no Abitur.[5] The Schulprogramm details annual pedagogical focuses and is available for download.[1]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students participate in arts projects like linocut prints (e.g., waves inspired by "Great Wave off Kanagawa"), atlas-based identity portraits, and Stromkastenstyling (street art competition).[1] Music events include Big-Band-Night on the schoolyard.[1] Civic engagement covers Tag des Grundgesetzes (with prosecutors discussing freedom of speech), democracy/vielfalt demos, Kehrenbürger clean-ups, and Mauerlauf runs.[1][6] Sports and community runs like the 12. Mauerlauf involve grade 10 teams.[6] A new school-wide **handy rule** bans private mobile device use during school hours.[1] Open Door Day is scheduled for January 23, 2026 (17:00-19:00).[1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Past awards include the 1997 "Demokratisch-Handeln" prize (one of six Berlin schools selected among 140 in Germany) for the "Learning by going" project, presented in Frankfurt's Paulskirche.[4] Enrollment has stabilized around 420-440 students with 40-53 teachers since 2011/12.[1] The school grew to West Berlin's largest Realschule (1,100 students, 70 teachers) by 1980.[2][4] Recent entries like "Kreativster Stromkasten Berlins ’25" indicate ongoing creative competitions.[1]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **public tuition-free school** (Öffentlich), enrollment follows Berlin's standard process for ISS: application after Grundschule (typically for grade 7), with no fees or tuition.[1][5] Open Door Days (e.g., January 23, 2026) allow visits; digital platform i-Serv supports parent/school communication, with support resources available.[1] Contact: info@benjamin-franklin-schule.de, Tel: 030 43983810.[3]

Berlin Bilingual School (BBS) provides affordable bilingual English-German education for native-level speaking students from Grade 1 to 13, emphasizing a top-notch curriculum from foundational learning to the bilingual Abitur.[1][2][4] The school celebrates diversity across 30+ nationalities, fostering creative and critical thinking to develop responsible global citizens, with a family-like atmosphere highlighted by students and staff.[1][2][4] Core values include embracing diversity of thought, experience, and culture; inclusion; anti-racism; sex positivity; and personal development through topics like empathy, nonviolent communication, drug education, online safety, and anti-bullying.[1][4][5]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 2007 by expat parents as an extension of a bilingual kindergarten (Berlin Kids International, started earlier in eastern Berlin), BBS opened its primary school that year in a renovated former East German building; it expanded to secondary in 2012, renamed itself, and split campuses in 2014.[1][2][3][4] Governed by Berlin Kids International e.V. and Pfefferwerk Stadtkultur gGmbH, it serves ~450 students from 30 nationalities, with ~100 staff from 25 nationalities; class sizes are 24-26 (primary) and 12-24 (secondary).[2] Primary campus: Weinstraße 1, 10249 Berlin (Friedrichshain); secondary (ISS/Gymnasium) campus: Granitzstr. 38A, 13189 Berlin (Pankow district).[1][2][3] No specific facilities details beyond campuses and school hours (primary: 8:30am-4pm, supervision 7:30-8:30am, afternoon to 6pm; secondary: 8am-4pm).[2]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
BBS follows a bilingual English-German program: primary (Years 1-6) uses Berlin State Curriculum and International Primary Curriculum (IPC)/UK National Curriculum; secondary (Years 7-13, as Integrierte Sekundarschule) blends Berlin State Curriculum (Berliner Rahmenlehrplan), UK National Curriculum, with sciences in English, humanities in German, languages (incl. French/Spanish) by native speakers, and electives (IT, Drama, Psychology, Philosophy) from Grade 9.[1][2][5] Graduation includes Mittlere Schulabschluss (MSA) pathways, International/Edexcel IGCSE (Year 11, English/Science), and bilingual Abitur (Years 12/13, highest German qualification for university access).[1][2][5] Personal Development Curriculum addresses modern challenges like relationships and anti-racism.[5]

Small classes promote individualized learning, self-reflection, and community immersion, with project-based activities like Grade 9 acid-alkali experiments on indigestion tablets.[1][5] School life includes internships (e.g., Grade 9 in Jan 2026), Personal Development projects on sex education, anti-racism, and online safety, plus a family-like environment where students report feeling happy and excited.[1][4][5] Events feature open houses, conferences, and after-school programs ("Hort"); no specific sports, arts, or exchanges detailed, though drama elective and blog posts suggest hands-on science and presentations.[1][2][5]

Secondary students achieve excellent academic results and view BBS as a second home; the school prepares for bilingual Abitur with university access in Germany and internationally.[2][5] No awards, rankings, or specific outcome metrics (e.g., Abitur pass rates) mentioned.[1][2][4][5]

description:
# Berlin British School - Comprehensive School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

Berlin British School is committed to providing **holistic education** that balances academic excellence with personal development[1]. The school's core mission emphasizes nurturing character development through an engaging curriculum and supportive pastoral care, with a focus on preparing students as **life-long enthusiastic learners and global thinkers**[1].

The school combines **the best of British, international and German education** to create an inclusive learning environment[1]. Key educational principles include:

- High academic standards with small class sizes supporting personalized learning[1]
- Strong pastoral care focused on fostering student self-confidence[1]
- Holistic development addressing academic, social, and emotional growth[1]
- Inquiry-based learning processes aligned with international best practices[1]
- Recognition that education extends beyond academics to include enrichment activities and work-life balance[2]

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Location and Structure:**
Berlin British School operates across three sites near Berlin's historic Olympic stadium and Grunewald forest[1]. The secondary school is located in the leafy surroundings of Grunewald in the Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf district[2].

**Community:**
The school serves approximately 500 students aged 3-18 from diverse cultural backgrounds[5]. It welcomes both local Berlin families and international families, including children of embassy staff[8]. The school emphasizes creating a **warm and caring community** where students develop meaningful friendships and connections with teachers[1].

**Facilities:**
The school provides appropriate facilities for each year group and maintains an all-day school structure with extracurricular activities and after-school care[1].

**Governance:**
Berlin British School gGmbH operates as a not-for-profit limited company. All shares are owned by Berlin British School e.V., a registered association under German law whose purpose is promoting British education and culture in Germany[1].

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**Age Range and Structure:**
The school serves students aged 3-18, organized into Early Years, Primary School, and Secondary School[1].

**Languages of Instruction:**
The school offers **two educational streams**[2]:

- **Monolingual stream**: Taught entirely in English[2]
- **Bilingual stream**: Teaching in both English and German, available for students with German nationality and those planning long-term residence in Berlin[4]

Bilingual stream students must be fluent in written and spoken German[4]. German subjects in the bilingual stream include History, Ethics, Political Education, Economics-Work-Technology (WAT), and PE, while Mathematics is taught in both languages[4].

**Secondary School Qualifications:**

**Grades 9-10 (Key Stage 4):**
- All students work toward the **International General Certificate of Secondary Education (IGCSE)**[6]
- Bilingual stream students also take the **German MSA (Mittlerer Schulabschluss)**, the German intermediate school-leaving qualification[6]
- Students sit both MSA and IGCSE exams in spring of Grade 10[6]

**Grades 11-12:**
All students pursue the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**, described as the "gold standard" of international education[1]. The IB DP is offered in English only[4].

**Progression Requirements:**

*Monolingual students* require six IGCSE grades at Grade C or above, including Mathematics and English[6].

*Bilingual students* require four IGCSEs at Grade C or above (including English and Maths) and successful completion of all MSA examinations with an overall score under 3.0[6].

**IB Diploma Curriculum:**
Students choose six academic subjects (three at Standard Level, three at Higher Level) across six groups[1]:

- Group 1: Studies in Language and Literature (English A, German A, Self-Taught A)
- Group 2: Language Acquisition (German B, French B, Spanish B, German ab initio)
- Group 3: Individuals and Societies (Economics, Geography, History, Psychology)
- Group 4: Sciences (Biology, Chemistry, Physics, Environmental Systems and Societies)
- Group 5: Mathematics (Approaches and Analysis, Applications and Interpretations)
- Group 6: The Arts and Electives (Music, Visual Arts, Theatre Studies)

**Additional IB Requirements:**
- **Theory of Knowledge (ToK)**: Introduces philosophical ideas and critical thinking about knowledge[1]
- **Creativity, Action and Service (CAS)**: Minimum 150 hours of community service, creative expression, and personal development[1]
- **Extended Essay**: 4,000-word independent research project[1]

**Important Admissions Notes:**
The school does not admit students to Grade 10 or Grade 12[1]. Starting in Grades 9 and 11 is unlikely due to the two-year design of IGCSE and IB Diploma courses[1].

**Certification:**
Berlin British School is an **IB World School** running the Primary Years Programme (PYP), IGCSE, and IB Diploma programmes[6]. The school holds official status as a **'staatlich anerkannte bilinguale Grundschule'** (state-recognized bilingual primary school), **'genehmigte bilinguale Integrierte Sekundarschule'** (approved bilingual integrated secondary school), and **'Ergänzungsschule'** (supplementary school)[1].

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**Extracurricular Activities:**
Secondary school students are encouraged to participate in a wide range of activities including[2]:

- GISST sports (competitive sports program)
- Model United Nations
- Various other clubs and activities enabling students to explore passions and develop leadership skills

**School Day Structure:**
- Lessons begin at 8:30 and end at 15:20 (3:20 PM)[2]
- School day officially ends at 16:00 (4:00 PM)[2]
- **After School Clubs** available until 16:30 (4:30 PM) or 17:00 (5:00 PM) for bilingual students[2][4]
- Before-school supervision available from 8:00 AM[2]

**Pastoral Care and Well-being:**
The school demonstrates deep commitment to student well-being through supportive teacher relationships, approachable administration, and solution-oriented problem-solving[1]. The school emphasizes maintaining work-life balance and addresses the health and well-being of all students[1].

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

**University Placement:**
Following education at Berlin British School, students have been accepted into top universities in Germany, the UK, the USA, and worldwide[1].

**IB Diploma Results:**
The school publishes previous IB Diploma results, though specific data is referenced but not detailed in the provided content[1].

**International Recognition:**
As an IB World School, the school operates internationally acclaimed programmes recognized globally[1]. The IGCSE qualification is one of the world's most recognized qualifications and serves as a foundation for higher-level courses[7].

## 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES

**Admissions Process:**

1. **Enquiry Form**: Initial contact to receive information[1]
2. **Application Submission**: Detailed application form with school reports from the last two years; no cost or commitment to apply[1]
3. **School Tour**: Individual tours (30-45 minutes) of appropriate campus once a place is available[1]
4. **Student Visit**: Free three-day visit (two mornings for Kindergarten) allowing students and teachers to get acquainted; school evaluates academic level and social adaptability[1]
5. **Admission Decision**: Teachers, Head of School, and School Principal make final decisions based on visit feedback[1]

**Timing:**
Admission is generally possible at any time during the academic year if places are available, except Grade 10 and Grade 12[1].

**Fees:**

The school website indicates that **annual school fees and additional fees are available** but does not display specific EUR amounts on the main pages[1]. Instead, the school provides separate fee documents for different school types:

- **Kita-Fees 2025-2026** (Early Years/Kita)
- **EGS-Fees 2025-2026** (International School/Ergänzungsschule)
- **ESS-Fees 2025-2026** (Ersatzschule - state-recognized bilingual primary and secondary school)
- **ESS-Schulgeldordnung 2025-2026** (Fee regulations)
- **Antrag Einkommensabhängiges Schulgeld 2025-2026** (Income-dependent tuition application)[1]

**Additional Fees (Potential):**
- After School Care[1]
- Lunch Service[1]
- Extra Curricular Activities[1]
- School & Sport Uniform[1]
- Music Lessons[1]

**Scholarships and Financial Support:**
- **Income-based tuition options** are available[1]
- Parents should contact the Admissions team regarding fee schedules, income-related fees, or scholarships[1]
- The school does **not accept Kita Gutschein** (Kita vouchers) for Early Years[1]

**Important Note:**
Exact tuition amounts in EUR are not displayed on the publicly accessible website pages. Parents must contact the school directly or access the PDF fee documents (ESS-Fees 2025-2026, EGS-Fees 2025-2026, etc.) to obtain specific pricing information.

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
Berlin Metropolitan School (BMS) strives for **academic excellence and strength in character** to contribute to a more informed world and an open-minded society.[1][5][6] The school's philosophy emphasizes international mindedness, global engagement, and respect for diverse cultures, fostering curiosity, empathy, and reflective thinking through inquiry-based learning inspired by IB principles.[web:scraped Curriculum Page] Teaching and learning promote engaged, self-directed learners who take positive action, with character building embedded via the IB Learner Profile, resilience, and self-awareness.[web:scraped Curriculum Page] BMS commits to adapting to societal changes, preparing students for future challenges with critical thinking, personal responsibility, and a balanced whole-day program from 7:30 a.m. to 6:00 p.m.[web:scraped About Page][1]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in **2004**, BMS is the oldest and largest international school in Berlin-Mitte, initially bilingual and focused on gifted students, transitioning to an integrated secondary school with an IB profile in 2006.[1][2][4][7] It serves over **1,000-1,135 students** from **60-65 nationalities** and **22 home languages**, with a diverse community of German nationals and international families in a dynamic urban setting surrounded by museums and galleries.[web:scraped Main Page][web:scraped About Page][1][3][4][5][6] Facilities include **2 libraries**, **2 science labs**, digital infrastructure (Apple MacBooks, iPads, iMacs, Office 365, classroom displays), an auditorium, cafeteria, rooftop extensions, music rooms, roof garden, and sports areas in prefabricated GDR-era buildings expanded for senior classes.[web:scraped Main Page][3][7] Support services encompass career/university counseling, student support, learning/language assistance, school counselors, nurses, and child protection officers.[web:scraped Main Page]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
BMS offers a **K-12 continuum** (ages 3-18) as an all-day **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** in Berlin-Mitte, combining international and German national curricula in **English** (primary) and **German** (alternate).[web:scraped School Information][1][3][4] Programs include:
- **Early Learning Center** (ages 3-6): IB Primary Years Programme (PYP) and Berlin State Curriculum.
- **Primary School** (Grades 1-6, ages 6-11): Foundation for education, IB PYP elements.
- **Secondary School** (Grades 7-8, ages 11-14): Bespoke BMS and Berlin State Curriculum.
- **Grades 9-10** (ages 14-16): IGCSE, Berlin State Curriculum, Berliner Bildungsgriffe.
- **Grades 11-12**: International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP), bilingual options, German National School Diploma (Mittlerer Schulabschluss - MSA).[web:scraped Main Page][3][6]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
No explicit mission statement is provided in the content, but the school's approach emphasizes individualized support, performance-differentiated learning, and preparation for diverse pathways including Abitur, with flexible course level changes half-yearly.[2] It promotes creativity, cultural engagement, and community through projects like multimedia tours and charity events, fostering skills in "learning to learn," time management, and media integration in teaching.[2][4] Values include fairness, teamwork, and social responsibility, evident in events like Weihnachtsaktion raising funds for school and hunger relief in Sudan.[1]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 1973, the Bertolt-Brecht-Oberschule (BBO) is a public integrated secondary school (Integrierte Sekundarschule, ISS) with gymnasial upper secondary level, named after playwright Bertolt Brecht, located in Berlin's Spandau district (Wilhelmstr. 10, 13595 Berlin-Wilhelmstadt).[1] It serves around 1113 students (2018/2019 data) with 120 teachers, led by Harald Schwarz (historical) or Magdalena Samborski-Parr (recent).[1][3] As a full-day school (Gebundener Ganztagsbetrieb), it features a modern auditorium for events, school library, student mentorships, canteen open from 7:30 AM with meals, seven computer rooms, mobile laptops, smartboards, internet access in all classrooms, and excellent natural sciences equipment.[1][2][4] Partnerships include Bauhaus-Archiv, Stiftung Preußische Schlösser und Gärten, Musikschule Spandau, and schools in Israel.[1][3]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Serves grades 7-13 (Klassenstufen 7 bis 13) with max 26 students per class in grades 7-8, offering core subjects: Gesellschaftswissenschaften (Ethik/Philosophie, Geschichte, Geographie, Politik), Darstellendes Spiel, Sport, Deutsch, Mathematik/Informatik, WAT (Wirtschaft-Arbeit-Technik), Naturwissenschaften (Biologie, Chemie, Physik), Kunst, Musik, and modern foreign languages (Englisch as S1, Französisch/Spanisch).[1][2] Special profiles: English bilingual class, music class (Gesang focus), political education class; leistungsdifferenziertes Lernen with GR/ER courses and flexible switches; Wahlpflichtfächer A/B.[2][5] Upper secondary (gymnasiale Oberstufe): 2- or 3-year Abitur (typically after 13 years), Leistungskurse (e.g., Physik, Englisch, Geschichte, Chemie, Biologie, Deutsch, Mathematik, Musik, Kunst, Politik/Geografie); Grundkurse in additional subjects; second foreign language option (Französisch/Spanisch) from grade 11.[2][3][4] Graduation: Berufsbildungsreife (BBR) end of grade 9, erweiterte BBR (eBBR) end of grade 10; Abitur; vocational training support, internships in grades 9-10, BOS-Team for career/study orientation.[2] Profiles: kunstbetontes, mathematisch-naturwissenschaftliches, umweltbetontes; bilingual Deutsch/Englisch; external differentiation from grade 9.[3]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars include Hip Hop dance with Flying Steps Education (all grades, weekly), unique "Be inspired!" Schul-AG with Stiftung Preußische Schlösser (creativity for Schloss Charlottenburg app, 15 spots, grades 8+, SPSG certificate).[1] Sports: basketball/volleyball/football tournaments (e.g., 8th/7th/10th grades, Bezirk finals), swimming competitions.[1] Arts/cultural: Poetry Slam, theater visits (GRIPS), music/singing profiles, Bildnerische Werkstätten, textile workshops, Bauhaus projects.[1] Exchanges/trips: BBO meets Oldenburg/Wien/EU/China principals, Israel (Young Ambassadors, Ashdod partner), England (bilingual Bath), Uni Potsdam, Gedenkstätten (history), Krakau-Auschwitz AG.[1][3] Community: Weihnachtsaktion (cards, €500 raised), Schuhkarton Christmas, Valentinstag, Sponsorenlauf, Projektwochen, EPAS conference, Juniorwahl, Stolpersteinverlegung.[1] After-school: AGs, homework supervision, school social work, leisure area.[2]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Content highlights event successes like 8.24 class winning basketball tournament, €500 from Weihnachtsaktion (split for Abitur and Sudan aid), successful "Be inspired!" integration into SPSG app.[1] Partnerships and profiles noted as unique (e.g., Berlin's only such Schul-AG); one of eight Berlin schools developing Bauhaus-Archiv formats (2019).[1] No specific awards, rankings, or test outcomes detailed beyond general inspection reports and Prüfungsergebnisse availability.[3][8]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a tuition-free public school (Öffentlich), enrollment follows Berlin Senatsverwaltung guidelines; no guaranteed spots, based on Förderprognose average grade if oversubscribed.[3][5] Profile classes (Music/Gesang, English Bilingual; possible Political Education) require aptitude tests/criteria.[2][5] Open Day (e.g., 10.01.2025) for tours and demos; contact sekretariat@brecht-oberschule.de or visit for Fachbereiche info.[3][5][7] Max 26/class in grades 7-8; two class teachers.[2]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
BEST-Sabel-Oberschule emphasizes individualized potential development, blending Gymnasium and Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS) pathways to Abitur tailored to students' learning pace, with profiles in **Wirtschaft & Berufsorientierung** and **Sprachen**.[1][2][4] The philosophy prioritizes a familial atmosphere in small classes (max 22 students), holistic education beyond knowledge transmission, and integration of life and learning in a campus-like setting with bound full-day operations (8-16 Uhr) including self-organized learning, AGs (working groups), and individual support like TiF for reading/writing difficulties.[1][2][3] As part of BEST-Sabel Bildungszentrum, active since 1991, it fosters reliable education from Kita to vocational completion, focusing on social competencies, practical orientation, and respectful interactions.[Main Page][1]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded within BEST-Sabel Bildungszentrum (active since 1991), the Oberschule occupies a historic, listed building at Lindenstr. 1, 12555 Berlin (Köpenick, Treptow-Köpenick district), originally built in 1910 as a boys' school by Hugo Kinzer and hosting BEST-Sabel programs since 2008.[2][3] A modern Oberstufenhaus extension supports upper grades since 2024/2025, creating a spacious campus with familial atmosphere.[1][5] Facilities include library, computer rooms, whiteboards, teaching kitchen, wood workshop, and bound full-day setup with meals; community ties involve local partners like Reiterhof "Pferde Land," BIZ, and initiatives like sustainable coffee cup projects with Bürgerstiftung Treptow-Köpenick.[3][Fees/Tuition Page][1]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Serves grades 7+ as state-recognized private Ersatzschule (ISS-Gymnasium), offering flexible switches between **Gymnasium** (Abitur after 12 years) and **ISS** (Abitur after 13 years) until grade 11, plus **Fachoberschule (FOS)** for Fachabitur (Wirtschaft or Sozialpädagogik) in 2 years post-grade 10.[1][2][4] Profiles: Wirtschaft/Berufsorientierung (with praxis weeks, internships, company ties) and Sprachen; more English than Berlin standard, S1 Englisch/Französisch, S2 Englisch/Latein, S4 Englisch/Spanisch; math/science focus, theater, PC/laptop training all grades.[3][2][4] Bound Ganztag includes differentiated instruction, Lerngruppen for reading/math, and pathways to MSA/Fachabitur.[1][3]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Full-day rhythm (8-16 Uhr) features self-organized learning, AGs, sports (rudern grade 9, Skischule grade 8, swimming/segeln due to water proximity), robotics (VEX IQ), Känguru math contests, theater/musicals, project weeks (Demokratie, sustainability, fitness trips), excursions (Bio-Apfelhof, museums), and social initiatives (anti-racism, donations).[Main Page][3][5][8] Extracurriculars build talents via Freizeitaktivitäten, sports groups, and practicals (Betriebspraktikum, social Praktikum).[3][1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific awards, rankings, or certifications extracted; highlights include world record (unspecified, Grundschule-linked), VEX Robotics participation, sustainable projects (biodegradable coffee cup with Bürgerstiftung), Erasmus+ to Iceland, and 20-year Oberschule milestone with new Oberstufenhaus.[Main Page][5][Fees/Tuition Page] Prüfungsergebnisse available via Berlin.de portrait.[3]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
Admissions support quereinstieg any grade, open doors (e.g., 30 Sep 2025, 11 Oct 2025), info events; alternatives for no Gymnasium placement, with three Abitur paths.[4][5][6] No exact fee amounts mentioned in content; no PDFs with fees found. Contact: +49 30 280360970, oberschule@best-sabel.de.[3]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Bettina-von-Arnim-Schule operates as an **Integrierte Sekundarschule (integrated secondary school) with gymnasialer Oberstufe (upper secondary level)**, emphasizing a reform-pedagogical approach inspired by Peter Petersen's Jena-Plan, focusing on individualization, heterogeneity, and inclusive learning without external performance differentiation.[1][3][4][6][7] Its core philosophy, implemented via the **ANKER-System** (Alternierende, Niveau, Konzentrations, Erweiterungs, Reduktionskurse), allows students in grades 7-10 to quarterly choose courses across subject groups (e.g., Deutsch/Geschichte/Erdkunde; Englisch/Mathematik; Physik/Chemie/Biologie; Musik/Bildende Kunst/Sport) in flexible 40-minute units, promoting self-directed learning in heterogeneous groups.[1][4][5][6] The school fosters a musisch-künstlerischer Schwerpunkt (musical-artistic focus) alongside inclusivity for disabled and non-disabled students, ganztagsbetrieb (all-day program), project-based work, and social-pedagogical support.[1][3][4][5][6]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 1970 (first students in 1972) by Dr. Karl Wagner as the "2. Gesamtschule Reinickendorf" in Berlin-Reinickendorf's Märkisches Viertel, the school transitioned from Oberschule to Gesamtschule and now Integrierte Sekundarschule; it received KMK approval in 1975 as a model school with special pedagogical profile, funded by Berlin.[1][3][6][7] Located at **Senftenberger Ring 49, 13435 Berlin (Bezirk Reinickendorf, Ortsteil Märkisches Viertel)**, it serves a diverse community in a "Schuldorf" campus of ten buildings, one per SEK I yeargang, with accessibility features like rollstuhlgerechter Aufzug, WC, and ramps.[1][2][5][6] Facilities include **Bibliothek, Cafeteria/Mensa, Computerräume, Holzwerkstatt, Metallwerkstatt, Textilwerkstatt, Druckerei, Werkstatt für Elektronik, Lehrküche**.[5]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Serving **grades 7-13** (SEK I: 7-10, gymnasiale Oberstufe: 11-13) with **Abitur** as the main graduation pathway, the curriculum features **ANKER-Wahldifferenzierung** in SEK I, Leistungskurse in Oberstufe (e.g., Bildende Kunst, Biologie, Deutsch, Englisch, **Französisch**, Geschichte, Mathematik, **Musik**, Physik, Politische Bildung), and profiles in **Kunst, Musik, Theater, MINT, Sprachen (Chinesisch, Französisch)**.[1][4][5][7] It offers gebundener Ganztagbetrieb, lerngruppen for non-German speakers, projektunterricht (grades 7-10), and inclusive heterogeneous learning; no explicit MSA or BBR mentioned, but integrated structure supports varied pathways.[4][5]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars emphasize arts and culture: **Musical, Schülerbands, Orchester, Schulchor, Big Band, Theater, Kunst, Siebdruck**; sports like **Tennis, Fußball, Basketball, Schach**; other AGs include Tierpflege, Jugend forscht, Speedcubing-Wettbewerbe (with German record), Workshop Zauber der Farben.[1][5][website main page] Events include **More-Than-Arts-Festival, Go Art Wandertag (grade 7), China-Abend, Infoveranstaltung**; international ties via **Schulpartnerschaft with Chiayi Senior High School (Taiwan)**.[5][7][website main page] All-day support includes sozialpädagogische Betreuung per yeargang.[5][6]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Recognized as a **Modellschule des Landes Berlin** and one of six German schools with special Mittelstufe (7-10) model status (120-student Großgruppen per yeargang); hosts successes like Jugend forscht in Adlershof, Speedcubing-Wettbewerb 2024 with Deutscher Rekord, and is among Berlin's most popular Integrierte Sekundarschulen.[1][3][5][website main page] Enrollment: ~1309 students, 37 classes (avg. 35.4 students/class); Schulleitung: Stefan Welde (or Stefan St. Welde).[4]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **tuition-free public Öffentlich school (ISS type, Schulnummer 12K02)**, enrollment for new 7th grade is March 5-12, 2026; phone appointments (030 40305-119, Jan 7 onward, 08:00-13:00); Infoveranstaltung Feb 25, 2026, 15:00 (Mensa, ~1.5h, registration required).[website main page][2][4]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Bröndby-Schule emphasizes an inclusive, diverse educational environment fostering all performance levels through integrated secondary schooling with a gymnasial upper level, promoting active student involvement via the "pädagogische Werkstatt" concept that teaches effective learning methods and includes students in evaluations.[1][2][6] It prioritizes individualized support, dual learning from grade 7, career orientation, and profiles in **Technik/Wirtschaft (TeWi)**, **Kunst**, and **Sport**, with reinforced instruction instead of electives.[1][2][9] The school values diversity with 29 nationalities, supports integration via welcome/language classes aiming for B1/B2 German diplomas, and commits to anti-racism ("Schule ohne Rassismus – Schule mit Courage").[2][4][5]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded around 1975 (celebrating 50-year anniversary in 2025), the school serves ~1,000 students across 30 classes in grades 7-10, five 11th-grade classes, and gymnasial upper secondary, with over 100 staff in a multicultural community in Berlin-Steglitz-Zehlendorf (Lankwitz district).[1][2][4][6] Facilities include large modern workshops with a master craftsman, 3D printers, automation room, computer labs, smartboards in most classrooms, school garden, large courtyards, on-site sports hall, beautifully designed schoolyard, cafeteria, and kiosk with fresh snacks; accessible features: parking for disabled, wheelchair elevator, and WC.[1][5] Open all-day system (ganztag) with 3 weekly study hours, AGs, counseling, mediation training.[1][2][6]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
**Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** for grades 7-10 (Mittelstufe) and gymnasial Oberstufe (grades 11+), offering all exits: **BBR**, **eBBR**, **MSA** (with/without upper-level transition), **Abitur** (G9 model), and Praxislerngruppen from grade 9 leading to BBR/eBBR or MSA.[2][3][4][5] Profile classes (one per yeargroup in TeWi, Kunst, Sport) require selection process; bilingual English-Biology track from grade 9 with advanced English prerequisites.[1][6][9] Subjects: Biologie, Chemie, Darstellendes Spiel, Deutsch, Fremdsprachen (Englisch, Französisch, Spanisch, Latein; advanced English possible), Gesellschaftswissenschaften, Informatik, Kunst, Lernen durch Engagement, Mathematik, Musik, Physik, Planen Bauen Verkaufen, Psychologie (from grade 11), Sport, WAT, Ergänzungskurs Studium und Beruf.[1][7] Upper level: two profile courses initially for Abitur prep, Leistungskurse in Physik, Erdkunde, Geschichte, Kunst, Englisch, Biologie, Mathematik, Politische Weltkunde, Deutsch.[7][8] Inklusion support, differentiated instruction in MINT subjects, Turkish elective, dual learning/9th-grade internships.[1][3][4]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars include Arbeitsgemeinschaften (AGs) like Umwelt-AG, Klimaschutz-AG; AUB (außerunterrichtlich) with Erzieher/Sozialpädagogen offering workshops (woodwork, silk painting), freizeit activities, counseling.[1][2][6] Sports via profile and on-site hall; arts/creativity in Kunst profile, music, darstellendes Spiel; debates ("Jugend debattiert in Sprachlernklassen"); logo design contests; Elterncafé.[1][4] Ganztag with school stations, mediation; career prep via internships, Studium/Beruf courses; events like Tag der offenen Tür (17 Jan 2026).[1][3][10]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
- **Silberstatus Verbraucherschule** (consumer education, building on 2024 Bronze; aims for Gold).[1][10]
- **Qualitätssiegel Berlin für exzellente Berufliche Orientierung** (career/study orientation; awarded by Staatssekretärin Christina Henke).[1][10]
- **KlimaVisionen-Schule** (climate protection roadmap, network member).[1]
- **Schule ohne Rassismus – Schule mit Courage**.[4]
High ratings in languages, MINT, Deutsch/GesWi, music, arts, sports, digital, individual support, career/social.[4]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **public tuition-free school**, enrollment follows Berlin public system; profile classes require selection process.[1] Tag der offenen Tür: 17 Jan 2026, 10-13 Uhr; "Die Schule stellt sich vor" info.[1][10] Welcome/language classes (12 students each, since 2015/16) for refugees/integration to B1/B2.[2][4] Contact: Sekretariat (Mo-Fr 07:00-14:00, sick notes from 07:30), Tel. 030-90 299 2070/2010, post@broendby.de.[1]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
Canisius-Kolleg is a Jesuit school with a **christlich-humanistische** (Christian-humanistic) profile, emphasizing excellent education in a comprehensive, error-friendly sense to foster responsible personal development.[1] It promotes talent development, independent learning, critical and creative thinking through an open all-day program, while keeping the question of God alive, encouraging reflection, mindfulness, justice, solidarity, and respect for human dignity.[1] Jesuit values include nurturing judgment, freedom, and commitment to a just society, with digital leadership education from grade 9 using school tablets.[1]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded as a Gymnasium since **1925**, with the Integrierte Sekundarschule “Pedro Arrupe” added since **2019**.[1] It serves **929 students** (Gymnasium: 829; ISS: 100) from **60 nationalities** and **41 mother tongues**, fostering diversity as a hallmark of good education.[1] Located at **Tiergartenstr. 30-31, 10785 Berlin-Mitte**, near Tiergarten (0.01 km), Potsdamer Platz (1.5 km), Brandenburg Gate (2 km), and Breitscheidplatz (1.6 km).[1] Facilities support an open all-day program, with ~105-120 in afternoon care and ~690-710 in the on-site youth organization ISG; classes average ~30 students, smaller in upper secondary groups and electives.[1]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Offers **Gymnasium from grade 5 (Sexta)** leading to a 4-semester gymnasiale Oberstufe (upper secondary with profile "extras"), and **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS) “Pedro Arrupe” from grade 9**, state-recognized including Sekundarstufe II.[1] Latin is compulsory from grade 5; open to all from grade 5, with lateral entries up to grade 10 (if places available) and into Oberstufe.[1] Catholic religious education for all (including non-Catholics; ~20% Protestant students receive their own); digital-supported teaching with tablets from grade 9; **handyfreie Schule** (phone-free).[1] ISS focuses on flight and migration; supports gifted education via "Zentrum für individuelle Begabungsförderung".[1] Graduation pathways include Abitur via Gymnasium Oberstufe; state-recognized ISS pathways (MSA implied, details in state recognition).[1][3]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
**Open Ganztagsangebot** (all-day program) with afternoon care (~105-120 participants), ISG youth organization (~690-710 members) for social/democratic skills.[1] Extracurriculars include AG-Programm with musical focus, school pastoral care, counseling, prevention, class/student councils.[1] International networking with Jesuit schools in USA, Lithuania, France, Near East; promotes peace, reconciliation, anti-antisemitism.[1] Kollegsgottesdienste (school services) weekly; diverse events like Ramadan observance.[1] Sports/arts not detailed beyond music AG and general talent development.[1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
**State recognition** for ISS Pedro Arrupe in Sekundarstufe II (recently achieved).[1] "Digital-Leadership-Education" implemented; Zentrum für individuelle Begabungsförderung established.[1] No specific awards, rankings, or outcome stats (e.g., Abitur rates) mentioned in content.[1]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
**Admissions**: Online application for grade 5 (Sexta) 2026/2027 open 9 Sep 2025–19 Dec 2025 (12:30); process includes Aufnahmeverfahren; lateral entries to grade 10/Oberstufe if places available; contact Frau Kaminska for queries.[1] Open to all, emphasizing diversity.[1]

**Fees**: Tuition is **income-scaled** (Schulgeldstaffelung) with moderate base rate; no exact EUR amounts listed on pages—detailed in PDF: https://canisius.de/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Canisius-Kolleg_Schulgeldinormationen_2025-2027.pdf.[1] School commits "am Schulgeld wird der Besuch ... nicht scheitern" (tuition will not prevent attendance); reductions/full waivers via simple written self-assessment request to Rektor (no financial disclosure required).[1] Additional SEPA mandates for Nachmittagsbetreuung (after-school), Lernmittel (materials).[1] Jesuit order, alumni, donors subsidize; no church tax funding.[1] No registration, meal, or specific material/after-school costs listed (after-school via separate SEPA).[1] Scholarships via donor network; income-based options explicitly available.[1]

tuition_notes: Canisius-Kolleg uses an income-based tuition model (Schulgeldstaffelung) for school years 2025-2027. The base tuition (Basissatz) is EUR 198.00 per month for families with annual income up to EUR 76,000. An entry-level tuition (Einstiegsschulgeld) of EUR 100.00 per month is available for families with annual income up to EUR 29,420 (gross). The maximum tuition cap (Höchstsatz) is EUR 396.00 per month. From grade 9 onwards, an additional fee for school materials (Tablets) applies. A one-time administrative fee (Verwaltungsgebühr) equal to one base tuition rate (EUR 198.00) is required upon contract signature and credited toward the first monthly payment. Tuition reduction or complete waiver is available upon request based on family self-assessment, with no formal income documentation required. Sibling discounts apply: 75% of base rate for second child, 50% for third child, and no tuition for fourth and subsequent children. The school operates on the principle that school attendance does not fail due to tuition fees (Bildung scheitert nicht am Schulgeld). For families receiving Berlinpass (unemployment/welfare benefits), tuition can be reduced to EUR 0.00-99.00 or fully waived.

description:
# Carl-Benz-Schule (Integrierte Sekundarschule) - School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

Carl-Benz-Schule's highest goal is **comprehensive education** of its students, emphasizing broad foundational knowledge and the ability to make reasoned judgments that will support learners throughout their lives[2]. The school views education as a personal, positive experience for all participants, with students at the center of the educational process[2].

The school's core values include:

- **Engagement**: The school actively fosters engagement for the school community and important societal issues, operating with a positive human image and working with competence, reliability, and constructive problem-solving[2].

- **Sustainability**: As "a school in the green," Carl-Benz-Schule emphasizes responsible resource use and environmental stewardship, aligning with UNESCO's sustainable development goals[2].

- **Future Readiness**: The school prepares students for tomorrow's world by integrating digitalization into instruction and developing the OECD's "4K" competencies—communication, creativity, critical thinking, and collaboration—as essential 21st-century skills[2].

The school operates under the **Carlchen-Modell**, a self-developed teaching model implemented since the 2024/2025 school year[2].

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Location and Setting**: Carl-Benz-Schule is located in Hermsdorf, a district of Reinickeldorf in Berlin, at Heidenheimer Straße 53-54[1][3]. The school occupies a spacious, green campus in a quiet environment[4].

**Enrollment**: The school serves approximately 430 students, making it one of Berlin's smaller secondary schools[3]. Student enrollment has remained relatively stable, ranging from 426 to 468 students over the past decade[1].

**Facilities and Infrastructure**[4][5]:
- Spacious, green school campus with barrier-free access
- Two computer rooms with 18 workstations each
- Media room for laptop-based work
- Computer workstations as media islands in classrooms
- Large school library with individual workstations
- Cafeteria and teaching kitchen
- Workshops for practical subjects (automotive, metalwork, woodwork, electronics)
- Two gymnasiums
- Artificial turf field with running track
- Instruction organized in grade-level houses

**Leadership**: Patrick Ulrich-Sroka serves as interim principal, with Frau Biessei as interim deputy principal[3].

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**School Type**: Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS) without a gymnasium upper level[1].

**Languages Offered**: English, French, and Spanish[1].

**Special Profile**: The school has an **Information Technology profile**, reflecting its comprehensive IT infrastructure and emphasis on digital competencies[1][10].

**Differentiation Model**: The Carlchen-Modell provides **internal differentiation** in core subjects[3][5]:
- Grades 7-8: German, English, and Mathematics are taught and assessed with internal differentiation
- Grades 9-10: Biology, Chemistry, and Physics are added to the differentiated subjects

**Academic Support**: The school offers targeted support including remedial instruction in core subjects and exam preparation[5].

**Graduation Pathways**: The school prepares students for various educational outcomes, with **above-average graduation rates** compared to similar schools in the district and across Berlin[3]. Students can fulfill foreign language requirements for the Abitur (university entrance qualification) at the school[3].

**Open All-Day Program**: The school operates an open all-day program with mandatory afternoon participation for all students, typically running from 8:00 AM to 2:30 PM, with additional afternoon activities available[3][5].

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**Extracurricular Activities**: The school offers numerous working groups (AGs), including football, track and field, and basketball[5].

**Practical Learning**: Workshops in practical subjects provide hands-on experience in automotive repair, metalwork, woodwork, and electronics[4].

**Career and Study Orientation**: The school provides structured offerings for career and study guidance[4].

**External Partnerships**: The school collaborates with external organizations including TÜV Rheinland Akademie GmbH and Lehr- und Ökobauhof Niederbarnim e.V.[5].

**Special Education Support**: The school has long-standing experience working with students with special educational needs[4].

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

Carl-Benz-Schule reports **graduation rates significantly above those of comparable schools** in the Reinickeldorf district and across Berlin[3]. The school attributes this success to its small size (approximately 400+ students), which enables individualized support, and its consistent focus on educational achievement[3].

The school's **Belastungsstufe (stress level) rating is 4**, indicating the socioeconomic challenges of the school's student population[1].

## 6. ADMISSIONS

**Enrollment Process**: The school conducts regular information sessions for students in grades 5-6 and their parents, held between autumn and winter holidays[2]. These sessions cover school information, the admission process, language offerings, and address individual questions[2].

**Open House**: The school hosts a Tag der offenen Tür (Open House) where prospective students and parents can observe instruction and facilities[2].

**Registration**: Interested families must register by phone with the school secretariat at (030) 90294 800 00, providing the desired date, family name, expected number of attendees, and the child's current school[2].

**Public School Status**: As a public school in Berlin, Carl-Benz-Schule is tuition-free[1].
