description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Carl-Bosch-Schule operates under the guiding principle **"Erfolgreich Zukunft planen"** (Successfully planning the future), emphasizing individualized learning, positive pedagogy, future orientation, and democracy.[1][3] Its educational approach includes a established school concept with Wahlpflicht-Unterricht (elective compulsory subjects), Lernatelier (learning workshops), temporary learning groups, practice-based learning, reading programs, project weeks, career orientation, and media education, supported by strong IT equipment and a collaborative team environment.[5] Key pillars are individualization through differentiated instruction in core subjects like Math, German, English, and sciences; a strengths-based "Positive Pedagogy – Glück" (happiness) concept; digital tools and career preparation for future professions; and fostering democratic responsibility.[1][3]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Located in the Hermsdorf neighborhood of Berlin-Reinickendorf at Frohnauer Str. 74-80, 13467 Berlin, the school serves approximately **400 students** in 16 regular classes, one welcome class, and two temporary learning groups for students needing support in cognitive, emotional, social, or learning development.[3][5] The community comprises students, engaged parents, about 40 teachers and staff, five social workers, leadership (Lisa Tolan and David Oels), administration, a janitor, and even a school dog.[5] Facilities include a modern sports hall with outdoor football/basketball areas, well-equipped workshops (wood, metal, electro, glass, textile, food), IT rooms, interactive whiteboards (chalk-free school), cafeteria, teaching kitchen, specialized workshops (textile, wood, metal, ceramics, bicycle), and barrier-free access with elevator and wheelchair-accessible WC.[8][9] It is a model school in the "Pädagogische Schulentwicklung" project for self-responsible learning and participates in hybrid teaching experiments, with open all-day care until 16:00.[2][8]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As an **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** (integrated secondary school, grades 7-10, without upper secondary), it offers differentiated instruction: inner differentiation (basic/advanced levels) in German, English, and Math until grade 8/10, then external courses; PROFI-courses for deepening skills in English/Math from grades 9/10.[5] Languages: English (1st foreign language for all, with PROFI options), French/Spanish (2nd foreign language electives).[5][8] MINT subjects (Math, Informatics, Physics, Chemistry, Biology) feature experiments and ITG (IT basics) in grade 8; GeWi (History, Politics, Ethics, Geography) includes excursions, witnesses, and "Glücksunterricht" (happiness lessons); Arts (Theater, Art, Music) via electives with practical projects; WAT (Economics-Work-Technology) in workshops with career prep; Sport in modern facilities.[5] Graduation pathways include **MSA+ (51%), MSA (26%), eBBR (9%)** (above district/Berlin averages), enabling vocational training, further education, or gymnasiale Oberstufe transition.[6] Open all-day with afternoons four days/week.[3]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in project weeks, excursions, career orientation (including internships, individual counseling), media education, reading bands, and Wahlpflicht electives like Theater (performances), Informatics, and PROFI-challenges in sports/Math/English.[1][5] Sports include hall classes plus outdoor fields, with personalized challenges in grade 7 PROFI.[5] Arts emphasize practical instrument playing, techniques, and ensemble work.[5] Additional experiences: Lernatelier/labs, temporary groups, hybrid learning, democracy projects, and all-day programs with varied afternoons.[3][8] The school fosters a supportive climate with social workers and parent involvement.[5]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
The school won the **Margot-Friedländer-Preis in 2017**, commemorated with a display case; Margot Friedländer (Holocaust survivor, honorary citizen) visited frequently, inspiring democratic values and remembrance.[5] MSA+ rates exceed district (43%) and Berlin (47%) averages at 51%; overall stable enrollment ~400 students with ~41-44 teachers.[2][6] Recognized as a model school for pedagogical development and self-responsible learning.[2][8]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **public tuition-free school**, enrollment is open via standard Berlin public school processes; contact for Anmeldung (enrollment) or info.[5] Open house: **20 January 2026, 16:00-18:30** (today).[5] Testing for new grade 7s occurs pre-start (e.g., weekends before August); first day includes welcome events.[7] Schulnummer: 12K10.[2]

description:
# Carlo-Schmid-Oberschule School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

The Carlo-Schmid-Oberschule is committed to providing **comprehensive education with specialized profiles** that allow students to develop individual strengths.[1][6] The school emphasizes **democratic school culture, ecological responsibility, and global learning**, as evidenced by its 2014 designation as a "Fair School" and its implementation of conflict resolution programs, environmental initiatives, and international partnerships.[1]

The school's pedagogical approach centers on **profile-based learning**, where each class from grade 7 onwards selects a specialized focus area including music, society and responsibility, natural sciences, work studies, art, or bilingual Spanish instruction.[6] This structure continues into the upper secondary level with diverse course offerings in both basic and advanced levels.

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Location and Basic Information:**
The Carlo-Schmid-Oberschule is located in Berlin-Spandau at Lutoner Straße 15/19, 13581 Berlin (Wilhelmstadt district).[2][8] The school building was constructed in the 1970s using steel-concrete skeleton construction and is currently undergoing comprehensive renovation.[2]

**Student and Staff Population:**
The school serves approximately **1,000 students** across six parallel classes (6-4 configuration) with **123 teaching staff members** and additional pedagogical support personnel.[2][9] The school operates as a **six-track all-day school** (Ganztagsschule) in partially bound form.[3]

**Facilities and Infrastructure:**
The school includes:[8]
- Four computer labs
- Science facilities equipped with computers
- Cafeteria
- Wheelchair-accessible elevator and accessible facilities
- Recently renovated schoolyard and sports field[3]
- Solar installation[1]
- "Green Classroom" (outdoor learning space)[1]

**Current Development:**
A project agreement for comprehensive school renovation was signed in September 2024, with planning expected to conclude by 2027 and construction completion targeted for 2031.[2]

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**Grade Structure and Pathways:**
The school serves students from grade 7 onwards as an **Integrated Secondary School with Upper Secondary Level (Gymnasium)**, enabling students to achieve all possible graduation qualifications.[3][8]

**Profile-Based Learning (Grades 7-10):**
Each class selects one of six specialized profiles:[6]
- **Music** (in cooperation with Spandau Music School)
- **Society and Responsibility**
- **Natural Sciences**
- **Work Studies**
- **Art**
- **Bilingual Spanish** (with special admission procedure)

**Language Offerings:**
- **First Foreign Language:** English
- **Second Foreign Language:** French or Spanish[3]
- **Special Program:** Bilingual Spanish profile with enhanced language instruction
- **Support:** German as a Second Language instruction available[8]

**Differentiation and Support:**
The school implements **performance-based differentiation** in:[8]
- Mathematics and English (from grade 7)
- German (from grade 8)
- Natural sciences (from grade 9)

Additional support includes double staffing in German, English, and Mathematics for integration classes, supplementary mathematics and science instruction in grade 7, and preparation for the MSA (Middle School Certificate).[8]

**Upper Secondary Level (Grades 11-13):**
The gymnasium offers diverse basic and advanced courses (Grund- and Leistungskurse) continuing the school's profile-based approach, including **Music as an advanced course option**.[3][6]

**Special Programs:**
- **Erasmus+ Accreditation:** Since 2023, the school has been Erasmus+ accredited, enabling group mobilities, long-term exchanges, and international projects with European partner schools.[6]
- **EU Ambassador Schools:** Participation in the EU Parliament's EU Ambassador Schools program since 2016, providing access to didactic materials and participation in European conferences.[6]
- **International Partnership:** Long-standing cooperation with El Salvador through KATE e.V.[1][3]

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**Extracurricular Activities and Student Enterprises:**

The school operates **Carlos Green Paper Shop**, a student-run enterprise managed by the grade 9 Work Studies elective course, where students gain practical business experience in sales, advertising, accounting, and purchasing departments.[3][6] The shop sells environmentally friendly school materials.

**Schmid's Print Studio:** Student-operated printing facility offering school merchandise including CSO shirts and hoodies.[6]

**International Exchange Programs:**
- Erasmus+ mobility programs with European partner schools (e.g., Solsona, Spain)
- Month-long exchange stipends for participating students
- Focus on intercultural exchange and language development[6]

**Academic Competitions and Events:**
- **The Big Challenge 2025:** Participation in Europe-wide English language competition with app-based preparation[6]
- **First Aid Day:** Annual training in emergency response and life-saving techniques[6]
- **NaWi Project Week:** Themed project weeks on topics like sustainable nutrition, conducted in cooperation with universities (e.g., University of Osnabrück)[6]

**Sports and Recreation:**
- Fair Play Sports Festival[1]
- Recently renovated schoolyard and sports field[3]

**Arts and Music:**
- **Darstellendes Spiel (Dramatic Arts):** Available as elective through upper secondary level[3]
- **Music Instruction:** Wahlpflicht (elective) music courses where students learn instruments[3]
- Music-focused profile with specialized instruction[1]

**Conflict Resolution and Democratic Culture:**
- Conflict mediator program (Konfliktlotsen)[1]
- High student satisfaction with respectful treatment documented in school inspection reports[1]

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

**Certifications and Awards:**
- **Fair School Certification (2014):** Awarded by EPIZ e.V. for commitment to fair trade and global learning[1]
- **Erasmus+ Accreditation (2023):** Recognition as accredited secondary school for European educational exchange programs[6]
- **EU Ambassador School Status:** Ongoing participation since 2016 in EU Parliament's educational initiative[6]

**Environmental and Sustainability Initiatives:**
- Solar installation on school building[1]
- Green Classroom for outdoor learning[1]
- Carlos Green Paper Shop promoting environmentally friendly products[1]
- Cross-curricular fair trade education projects[1]

**Academic Recognition:**
The school's **special profile in natural sciences, music, and art** creates high demand for enrollment, requiring adaptation of facilities to meet specialized space requirements.[2]

## 6. ADMISSIONS

**Enrollment Process for Grade 7 (School Year 2026/27):**

**Registration Period:** March 5-12, 2026[6]
- **Standard Hours:** Daily 8:20 AM - 2:00 PM
- **Extended Hours:** Thursdays 3:00 PM - 6:00 PM
- **Special Accommodation:** Students with special education status and parents are requested to schedule appointments for March 12

**Registration Method:** Online appointment booking system or telephone registration at 030 35 19 530[6]

**Upper Secondary Level Admissions (Grades 11-13):**

**Registration Period:** February 9-27, 2026[6]
**Required Documents:**
- Completed application form (available on upper secondary pages)
- Mid-year school report (Halbjahreszeugnis)
- Transfer recommendation form (Leitbogen) from previous school

**Contact:** Upper secondary coordinator Frau Blücher or secretariat for appointment scheduling[6]

**Bilingual Spanish Profile:** Requires special admission procedure with additional selection criteria[6]

**Public School Status:**
As a public school (öffentlich), the Carlo-Schmid-Oberschule is **tuition-free** with no enrollment fees or material costs charged to families.[2][8]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Carl-Zeiss-Oberschule prioritizes individualized student support to achieve optimal school qualifications, with no grade repetition or probation year in 7th grade, emphasizing promotion and challenge through multi-level teaching in the middle school, elective subjects, working groups, and ongoing development of a support concept.[1][7] Central to its profile is **global learning and sustainability**, supported by foreign language application, modern media use, and international contacts via **Erasmus+** and **UNESCO** programs; it fosters tolerance, openness, collaboration, and performance motivation in a community focused on independent learning, communicative skills, and team orientation.[1][3][4][7] As a UNESCO project school, it advances human rights, peace, and global heritage education, aiming to develop tolerant, worldly, and self-reliant personalities while preparing students for study or work in an inclusive, open learning climate.[3][4]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Located in Berlin's Lichtenrade neighborhood (Tempelhof-Schöneberg district) at Barnetstr. 12-14, 12305 Berlin, the school serves approximately 1036 students, with about 840 from the district; two-thirds are in grades 7-10.[1][2][6] It operates as a bound all-day school (GGB) with intensive support, including two teachers per middle school class for social competency development and collaboration with youth work providers; facilities include workshops for wood, metal, textiles, cooking, and arts.[1][7] Recognized as a model school for pedagogical development, it holds certifications like the Berlin Quality Seal for vocational orientation and features an engaged leadership team under Herr Alexander Wöß.[1][4][8]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
This integrated secondary school (ISS) spans **grades 7-13** with its own gymnasial upper level, enabling Abitur after 12 or 13 years; instruction is in German, with differentiated levels in German, English, and math from grade 7, and sciences from grade 9, plus support programs in these areas and presentation skills.[1][5][7] Language options include S1 (English/French), S2 (English/Latin), S4 (English/Spanish); advanced courses (Leistungskurse) cover art, biology, German, English, geography, history, math, physics, and political education.[5] Profiles emphasize media, environment, MINT (math, IT, natural sciences, technology), art, music, and sport; broad electives, projects, and off-site learning enrich the curriculum, with all qualifications from vocational readiness to Abitur available.[1][7] Possible graduates include:

| Qualification | Requirements |
|---------------|--------------|
| **Abitur** | After 12/13 years, meeting performance standards and passing exams[7] |
| **Fachhochschulreife (school part)** | After ≥2 upper-level semesters with sufficient performance[7] |
| **Mittlerer Schulabschluss (MSA)** | End of grade 10, passing MSA exams[7] |
| **Erweiterte Berufsbildungsreife** | End of grade 10, passing exams[7] |
| **Berufsbildungsreife** | End of grade 9/10, passing comparison tests[7] |
| **Berufsorientierender Abschluss** | End of grade 10, passing comparison tests[7][1]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
As a bound all-day school, it offers diverse educational and care programs with social-pedagogical support and youth partnerships; extracurriculars include extensive arts (e.g., baking, sculpture, comics, theater, dance, textiles, ceramics), vocational orientation (debate club, creative writing, mediation, recycling, student company/newspaper, UNESCO AG), sports (archery, soccer), and projects like GSV trips, Fackelschießen (torch shooting), and international exchanges (e.g., Konya/Turkey, Erasmus+/Comenius).[1][7] Recent activities feature boys' soccer team placing 3rd in district hall championship, first participation in Altsprachenfest (ancient languages festival), and environmental outings.[7] Open house is scheduled for January 24 (10-13 Uhr).[7]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Certified as a **UNESCO project school** with focuses on global learning, sustainability, human rights, peace, and heritage; awarded Berlin's **Quality Seal for excellent vocational orientation**.[1][3][8] Abitur pass rates exceed Berlin averages for integrated secondary schools, with 100% success in 2014/15; strong artistic-musical sector links learning to student diversity.[2][8] Ranked 28th overall on schulen.de; active in Comenius projects and model pedagogical development.[1][3]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **public, tuition-free** school (Öffentlich), enrollment follows Berlin public school processes with no fees mentioned; it welcomes all eligible students for grades 7-13, prioritizing individual potential in an inclusive setting (Förderbedarf KöMo focus).[1][7] Contact via phone (030 902778206), fax (030 902778209), or email (sekretariat@carl-zeiss-oberschule.de); next open house on January 24, 10-13 Uhr.[7]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Caspar-David-Friedrich-Schule (CDF) is an **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** with a strong emphasis on arts and music, named after the Romantic painter Caspar David Friedrich, highlighting special attention to subjects like music, art, dance, and theater throughout the school day.[3][1] Its guiding principle is **"Miteinander für das Leben lernen"** (Learning together for life), supported by differentiated elective courses, open all-day programs, and extracurricular activities tailored to student interests.[3] The school promotes inner and outer differentiation, including inclination classes in music, visual arts, movement, and language, with pathways to upper secondary education and Abitur preparation.[3][4]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Located in Berlin's **Marzahn-Hellersdorf district (Hellersdorf neighborhood)** at **Alte Hellersdorfer Str. 7, 12629 Berlin**, the public school (Schulnummer 10K07) serves a community with around 475-486 students and 47-49 teachers in recent years (2021/22-2023/24).[1][2] Facilities include a school garden, theater workshop, music/band room, theater props, ceramics workshop, libraries (large and small with internet cafe), student club with billiards, table football, table tennis, photo lab, media workshop (video, photography, sound), student radio, creative room, and computer room.[4] Community partnerships feature Förderband e.V. for youth programs until 17:00 daily, Sodexo for meals, and collaborations with local music schools and other institutions.[3][4][5]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Serving **grades 7-10** with a developing **gymnasiale Oberstufe (upper secondary level for Abitur)** starting 2025/26, the curriculum features arts/music profiling (Kunstprofilierung, Musikprofilierung, theater-focused), subjects like Gewi, Künste, Mathematics, Nawi (natural sciences), Languages (English as 1st foreign language, French as 2nd), Sport, and WAT.[1][3][4] Elective options, vocational orientation, dual learning (internships, application training), and open all-day operations support pathways to **Abitur** (via cooperations and new Oberstufe), **MSA** (Mittlerer Schulabschluss), **eBBR/BBR** (vocational qualifications).[3][4][6] School programs and inspections date back to 2009-2017, focusing on dual learning, performance differentiation, and PSE concepts.[scraped Curriculum Page]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in arts-focused extracurriculars like theater project "Plan B," artistic workshops (grades 7-10), reading club, school garden ("Essbare Schule"), climate events (e.g., European Climate Policy Day), memorial trips, toilet design, schoolyard projects, and exchanges (Switzerland, Berlin).[scraped Main Page][1][5][9] Sports and AGs (working groups) are offered, alongside healthy school meals, "40 Seconds Berlin," student club, radio, and events like Christmas market, World Book Day, and Welttag des Buches projects.[scraped Main Page][4] Social work and parent involvement enhance experiences.[scraped Main Page]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
In 2021, the school outperformed district/ city ISS averages in several metrics: **MSA with Sek2 transition: 44%** (Berlin 47%, district 38%); **no qualification: 2%** (district 10%); **BBR: 20%** (district 17%); Math MSA overall: 66% (district 38%).[6] Recognized for ecological initiatives like the sustainable school garden supplying students.[5] Steady enrollment growth to 486 students by 2023/24.[1] Prepares students well for gymnasiale Oberstufe transitions.[3]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **tuition-free public school**, enrollment for **grade 7 (2026/27)** occurs March 5-12, 2026; info events for parents January 12, 2026 (18:00); open house January 22, 2026 (17:00-19:00); Abitur intake info January 22 (18:00) and February 9 (17:30).[scraped Main Page][8] Criteria apply if oversubscribed; second educational pathway available.[4][8]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Clay-Schule is a **music-focused Integrierte Sekundarschule (integrated secondary school) with gymnasialer Oberstufe (upper secondary level leading to Abitur)**, emphasizing an innovative, flexible learning concept that integrates school, music, sports, and community under one roof.[1][2][8] It serves as one of three pilot schools in Berlin for modern educational buildings, supporting Lerncluster (learning clusters) organized by year groups, with flexible spaces for concentrated lessons, music rehearsals, and performances.[1][2][6] The school promotes integration, including for students with special needs, and maintains a strong commitment to historical remembrance, such as a Gedenkort (memorial site) for a former NS-Zwangsarbeiterlager (Nazi forced labor camp) on the site, created by students with the Museum Neukölln.[2][3][7]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded as part of Berlin's Gesamtschule reform in the 1970s, the school began with preparatory groups in 1975, moved to its first building in August 1975, and faced space shortages leading to multiple relocations across Neukölln sites until the new construction.[5] The current modern building at Neudecker Weg 22, 12355 Berlin-Neukölln, opened in October 2023 with a musical ceremony featuring the school's Big Band; it cost approximately 66-68 million Euros and accommodates over 1,000-1,100 students.[1][2][3][4] Facilities include a large concert-suitable Aula (auditorium), Mensa (cafeteria), Doppelsporthalle (double sports hall), Außensportanlage (outdoor sports area), Außenterrasse (outdoor terrace), flexible Lernterrassen (learning terraces), mobile Trennwände (partition walls), and spaces for music, sports, and community events; an internal "Schulstraße" connects areas.[1][2] The site previously housed a WWII forced labor camp, now commemorated.[2][3][6]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As an **Integrierte Sekundarschule with gymnasialer Oberstufe**, it covers secondary education from 7th grade through upper secondary, offering pathways including Abitur (gymnasial qualification); it includes berufliche Vorbereitung (vocational preparation) and integration for students with special needs.[8][9] The musicbetonte (music-emphasized) profile integrates MINT (STEM) elements, with flexible Räume for various learning forms and year-group Lerncluster.[1][2] Resources include IServ for home learning, LGL (Lern- und Gedenklabor, learning and memorial lab), Speiseplan (cafeteria menu), and Kalender (calendar).[Main Page]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
The school offers extensive extracurriculars centered on **music and performing arts**, including musicals ("Werwolf," "Blutwig und Ludwig," "Durch die Zeit," "Alice im Anderland"), concerts ("Musik bei Kerzenschein," "Summer Brass" with Big Band, Bläserklassen, and Orchester), theater ("Der Durchschnitts-mensch"), Dance- und Bandfestival, Rocktreff festival involvement, and Tanz in der Schule.[Main Page] Other activities encompass Schnuppertag (trial days), Skifahrt (ski trip), Finnlandaustausch (Finland exchange), CAM Ausbildungsmesse (career fair with 40+ exhibitors), Touch Tomorrow MINT truck for Oberstufe, Schüler*innen-haushalt (student budget initiative with 1,500 Euros), Orange Day (UN campaign against violence), Schulfest, and community events like Spreewild demonstration.[Main Page] Sports facilities support physical activities.[1][2]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific awards, rankings, or certifications are detailed, but the school is recognized as a pilot for innovative Berlin school construction, with a brilliante musikalische Eröffnung (brilliant musical opening) and praise from Senatorin Katharina Günther-Wünsch for its flexible, sustainable design supporting music and pedagogy.[2] Historical highlights include Schulgarten AG awards in 1993, international exchanges since 1991, and public resonance from events like "Clay Together" festival.[5] Student-led initiatives, such as the Gedenkort and career fair, demonstrate strong engagement.[2][Main Page]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **tuition-free public school (Öffentlich)**, enrollment for new 7th graders begins online from 07.01.2026 for booking appointments; a valid email is required for the digital Anmeldeformular (enrollment form) sent days before the appointment—no walk-ins without booking.[Main Page] Tag der offenen Tür (open house) is scheduled for 17.01.2026 (10:00-14:00), and Schnuppertag trial days start from 02.12.2025 (11 dates, online registration via homepage).[Main Page]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Elsa-Neumann-Schule emphasizes equal access to education regardless of background or financial means, aligning with Berlin's Elsa-Neumann-Stipendium for doctoral students.[2][4] It commits to the **Dalton-Plan**, promoting **self-organized learning** (also called kooperativ-offene Lernform), **competency development**, and student responsibility for learning success as part of its pedagogical realignment.[1][5] The school's Leitbild addresses power dynamics in society, fostering a modern approach with values of openness, diversity, and individual responsibility.[1]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded as a new **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** with gymnasialer Oberstufe, operations began in 2022/23 in a new building at Wartiner Str. 1-3, 13057 Berlin (Lichtenberg, Neu-Hohenschönhausen district; school number 11K14), following groundwork in April 2020 due to rising student numbers.[1][3][5][9] Named after physicist Elsa Neumann in 2024, the first woman to receive a doctorate from the University of Berlin.[1][10] Facilities include 2 computer rooms, music room, 2 art rooms, WAT room, teaching kitchen, library (under development as a learning center), cafeteria (Mensa with Luna Restaurant GmbH), interactive whiteboards in every classroom, LED-glass ceiling in the central aula/theater space, and a 2024 "Kunst am Bau" award-winning art installation by Birgit Schuh symbolizing diversity.[1] Contact: sekretariat@elsa-neumann-schule.de, 030 98312886; leadership: Schulleiter Herr Liegmann (schulleitung@elsa-neumann-schule.de).[9] Enrollment grew from 148 students (14 teachers) in 2022/23 to 328 (30 teachers) in 2023/24.[3]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Currently offers grades 7-10 and Willkommensklassen (welcome classes) for 2025/26, with gymnasialer Oberstufe (grades 11-13) starting in 2026 in cooperation with May-Ayim-Schule.[1][2][5] Lessons primarily in 45-minute blocks, with planned revisions; **teilgebundener Ganztagsbetrieb** (partial all-day program) in planning.[1] Qualifications: **BBR** (Berufsbildungsreife), **eBBR** (erweiterte BBR), **MSA** (Mittlerer Schulabschluss), **Abitur** (Allgemeine Hochschulreife).[1][5] Foreign languages: English, Spanish, French, Russian (also Latin per external source).[1][3] Wahlpflichtunterricht (WPU): NaWi (Physics, Biology, Chemistry), Sport (with aptitude test), WAT (Wirtschaft-Arbeit-Technik), Art; includes Teilungsunterricht (split classes), off-site learning, career counseling from grade 9, digital equipment.[1] Strong focus on berufsorientiertes Lernen (career-oriented learning) via numerous partners.[1]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Promotes self-responsible, cooperative learning with außerschulische Lernorte (off-site learning locations).[1] International partnership with Collège Jean Jaurès in Pantin, Paris, via Erasmus program.[1][5] Berufsberatung (career counseling) from grade 9; modern facilities support arts (music/art rooms, Kunst am Bau), tech (computer rooms, WAT, kitchen), sports, and social competency development.[1] Open Day: January 14, 2026, 16-18 Uhr.[1] Books lists and enrollment forms for grade 7 available.[1] Partial all-day operation planned.[1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Students achieved successes in "Känguru der Mathematik" competition and 3rd place for Josua in national Informatik-Biber (BWINF) contest with over 500,000 participants.[1] Features a motivated teaching team and modern digital equipment; growing enrollment reflects community demand.[1][3]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **public (öffentlich), tuition-free** school, admission is open; Anmeldebogen (enrollment form) for grade 7 available for download, along with other documents.[1] Willkommensklassen for integration; no probezeit (trial period) mentioned, with regular promotion to next grade.[1][7] Open Day on January 14, 2026, for interested families; career counseling and partnerships support transitions.[1] Sekretariat hours: Mon-Fri 07:45-15:00.[9]

description:
# Ernst-Adolf-Eschke-Schule für Gehörlose: School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

The **Ernst-Adolf-Eschke-Schule** is a **special education center (Sonderpädagogisches Förderzentrum) specializing in deaf and hard of hearing education**[1][4]. The school's core educational philosophy centers on a **bimodal-bilingual approach** that integrates spoken German, German Sign Language (Deutsche Gebärdensprache/DGS), and written German in instruction[4][9]. Mastery of sign language is a mandatory requirement for all teaching staff, as DGS serves as the primary instructional language in most contexts[1].

The school serves students from across Berlin's entire city area, offering comprehensive support from early intervention through vocational training[1].

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

The school is named after **Ernst Adolph Eschke (1766-1811)**, a pedagogue and founder of Berlin's oldest special education institution (the Taubstummeninstitut/deaf-mute institute)[2].

**Location and Facilities:**
The school is located at **Waldschulallee 29, 14055 Berlin** in the Westend district of Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf[1][3]. The campus was constructed in 1959-1960 by the Charlottenburg Building Authority and was designed to accommodate 160-200 students[2]. The facility comprises:

- Three two-story classroom buildings
- One three-story administrative building
- One single-story side wing containing a gymnasium and auditorium[1][2]

The modernist steel-reinforced concrete structure features a facade articulated by pillars and window/parapet bands, with colored plaster surfaces in yellow, red, green, and gray[2].

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**Grade Levels and Educational Stages:**
The school offers instruction across multiple educational levels[1][8]:

- **Primary Education:** Grundschule (elementary school)
- **Secondary Education:** Sekundarstufe I (lower secondary)
- **Support Classes:** Förderklassen
- **All-Day Care:** Ganztag – EFöB (extended educational and care program)

**Vocational and Professional Training:**
The school provides comprehensive career preparation pathways[1][8]:

- Dual apprenticeships (Duale Ausbildungen)
- Integrated vocational preparation (Integrierte Berufsausbildungsvorbereitung/IBA)
- Social assistance training (Sozialassistenzausbildung)
- Professional schools (Berufsschule, Berufsfachschule, Fachschule)

**Instructional Approach:**
The bimodal-bilingual curriculum integrates spoken language, sign language, and written language throughout all subject areas[4][9]. The school also offers early intervention (Frühförderung) services[1][8].

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**Special Support Services and Programs:**
The school provides several enrichment and support offerings[8]:

- Reading promotion (Leseförderung)
- School social work (Schulsozialarbeit)
- Student enterprises (Schülerfirmen), including:
  - EAE Media
  - Händewerk (crafts)
  - Café Eschke
  - TextilSalon (textile salon)
- Therapy services
- Media education (Medienbildung)
- Diagnostics services
- Internship opportunities

**Research and Partnerships:**
The school participates in international research projects, including an Erasmus+ initiative (2018-2026) titled "Promoting Deaf and Hard of Hearing Children's Theory of Mind and Emotion Understanding," led by Prof. Dr. Claudia Becker[7].

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

The search results do not contain specific information about awards, rankings, or standardized test outcomes. However, the school's participation in international Erasmus+ research projects indicates recognition as a center of expertise in deaf education[7].

## 6. ADMISSIONS

As a **public school (öffentlich), the Ernst-Adolf-Eschke-Schule is tuition-free**[3][4]. The school is open to students from all Berlin districts (Schülerschaft kommt aus dem gesamten Stadtgebiet)[2]. Specific enrollment procedures and requirements are not detailed in the available search results, though interested families can contact the school directly.

**Contact Information:**
- **Telephone:** +49 30 902925000
- **Fax:** +49 30 902925033
- **Email:** sekretariat@eschke.schule.berlin.de
- **Office Hours:** Monday-Thursday 7:10 AM - 2:00 PM; Friday 7:10 AM - 12:30 PM[6]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Ernst-Haeckel-Schule aims to impart comprehensive, application-oriented general knowledge, work and learning techniques, and foster self-confident, tolerant, engaged, and open-minded young people, emphasizing both theoretical education and practical skills.[5][6] It operates as a **Sekundarschule mit gymnasialer Oberstufe** (integrated secondary school with upper secondary level) featuring a **mathematisch-naturwissenschaftliches Profil** (mathematics and natural sciences focus), alongside options for musisch-künstlerisches (arts), historisch-geografisches (history-geography), and sportliches (sports) profiles.[1][2][3] The school promotes inner differentiation in core subjects like math, German, English, and sciences, with support for both stronger and weaker learners, and holds the title "Schule ohne Rassismus - Schule mit Courage" (School without Racism - School with Courage).[3]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 1991 as a Gesamtschule (comprehensive school) with gymnasialer Oberstufe, the school was renamed after naturalist Ernst Haeckel in 1995 and relocated to its current site in 2002/2003 after renovations; a new building for German, social studies, and upper secondary spaces was added in 2023.[2][5] Located in Berlin's **Marzahn-Hellersdorf** district (Hellersdorf neighborhood), it serves around **886 students** (2023/24) with **77 teachers**, plus social pedagogues and educators, across grades 7-13.[1][5] Facilities include a main building plus four others (including containers), two gymnasiums, a sports field, eight science labs, two IT rooms (80 computers/48 laptops), library, theater room, workshops (wood/metal, electronics, cooking, sewing), art rooms, keyboard room, renovated cafeteria, and a multi-purpose room.[2][3][5]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As an **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)**, it covers secondary levels (Sekundarstufe I: grades 7-10, with MSA qualification) through gymnasiale Oberstufe (grades 11-13, leading to **Abitur**); offers part-time all-day operation (teilgebundener Ganztagsbetrieb), dual learning, and second educational pathway.[1][3][5] Core focus is mathematics-natural sciences, with electives in arts, history-geography, or sports; languages include **Englisch/Französisch** (S1) and **Englisch/Spanisch** (S4).[2][3] Curriculum pages detail subject-specific content from departments, documenting student work.[scraped] Upcoming events include Abitur enrollment info session on February 10, 2026, and school enrollment for 2026/27 from March 5-12, 2026.[scraped]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in sports like the "Jugend trainiert für Olympia" volleyball tournament (Dec 2025), Nikolausturnier basketball tradition (over 20 years), and events like Tag der Mathematik (Jan 26).[1][scraped] Project weeks cover topics such as "Jüdisches Leben" (Jewish Life) for grade 9 (Nov 2025), with district walks.[scraped] Arts include theater projects since 2008 (e.g., Theatertreffen Hellersdorf), vorlesewettbewerb (reading competition), and facilities for drama, music, and crafts.[3][8][scraped] Extracurriculars feature info afternoons for grades 5/6 (Jan 13, 2026) and upper secondary orientation.[scraped]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Teacher **Antje Liebau** received the Marzahn-Hellersdorf Schulpreis for outstanding performance (awarded Nov 3, 2025).[scraped] The school participates successfully in regional sports events and maintains steady enrollment growth (476 in 2015/16 to 886 in 2023/24).[1][scraped] It holds anti-racism certification and supports high achievement via differentiated programs and Abitur pathway (131 upper secondary students in 2020).[2][3]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **public, tuition-free** school, enrollment for 2026/27 occurs March 5-12, 2026; info events include grade 6 afternoon (Jan 13, 2026) and upper secondary session (Feb 10, 2026, 6 PM).[scraped][3] Open to local and external students; downloadable forms for Abitur track; contact sekretariat@haeckel-os.de or +49 30 9909332.[4][scraped] Principal: Jörn-Peter Roloff.[4]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Ernst-Reuter-Schule embodies the motto "Hören. Sehen. Lernen." ("Listen. See. Learn."), emphasizing comprehensive listening to students' voices, recognizing individual talents in diverse groups, and fostering critical, multiperspectival worldviews through differentiated instruction and project-based learning.[2] Named after Ernst Reuter, a politician and teacher who championed freedom, democracy, tolerance, and community, the school's Leitbild draws from Rainer Maria Rilke, promoting a participatory school community where students and attentive adults co-shape an evolving educational process focused on competence acquisition, personality development, and communal living.[1][2] Education is viewed as a dynamic process integrating theoretical sciences with practical projects to enable self-directed learning, resilience against ideologies, and responsible agency.[2]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded and named in 1953 after Ernst Reuter's death, the school honors his post-WWII legacy as Berlin's mayor advocating democracy amid division, located near the Berlin Wall Memorial in a multicultural Berlin-Mitte/Wedding area.[1][2] It serves ~1000 students from grades 7-13 with ~130 teachers, educators, and social workers, each class supported by dual class leaders and a dedicated educator/social worker for regular goal and progress discussions.[1][2] Facilities include **Science Space**, **Art Space**, **Social Space**, **Tech Space** for project hours; Makerspace (robotics), Vivarium, beekeeping, school garden, workshops, digital media tools (film, art, design, school radio), and a modern career orientation Dialog-Raum.[2] Centrally accessible via U6 (Voltastraße/Bernauer Straße), Tram M10, and bike.[1][2]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As an **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)**, it offers grades 7-13 (13 years total), with no probationary year and an extended 11th grade for Abitur preparation; features broad upper secondary courses like theater, philosophy, informatics, and economics.[1][2] Instruction is fully **binnendifferenziert** (in-class differentiated) with varied methods, interdisciplinary approaches, and Spaces for non-canonical projects; recognized as a **Bega-Schule** for gifted education.[2] A **UNESCO-Projektschule** emphasizes sustainability (school garden, student firms in catering/textiles), intercultural learning, human rights, and Erasmus+ international exchanges; integrates digital tools (schul.cloud, EduPage).[2] Public pathways include Abitur (recent average 2.52, best since 2001); supports MSA and BBR via comprehensive secondary structure.[1]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in **Spaces** (Science, Art, Social, Tech) for project-based deepening of interests, strengthening class cohesion; extracurriculars include Makerspace robotics, Vivarium, beekeeping, school garden, workshops, digital media (film, art, design, school radio), and career Dialog-Raum.[2] **UNESCO** focus offers sustainability via student firms, intercultural/human rights projects with Berlin Wall Memorial cooperation, and Erasmus+ exchanges (e.g., France, Poland).[1][2] Mental health program "Me & We" highlighted in media; Profilwoche events (cooking, baking, school beautification); Bilanz- und Zielgespräche for personalized guidance; no specific sports mentioned, but arts via Art Space/Theater courses.[2]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Received **Erasmus+ Qualitätssiegel** (90+ of 100 points) for international projects.[2] Abitur average of **2.52** (highest since 2001).[1] Featured in Spiegel for "Me & We" mental health program; maintains Pressespiegel for media coverage; UNESCO-Projektschule status and Bega-Schule recognition.[2] ~1000 students, 130 staff, ongoing Profilwoche and open house events.[1][2]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a tuition-free public school (Öffentlich), enrollment follows Berlin's standard process post-primary (no tuition, probe year, or fees noted); open to grades 7+ via central allocation ("Wohin nach der Grundschule?").[2] Hosts **Tag der offenen Tür** (Jan 23, 2026, 13:00-18:00) for info on offerings; Bilanzgespräche (Jan 28-29, 2026) for enrolled students/parents; contact via website, email (verwaltung@ernst-reuter-schule.berlin), phone (+4930467779910).[1][2]

poi_kita_03_name: Kita an der Hochschule für Technik Berlin
description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The school's motto, **"Wir wollen, dass du es schaffst!"** ("We want you to succeed!"), emphasizes student success and support.[1][6] It promotes an inclusive, supportive approach with extensive beratung (counseling) including schulsozialarbeit, psychological help, learning/German promotion, tutoring, exam prep, methods weeks, and learning discussions.[1][6] As a berufsorientierte ISS with "Brücke-Schule" profile, it focuses on practical, dual learning bridging school and work via close ties with businesses.[2][5]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Located in **Berlin-Mitte (Wedding), Lütticher Str. 47-48, 13353 Berlin**, this public ISS serves a diverse community with multiple nationalities noted (exact numbers not specified).[1][2][4] Leadership includes **Schulleiterin Dr. Beyer**, deputy Herr Feldkamp, and coordinator Frau Follert.[4][8] Facilities include library, two computer rooms, learning offices, teaching kitchen, sewing workshop, pottery workshop, mensa (cafeteria), workshops, and specialist rooms; limited parking, good public transport access (U6/U9, Tram 13/50), mostly stair access (not fully barrier-free).[4][5] Enrollment grew from 487 students/54 teachers (2011/12) to 583/62 (2023/24); founding year listed but unspecified in content.[2][6]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
**Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** for grades **7-10**, offering all secondary qualifications up to Abitur (after 13 years, unlike standard Gymnasium).[1][3] Core subjects: Mathematik, Naturwissenschaften, Gesellschaftswissenschaften, Deutsch, Fremsprachen (Englisch primary; Spanisch/Französisch from grade 7 for 4 years; Chinesisch/Türkisch/Russisch from grade 9), Sport, WAT/ITG (Wirtschaft, Arbeit, Technik – dual learning focus), Kunst/Musik, Wahlpflichtfächer.[1][2][5][6] Special programs: Produktives Lernen (PL, high practice share), Praxislernen (PLG), Move Plus (individual support), Förderunterricht, Berufsorientierung (BSO) with praktika, firm visits, apprenticeships; inner differentiation grades 7-8, outer grades 9-10; ganztag (all-day) to 16:00 with structured offers.[1][3][5] Abschlüsse include MSA, BBR pathways via duales Lernen (WAT lead).[1][3]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
**Ganztag offerings**: Freizeit-/pausenangebote (AUB), Arbeitsgemeinschaften (AGs, e.g., sport, theater, art, music, student work hours), mensa.[1][5][6] Extracurriculars: Sprachreisen (language trips), international exchanges (e.g., Jeddah, Saudi Arabia), digital projects like "Voneinander Lernen!" with First Lady Elke Büdenbender and Liz Mohn.[1][6] Berufs prep: Schnupperpraktika, lab praktika at Bayer Schering Pharma, schülerfirmen.[5] Additional: Hausaufgabenhilfe, nachhilfe.[6]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Participation in national digital project **"Voneinander Lernen!"** (2025) with high-profile figures.[1][6] New school website launch (Sep 2025).[1][6] Cooperation with Jüdisches Museum against antisemitism (visit now mandatory).[10] Berufsbetontes profile with business ties ("Brücke-Schule"); Europäischer Computer-Führerschein offered.[2][5] No specific awards, rankings, or Prüfungsergebnisse detailed in content.[5]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **tuition-free public school**, enrollment follows Berlin public processes (e.g., Oberschulmessen, Tage der offenen Tür).[2] Open house: **Jan 22, 2026, 16:00-19:00**.[1][6] Contact sekretariat (Mo-Fr 08:00-14:00, tel 030 45 30 87 10, email sekretariat@01k02.schule.berlin.de) for info; krankmeldung via phone/Webuntis by 08:00, written excuse on return.[1][4][6] No fees or special requirements noted beyond standard ISS assignment.[2]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Evangelische Schule Charlottenburg operates under the motto **"verstehen bilden handeln"** (understand, educate, act), emphasizing strengthening children and youth to unfold their potential, assume responsibility for themselves, others, and creation, and provide a solid foundation for further life and education.[1][2][3] As an evangelical school in the sponsorship of the Evangelische Schulstiftung EKBO, it is open to diverse faiths and worldviews, with evangelical religious education as a core subject focusing on questions of belief, ethics, and responsible living in a pluralistic world.[1] The philosophy prioritizes individual support, potential development, and ethical responsibility, guided by Christian values like "Gott spricht: Ich mache alles neu!" (God says: I make all things new!).[1][2]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
No specific founding date is mentioned in the content. The school is located at **Guerickestr. 4-6, 10587 Berlin (Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf district)**, serving as a private evangelical institution under the Evangelische Schulstiftung EKBO.[1][5][8][9] It fosters a close-knit community with small class sizes (two-stream ISS to prevent anonymity), dual tutors per class, sonderpädagogik support across all grades, a school social worker, and partnerships with external providers like Intellego for after-school tutoring.[1][4][6] Facilities include an open all-day program (Hort/Ganztagsschule) with dedicated spaces (e.g., Ganztagsbereich at Alt Lietzow 30), a school canteen (Schulmensa), and digital learning via the "it's learning" platform since 2019 for materials, individualized paths, grades, and homework.[1] Staff includes expanded leadership, subject coordinators, teachers, social pedagogues, a janitor, secretaries, and trainees.[5][6]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The school spans **grades 1-10** as a combined primary and **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)**, leading to **mittlerer Schulabschluss (MSA), MSA+, or (e)BBR** (no Abitur; not a Gemeinschaftsschule, requiring new applications post-grade 10).[1][2][4] Core elements include evangelical religious education as a main subject, mandatory elective courses (Wahlpflichtunterricht): grade 5 offers artistic/musical/handcrafting/culinary options; grade 6 focuses on social learning; grades 7-10 require binding choices in "Arbeits- und Ernährungslehre" (work/nutrition, including food processing, wood/textile work, computers), **French**, or **natural sciences**.[curriculum page] Languages include German, French (elective), and subject-specific instruction; career preparation integrates across programs with berufsorientierung and business/work/technic courses.[1][curriculum page] Support includes diagnostics, individual Förderung in all sonderpädagogik areas, and collaborations with upper secondary centers and evangelical schools.[1][4]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in **open all-day care (Hort/Ganztagsschule)** with tutoring hours, working groups (Arbeitsgemeinschaften), elective courses in arts, music, crafts, social learning, French, sciences, and practical skills like computer and material processing.[1][curriculum page] Additional offerings include career counseling (Berufsorientierung), student advising, Freiwilligendienste (voluntary services), school partnerships, and a gallery of activities; sports, art, and music are coordinated across primary and secondary levels.[1][4][6][curriculum page] Digital tools via "it's learning" enable personalized learning; no specific exchanges or sports details are listed, but practical berufsleben preparation occurs throughout.[1][curriculum page]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No awards, rankings, or specific certifications are mentioned. The school aims for all students to achieve an ability-appropriate **(e)BBR, MSA, or MSA+** without leaving without a qualification, supported by tutoring, partnerships, and post-grade 10 pathways to upper schools or apprenticeships.[4]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
**Enrollment process**: Download and submit the Anmeldeformular (intake application) in person at the secretariat, by post, or password-protected email (due to data protection); separate form for OGS (open Ganztagsschule).[admissions page] Contact secretariat (Nicole Jahnke, Kathleen Mrowietz; 030 343571610/620; hours: Mo 08:00-13:30, Di/Do/Fr 08:00-14:30, Mi 08:00-10:00/13:00-14:30) or Ganztagsbereich (Monika Lemke; 030 33091894; Mo 14-16 or by appointment).[5][admissions page] No requirements specified beyond forms; personal discussions or open days available.[1]

**Fees**: No tuition amounts, registration fees, material fees, meal costs, after-school care costs, scholarships, or income-based options are mentioned on web pages. PDFs (e.g., Grund- und Hausordnung) are listed but marked as unlikely fee-related; contact school for details.

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Evangelische Schule Neukölln (ESN) follows a **holistic pedagogy** that promotes independent thinking, feeling, and acting among students through work, play, and celebration.[1] It operates as a state-recognized public school rooted in the Christian tradition of the Bible and history, maintained by the Schulstiftung der Evangelischen Kirche Berlin-Brandenburg-schlesische Oberlausitz, while embracing ecumenical and interreligious dialogue and welcoming students from other confessions and religions.[1] The Christian profile is evident in mandatory religion classes, devotions, and services, with opportunities to address ethical issues from a Christian perspective across subjects; it contributes to educational equity by avoiding early tracking into specific school tracks.[1]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
No specific founding date or historical details are provided in the content.[1] The school is located at **Mainzer Str. 47, 12053 Berlin** in the Neukölln district and serves as a private, state-recognized institution open to diverse religious backgrounds.[1] Contact details include phone (+49(0)30 624 20 27), fax (+49(0)30 624 20 28), and email (buero@evangelische-schule-neukoelln.de), with secretariat hours: Mon/Wed/Fri 08:00–13:45, Tue/Thu 08:00–15:00.[1] No explicit facilities details are mentioned beyond a coordinating educator in the ground-floor Hort (after-school care) and standard classroom structures implied by lesson times.[1]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
ESN spans **classes 1-13**, combining a Grundschule (primary), Integrierte Sekundarschule (integrated secondary), and gymnasiale Oberstufe (upper secondary), allowing all general education qualifications without early tracking.[1] Subjects include **Biologie, Kunst, Chemie, Deutsch, Englisch, Geografie, Geschichte, Mathematik, Musik, Physik, Religion, Sport, and WAT (grades 8/9)**.[1] Religion is mandatory, integrated with Christian ethics across subjects; the school is state-recognized, enabling standard Berlin qualifications like MSA (Mittlerer Schulabschluss), Fachabitur, and Abitur.[1]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars emphasize music and history: Class 10A's "Musik macht Geschichte" project in Oranienburg (2025/26), Class 10c's remembrance trip to Krakau and Auschwitz (Nov 2025), and music trips by Sek I choir and orchestra AG.[1] Community activities include a devotion on the Ukraine war by teachers and students, student council meetings (SV-Tagung 21.11.2025), and Hort programs during autumn holidays 2025.[1] Sports and arts are core subjects; no exchanges are specified.[1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No awards, rankings, certifications, or specific outcomes (e.g., graduation rates) are mentioned in the content.[1]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
**Enrollment deadlines**: Grade 1: Fri, 19.06.2026; Grade 7: Wed, 21.01.2026 (today is 20.01.2026, so Grade 7 deadline is tomorrow).[1] Detailed info in PDFs: Grade 7 process (https://evangelische-schule-neukoelln.de/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Informationen-zum-Anmeldeverfahren-fuer-die-7.-Klasse-2026_2027.pdf) and Grade 1 (https://evangelische-schule-neukoelln.de/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Anmeldung-ESN-1.-Klasse-zum-01.08.2027-AKTUELL.pdf).[1] After-school care (Hort) for grades 1-6: Free core from 7:30-13:30 (halbtags Grundschule); paid modules: 6:00-7:30 (early), 13:30-16:00 (afternoon, free for grades 1-2), 16:00-18:00 (late); combinable, with ferienbetreuung for grades 5-6 (included for 1-4).[1] Apply via Betreuungsgutschein at Amt für Tagesbetreuung, then school contract; forms: Antrag auf Betreuung (https://evangelische-schule-neukoelln.de/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/antrag-efoeb-ogb.pdf), Hinweise (https://evangelische-schule-neukoelln.de/wp-content/uploads/2018/12/Unterlagen-Hort.pdf), Erläuterungen zur Beteiligung (https://evangelische-schule-neukoelln.de/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/erlaeuterungen-zur-kostenfestsetzung-2018-hort.pdf), Erklärung (https://evangelische-schule-neukoelln.de/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/erklaerung-fuer-die-festsetzung-der-hortkosten.pdf).[1] **Tuition**: Income-dependent Schulgeld (details on Schulstiftung homepage, not specified here); no exact EUR amounts for tuition, registration, materials, or meals listed on pages.[1] Contact coordinator Frau Stöwe (Tel: 6200991-32, stoewe@evangelische-schule-neukoelln.de) for care queries.[1]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The school's mission emphasizes "Lernen mit Freunden" (Learning with Friends), fostering a familial atmosphere where social learning, successful collaboration, and Christian values like "Voneinander lernen. Miteinander arbeiten. Fürinander leben." (Learn from each other. Work together. Live for each other) are central.[2][1] It promotes holistic education with a strong **sozial-diakonisches Profil** (social-diakonic profile), openness to all regardless of background, mandatory evangelical religious education, and participation in church events like Andachten, Gottesdienste, and school festivals.[2][1][9] The philosophy prioritizes stable frameworks for the entire school career, social behavior training, and relationships as key to successful learning.[1][5]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded around 1951 as a small one-room school in the Melanchthonhaus on the Evangelisches Johannesstift campus, the school celebrated its 75th anniversary in 2026, evolving into a comprehensive institution from Grundschule to Abitur.[5] It is owned by the Evangelische Schulstiftung Berlin-Brandenburg (EKBO Schulstiftung), a church foundation established in 2004 now overseeing 33 schools.[6][1] Located on the expansive Johannesstift grounds in Berlin-Spandau (Hakenfelde), it serves as a Stadtteilschule for northern Spandau and collaborates closely with Johannesstift facilities for elderly care, disability support, and youth services, including intergenerational projects like "Generationenbrücke."[1][3][2] Facilities include a main building with specialized rooms for music, chemistry, physics, cooking, crafts, computers, and an aula; pavilion-style classroom buildings; a Kunstraum; spaces in Gustav-Werner-Haus and Reinhard-Lange-Haus since 2008; and bright classrooms with flexible work areas.[1]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
This state-recognized private school offers continuous education from **Grundschule** (grades 1-6, zweizügig with year-homogeneous groups and cross-grade projects), **Integrierte Sekundarschule** (grades 7-10), to **gymnasiale Oberstufe** (grades 11-13, small courses).[1][2][3] Curriculum follows state Rahmenpläne and Versetzungsbeschlüsse, including evangelical religion as a core subject, technical-natural science in grade 4, differentiation in subjects like math, English, German, and chemistry, and dual learning elements like internships and social praktika.[1][8][9] Graduation pathways include **Berufsbildungsreife**, **Mittlerer Schulabschluss (MSA)**, and **Abitur**.[3] Inklusive programs like "Schule ohne Grenzen" integrate with the August Hermann Francke Schule; materials are loaned; assessments are process-oriented with verbal reports in early grades.[1][4][9]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
The school emphasizes social-diakonic engagement through collaborations with Johannesstift (e.g., social praktika, "Chance auf Schule" for grades 7-8 at-risk students), Wahlpflichtfach Sozial-Diakonisches Lernen, and projects like "Orientierungs- und Bildungszentrum" for school-to-work transitions.[3][5][8] Extracurriculars include jahrgangsübergreifende projects, Generationenbrücke visits to senior homes, außerschulische Bildungsangeboten focused on nature, sustainability, and inclusion, school events (Einschulungsfeste, Erntedankfest, Weihnachten, Fasching, Abschlussball), after-school Hort, and involvement in Wettbewerben.[1][3][2] Sports, arts, and exchanges are not explicitly detailed beyond Kunstraum, Musikraum, and Werkraum facilities.[1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific awards, rankings, or quantitative outcomes like Prüfungsergebnisse are detailed in the content, though the gymnasiale Oberstufe received Senatsverwaltung recognition around the 2025/2026 Jubiläum.[5] Achievements highlighted include growth from a small school to comprehensive coverage (Grundschule to Abitur), high student motivation in projects, and strong partnerships.[5]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
Admissions require an **Anmeldebogen** (non-binding interest form) submitted to the Sekretariat, potentially followed by an Aufnahmegespräch or Hospitieren; no fixed criteria, but priority for siblings and internal transfers (e.g., Grundschule to class 7).[Admissions Page] Deadlines: Grundschule 17.10.2025; Sekundarschule class 7: 23.01.2026; Oberstufe via coordinator (*protected email*).[Admissions Page] Church membership desired but not required; openness to Christian content and mandatory religion/Events expected.[Admissions Page][9] Open spots in classes 7 and 11; high demand limits interviews.[Main Page] **Tuition**: Starts at **30€/month**, income- and family-size scaled (Ermäßigungen for siblings, full exemptions possible); details via Schulstiftung website.[Admissions Page][Curriculum Page] No specifics on registration, materials (loaned), meals (Mensaplan mentioned but no costs), or Hort fees. ### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Evangelische Schule Steglitz operates as a "Haus des Lebens und Lernens" (House of Life and Learning), emphasizing a **familial school atmosphere**, integration of **Evangelical religious education** as a regular subject, and alignment with state school frameworks for curricula and promotion decisions. Its school program, titled "Aus der Vergangenheit lernen – Gegenwart gestalten – Zukunft vorbereiten" (Learn from the past – shape the present – prepare for the future), serves as the "director's script" outlining key priorities for implementation, approved by the school conference on June 17, 2015.[1][2] Core values include community celebrations, festivals as part of school life, and a quote from Friedrich Fröbel: "Erziehung ist: Beispiel. Und Liebe. Sonst nichts." (Education is: example. And love. Nothing else.).[1]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in **1948**, this historic private school in southwest Berlin focuses on a supportive community environment, serving students from grades 1-13 in a church-sponsored (EKBO) structure.[1][2] Located at **Beymestr. 6-7, 12167 Berlin** in the **Steglitz-Zehlendorf district**, it fosters a diverse, inclusive community evident in events like the school festival on September 26, 2025, featuring shared activities for students, parents, teachers, alumni, and volunteers, including sports fields, gardens with home-grown vegetables, and sustainability exhibits.[1] Facilities support all-day care, animals on campus, and project days; no additional details on buildings or capacity provided.[1]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a **state-recognized primary school (Grundschule), integrated secondary school (Integrierte Sekundarschule, ISS), and gymnasiale Oberstufe (upper secondary with gymnasium track)**, it covers **grades 1-13** with curricula and promotion rules matching state schools, including Evangelical religious instruction.[1] The integrated model supports pathways to **Abitur** via the gymnasiale Oberstufe; MSA (Mittlerer Schulabschluss) and BBR (Berufsbildungsreife) likely available through the ISS structure, though not explicitly stated.[1] Timetables (Zeittafel) and school program PDF detail scheduling; digital education emphasized via Fobizz certification.[1]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in **diverse extracurriculars** including Arbeitsgemeinschaften (clubs), **sport courses**, music events, all-day care activities, and sustainability projects like vegetable gardens and peace-building crafts.[1] Community-building includes school festivals with games, markets, grilling, memory games, and swap meets; conflict mediation via Konfliktlotsen, student counseling, and class councils.[1] Ganztagsbetreuung (all-day care) features regular events; animals are present on campus; timetables support structured days.[1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
The school holds the **Fobizz-Siegel**, recognizing exceptional commitment to **digital education and teacher training**.[1] Successful community events like the 2025 school festival highlight creativity and diversity; no specific academic outcomes, rankings, or graduation rates mentioned.[1]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
**Admissions** are level-specific: Grundschule (grade 1) via non-binding pre-registrations May to early summer holidays prior year, with lottery if oversubscribed; requires church affiliation form and feedback ~6 weeks post-summer break.[1] ISS (grade 7) deadline January 21, 2026, needing grade 5 half-year 2 report; feedback aligns with state timelines.[1] Gymnasiale Oberstufe (grade 11) from November to February 14 prior year, via secretariat.[1] Open days: January 13, 2026 (ISS/GO), January 16 (Sek II/GO).[1] Contact: Karen Meincke & Johanna Culjak, protected email, 030 7916001/0021.[1]

**Fees**: Monthly **Schulgeld starts at 30€**, income-scaled by family size and earnings; sibling discounts; full exemptions possible. description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Ferdinand-Freiligrath-Schule is an **integrierte Sekundarschule (integrated secondary school) with gymnasialer Oberstufe im Verbund**, emphasizing communal learning and living among children from diverse cultures, performance levels, interests, talents, personalities, and backgrounds.[1][2] Core values include fostering individual strengths and creativity, intercultural competencies, openness, non-violence, mutual respect, tolerance, responsibility, and team-based social skills development.[1][2][3] The educational approach prioritizes exciting, demanding instruction through project-oriented, independent learning in interdisciplinary contexts, collaboration with professionals from various fields, and language/reading proficiency as a foundation for communication and achievement, aiming to prepare students for fulfilling careers and optimal life planning via the best possible school qualifications.[1][2][3]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Located in Berlin's Friedrichshain-Kreuzberg district (Kreuzberg area) at Bergmannstraße 64, 10961 Berlin (school number 02K10), the public school serves a diverse, intercultural community in a vibrant neighborhood.[2][4][6] It has operated as an integrated secondary school since at least 2006 under the Pestalozzi-Fröbel-Haus framework and introduced a partnered gymnasiale Oberstufe in 2020/21 with the neighboring Bergmannkiez-Gemeinschaftsschule.[6][7] No specific founding date or detailed facilities are mentioned, though it hosts events like a Weihnachtsmarkt on school grounds and offers ganztags (all-day) learning.[1][2]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Serving grades 7-10 in integrated settings with a partnered upper secondary phase (gymnasiale Oberstufe: 11th-13th grade, three-year format with Einführungsphase and Qualifikationsphase leading to Abitur), the curriculum combines year-specific courses in standard subjects (Deutsch, Mathematik, Englisch, Naturwissenschaften, Gesellschaftswissenschaften, Bildende Kunst, Sport, Wahlpflichtfach like Französisch/Spanisch/WAT) with jahrgangsübergreifende (cross-grade) Arenen.[1][5][7] Languages include Englisch (1st foreign language from grade 7), Spanisch (from grades 7/11), Französisch (from grade 7).[1][5][7] Graduation pathways: berufsorientierender Abschluss (BOA), Berufsbildungsreife (BBR), erweiterte BBR (eBBR), mittlerer Schulabschluss (MSA), Abitur.[1] Special features: Willkommensklassen (welcome classes), berufsorientierung (career orientation), small groups with two teachers, binnendifferenzierung (differentiated instruction within classes), and ganztag (all-day) programs.[1][2][5]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Central to experiences are **Arenen** (cross-grade 7-10 classes chosen by interests/stengths, e.g., Arena Animal Studies, Atelier, Basketball, Buchkunst, Content Creators, Courage, Gastronomie, Lifehack, Medien 1, Musik, Naturforscher, re-Design, Stadion, Storyboard, Theater & Film), involving dual learning with external experts like artists, scientists, and professionals in themes like society/media, arts, sports, theater, music, nature/technology, economics.[1][3] Extracurriculars include sports (Basketball, Volleyball, Fußball, Leichtathletik, Turnen/Gymnastik/Tanz, Badminton, Unihockey), arts (Theater, Film, Musik, Poetry-Slam workshops), international exchanges (e.g., Erasmus+ with Danish school), Weihnachtsmarkt, Tag der offenen Tür, and social elements like Schülervertretung, Pat*innen, Förderverein.[1][2][3][5] Ganztag offers extended learning; upper secondary includes joint courses across partner schools.[5][7]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific awards, rankings, or recent exam outcomes are detailed in the content; historical enrollment shows decline from 395 students (2011/12) to 284 (2014/15) with 37-42 teachers.[4] The Arena model is described as a proven success for dual learning, with student testimonials highlighting motivation and openness.[1][2] A 2004 Mete-Eksi-Preis mention appears in press archives, but details are unavailable.[10]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a tuition-free public school (Öffentlich), enrollment follows Berlin public system processes; specific requirements are not detailed, but Tag der offenen Tür (e.g., 24.01.2026, 11:00-14:00) and Hospitationen (visits) are offered for prospective families.[1][2] Upper secondary (gymnasiale Oberstufe) has separate Anmeldebogen for internal/external students.[7] Contact sekretariat@ferdinand-freiligrath-schule.de or +49 30 50585611 for inquiries; office hours 7:00-14:00 (Fri to 13:00).[2]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Flatow-Oberschule focuses on optimally fostering athletic talents while ensuring strong academic outcomes, such as the Mittlere Schulabschluss (after grade 10), Fachabitur, or Abitur. As one of Berlin's three Eliteschulen des Sports, it emphasizes individual support for elite athletes (Kadersportler*innen), close collaboration with the Olympiastützpunkt Berlin, Landessportbund Berlin, sports associations, and clubs, and combines top-tier sports training with high-quality education.[1][2][3][6][10]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Originating from the Kinder- und Jugendsportschule (KJS) Paul Gesche, the school was renamed Flatow-Oberschule in 1992 after Olympic gold medalists Alfred and Gustav Felix Flatow, Jewish athletes persecuted by the Nazis. Located in Berlin-Köpenick (Treptow-Köpenick district) at Birkenstraße 11, 12559 Berlin, it serves about 260 students and includes an Internat (boarding facilities), Essenversorgung (meal services), and partnerships with sports facilities nearby, such as training sites for its focus sports.[1][2][3][5][6] It is part of the Schul- und Leistungssportzentrum Berlin (SLZB) and Olympiastützpunkt Berlin.[1][5]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As an Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS) with gymnasialer Oberstufe (gymnasium upper level), it offers grades starting from 7 (with enrollment for 2026/2027 noted), covering all Berlin school qualifications including Mittlere Schulabschluss, Fachabitur, and Abitur. Core subjects include Deutsch, GeWi (Geography/History/Social Studies), Kunst, Mathematik, Musik, NaWi (Natural Sciences), Sport, and Sprachen (languages). Sport is a mandatory Leistungskurs (advanced course) in Sekundarstufe II for all students.[2][3][5][6][10]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
The school specializes in elite sports like Segeln (sailing), Kanu (canoeing), Fußball (soccer, male and female), Rudern (rowing), and BMX, with dedicated pages for Aufnahmekriterien (admission criteria), training sites, successes, and stories. Extracurriculars include project weeks (e.g., "Demokratie mitgestalten und hautnah erleben" and "Berlin – eine Stadt zwischen zwei Diktaturen"), Infotag (open house), Seglerehrung (sailing awards), and Berufsorientierung (career orientation). Facilities support boarding, meals, and Schülerfreizeitbereich (student leisure area).[2][4][5][6][10]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Recognized as an Eliteschule des Sports by the Deutscher Olympischer Sportbund (DOSB), Eliteschule des Fußballs, and awarded "Eliteschule des Jahres" in 2009 by DOSB. Recent sports successes include all four teams qualifying for Bundesfinale (national finals) in 2025/26, with U16 boys winning the championship 4:0. It partners with Olympiastützpunkt and Landessportbund for elite training.[2][4][5][7][10]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
Enrollment is open for new 7th classes (Anmeldezeitraum for 2026/2027 announced), targeting talented athletes via Sichtungstermine (talent scouting dates) and sport-specific Aufnahmekriterien. As a public tuition-free school (Öffentlich), it accepts students from all German states, prioritizing those in focus sports like BMX, soccer, canoeing, rowing, and sailing, with boarding options.[2][3][6][8]

# Freie Schule Berlin-Mahlsdorf School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

Freie Schule Berlin-Mahlsdorf is a **state-approved alternative school (Ersatzschule) based on anthroposophical principles**[3]. The school's pedagogical approach emphasizes holistic child development within an anthroposophical educational framework, though specific mission statements and core values are not detailed in the available website content.

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Location and Structure:**
Freie Schule Berlin-Mahlsdorf is located in the Marzahn-Hellersdorf district at Elsenstraße 13A, 12623 Berlin[3][8]. The school operates as an integrated school (ISS) serving grades 1-11.

**Community:**
The school maintains an active parent community with structured parent involvement opportunities. The admissions process includes mandatory attendance at information evenings, suggesting emphasis on family alignment with the school's philosophy[user content].

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

The school offers education from **grades 1 through 11** (Unterstufe, Mittelstufe, and Oberstufe)[3]. The curriculum is structured according to anthroposophical educational principles, though specific subject details and language offerings are not elaborated in the available content.

**Graduation Pathways:**
The school provides pathways to recognized German school certificates, though specific details about Abitur, MSA (Mittlerer Schulabschluss), or BBR (Berufsbildungsreife) offerings are not specified in the provided materials.

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

Limited information is available regarding extracurricular activities, sports programs, arts offerings, or student exchanges. The school maintains a structured calendar with published holiday schedules and events[3].

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

No specific awards, certifications, or academic rankings are mentioned in the available website content.

## 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES

**Admissions Process:**

For **first-grade entry**, the process includes:
- Application submission by early-to-mid September (year before enrollment)
- Mandatory attendance at an information evening
- Family interviews in November to discuss family circumstances and school expectations
- Play afternoon in January where children interact without parents
- Final class composition and acceptance notification in February[user content]

The school receives more applications than available spaces for first grade and must decline some families[user content].

For **grades 2-11 (lateral entry)**, admission is generally possible at any time. The process requires submission of an application form, questionnaire, and copies of the last two school reports. Following a getting-to-know conversation and positive observation (Hospitation), a school contract can be concluded with a trial period allowing termination by either party[user content].

**Tuition and Fees:**

As a state-approved alternative school (Ersatzschule), Freie Schule Berlin-Mahlsdorf receives **93% of personnel costs from the Berlin state government** according to § 101 of the Berlin School Law. This subsidy includes contributions for material costs and school facility acquisition and operation[user content].

Due to the 7% funding gap, the school charges tuition to parents. **Specific tuition amounts are not listed on the main website pages**. The school maintains a detailed fee schedule (Schulgeldregelung) available as a PDF document[user content].
