description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Hedwig-Dohm-Oberschule aims to optimally promote and challenge its students in a socially demanding environment, enabling them to achieve school qualifications or enter vocational training.[1][3] It emphasizes integration starting before enrollment, with visits to 6th-grade students, foster plan conferences, and support for special needs in regular classes or small groups like Club 115, in cooperation with youth offices, schools, and therapy centers.[1] The school promotes equality through the **Goldene Hedwig** award and quotes Hedwig Dohm: “Die Freude aus der Schule verbannen heißt den Kindern ihr Lebens­element rauben” (Banning joy from school means robbing children of their life element).[1]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Located in Berlin-Mitte (Moabit/Tiergarten area) at Stephanstr. 27, 10559 Berlin, the school serves a diverse community and distinguishes itself from another Hedwig-Dohm-Schule in Stuttgart.[1][3] The building is described as "märchenhaft verwunschen" (fairytale-like enchanted).[1] Facilities include a gym (Turnhalle) in Stephanstraße and an aula for events; it offers midday meals.[1][3] Led by Schulleiterin Anke Uhlmann, it participates in city-wide projects on differentiation and inclusion.[2][3]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As an **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** for grades 7-10, it offers all standard ISS qualifications in grades 9 and 10: **Berufsorientierender Abschluss (BOA)**, **Berufsbildungsreife (BBR)**, **erweiterte Berufsbildungsreife (eBBR)**, and **Mittlerer Schulabschluss (MSA)**, with seamless transition to gymnasiale Oberstufe at Theodor-Heuss-Gemeinschaftsschule via MSA GO.[1][3] Languages include English (1st foreign language), French (2nd), with external differentiation in English and Math for grade 7, and special language support.[3] Programs feature **gebundener Ganztagbetrieb (GGB)**, duales Lernen (internships, job explorations, application training), produktives Lernen, verstärkte Berufswahlvorbereitung, and Zweiter Bildungsweg.[3]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars include Arbeitsgemeinschaften (AGs) such as Artistik, Arabisch, Ballsport/Badminton, Boxen, Computer, Foto/Film, Inlineskate-Hockey, Kreatives Gestalten, Rudern, Streetdance, Tennis, Theater, Volleyball.[3] Events feature Wanderung (hiking day), American-Football taster session, theater projects like "Die Welle," Sommerfest, and an image film by graduating classes.[1] The school hosts a Tag der offenen Tür (open day) and Bildungsmesse for primary-to-secondary transitions, with Instagram stories/reels.[1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
The school awards the **Goldene Hedwig** for equality and is noted as popular in Berlin-Mitte, especially for theater and arts profiles among 7th graders.[1][7] It participated in a 2018 project on Niveaustufenband for competency levels, differentiation, individualization, and inclusion, with ongoing support.[2] Graduates receive congratulations for achievements, with smooth transitions to upper secondary.[1]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a tuition-free public ISS, enrollment prioritizes average grade from primary school Förderprognose and other criteria if oversubscribed.[3] Anmeldezeitraum for 26/27 school year is March 5-12, 2026, requiring a secretariat appointment; new 7th graders have an orientation event.[1][6] Open house on November 29, 2025, and school tours for primary transitions.[1]

description:
# Heinrich-Böll-Oberschule Berlin: Comprehensive School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

The **Heinrich-Böll-Oberschule is a reform school** that emphasizes self-directed, individualized learning within a structured pedagogical framework[1][3]. The school's core educational philosophy centers on **reformpädagogische Strukturen** (reform pedagogical structures), where students learn independently through a "Lernbausteinsystem" (learning module system)[1]. 

The school operates as a **partially bound all-day school** (teilgebundener Ganztag) with mandatory afternoon courses three days per week[2]. The instructional structure deliberately moves away from traditional 45-minute lessons to **90-minute learning blocks**, allowing students to engage more deeply and intensively with content[2]. The school balances concentrated work phases with periods of relaxation and recovery, recognizing that sustainable learning requires rhythmic variation[2].

Key pedagogical principles include:
- **Gruppenzeit** (group time): A 30-minute opening period where students prepare for the day, discuss current issues, and participate in student-led class councils[2]
- **Lernbüro** (learning office): Self-directed work with modular learning materials at individual pace[2]
- **Projektunterricht** (project-based learning): Cross-disciplinary, team-based learning connected to real-world contexts[2]

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

The school was **founded in 1973 as Carl-Diem-Oberschule**, a comprehensive school named after sports administrator Carl Diem, and was **renamed Heinrich-Böll-Oberschule in 1993** after the German writer Heinrich Böll[1]. The school became a **reform school in the 2012/13 school year**, introducing learning groups spanning grades 7-9[1].

**Location and Contact:**
- **Address:** Am Forstacker 9-9a, 13587 Berlin-Spandau[3][5]
- **District:** Spandau, Ortsteil Hakenfelde[1][4]
- **Phone:** 030/3350070[5]
- **Email:** hbo@boell-schule.de[5]
- **Website:** https://boell-schule.de[6]

**Leadership:**
- **Headmistress:** Frau Faak[2][5]
- **Deputy Headmistress:** Frau Thalemann[5]
- **Administrative Director:** Frau Zywny-John[5]

**Enrollment:**
The school currently serves **1,009 students** (as of September 27, 2024)[2].

**Facilities:**
The school provides a **cafeteria (Mensa)** with lunch offerings[3]. The school library serves as a venue for student activities such as the upper-level book club[2]. The school maintains partnerships with external cultural institutions: the **Kunstbastion Zitadelle Spandau** for art profile cooperation and the **Musikschule Spandau** for music profile cooperation[2].

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**School Type and Graduation Pathways:**
The Heinrich-Böll-Oberschule is an **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS) with a gymnasium upper level** (gymnasiale Oberstufe), allowing students to earn all school-leaving certificates[3]. The **Abitur can be completed in either two or three years**[3].

**Grade Levels:**
The school serves students from grade 7 through the Abitur (grade 12/13).

**Core Subjects Offered:**
Deutsch, Englisch, Kunst, Geschichte, Mathematik, Physik, Biologie, Informatik, Musik[2]

**Language Programs:**
- **Bilingual Program:** German/English with enhanced English instruction[1][2]
- **Additional Languages:** Französisch (French), Latein (Latin), Spanisch (Spanish)[2][7]

**Profile Programs (4-year specialization for middle school students):**

| Profile | Offerings | Notes |
|---------|-----------|-------|
| **Sport** | Allround-Sport, Fußball (boys), Leichtathletik, Tanzen (girls), Volleyball | Requires entrance screening/test; 3 double-periods weekly in morning slots[2][7][8] |
| **Art** | Enhanced instruction in drawing, painting, sculpture | Requires entrance screening/test; cooperation with Kunstbastion Zitadelle Spandau[2][7][9] |
| **Music** | Diverse instruction from opera to classical instruments and bands | Cooperation with Musikschule Spandau[2][7][10] |
| **Languages** | Latein, Französisch, Spanisch | [7] |

**Elective Courses (Wahlpflichtangebote):**
Informatik, Wirtschaft-Arbeit-Technik (WAT), Schülerfirma (student business), Sozialkunde, Pausenhelfer, Verantwortungsprojekt, Naturwissenschaften, Darstellendes Spiel (theater), Schülerzeitung[2]

**Support and Enrichment Programs:**
- **Language Support:** Förderunterricht Deutsch, Englisch (German and English remedial instruction)[2]
- **Mathematics Support:** Förderunterricht Mathe (mathematics remedial instruction)[2]
- **Additional Learning Time:** Extra study periods with teacher support for homework and test preparation[2]
- **Temporary Learning Groups:** For students requiring additional support[2]

**Career Orientation:**
The school provides **vertiefte Berufsorientierung** (in-depth career orientation) including workplace internships (9th grade), career field exploration (8th grade), attendance at career fairs, presentations by apprentices, and "Girl's Day" participation[3].

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**Sports Programs:**
The school is **known for its sport profile**, offering specialized training in multiple disciplines[3]. The sports program emphasizes age-appropriate motor development in grades 7-8, with individual specialization in sprint/running, throwing, and jumping in grades 9-10[8]. Sport profile students receive three double-periods of sport-specific training weekly, scheduled in early morning slots to allow recovery before afternoon club training[8].

**Arts and Cultural Activities:**
- **Theater:** Darstellendes Spiel (theater elective) and theater working groups[2]
- **Music:** Choir and instrumental instruction[2]
- **Visual Arts:** Künstlerisches Gestalten (artistic design) working groups[2]
- **Poetry:** Poetry Slam activities[2]

**Extracurricular Activities (Arbeitsgemeinschaften):**
Kochen und Ernährung (cooking and nutrition), Umwelttester (environmental testing), Theater, Outdoor-Sport, Fitness, Künstlerisches Gestalten, Gamedesign, Chor (choir), Instrumente spielen (instrument playing), Poetry Slam, Spanisch[2]

**Social Engagement:**
- **"Woche der Herausforderungen"** (Week of Challenges): Annual event[3]
- **Service Learning:** Project-based learning through social engagement[3]
- **Conflict Resolution:** Workshops on civil courage and bullying prevention[3]
- **Student Government:** Gesamtschülervertretung (student council) represents student interests to school leadership and authorities[1]
- **Socio-Pedagogical Support:** Counseling for students and parents on social issues; organization of leisure activities[1]

**Conflict Resolution and Support Projects:**
The school operates specialized projects including **Mediatorausbildung** (mediator training), **Zuspätkommer-Projekt** (late-comer project), and **Reflektorium**, all aimed at conflict management within the school[1].

**Recent Student Activities (from school blog):**
- French courses participating in Cinéfête (film festival)[2]
- Theater visits (Grips Theater)[2]
- Interdisciplinary excursions exploring constitutional rights and artistic perspectives[2]

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

The school holds **certifications** (specific certifications referenced but details not fully specified in available content)[6]. The school underwent a **school inspection** with a current report available to prospective families[6].

The school demonstrates **sustained enrollment growth**, with student numbers increasing from 818 in 2012/13 to 1,009 by 2024, indicating growing community confidence[4].

## 6. ADMISSIONS

**Enrollment Process for Grade 7:**
- **Registration Period:** Online calendar for in-person appointments opens January 28, 2026[2]
- **Required Documents:**
  - Completed application form (Aufnahmeantrag HBO) - available in digital format[2]
  - Förderprognose (learning recommendation from previous school)[2]
  - Copy of most recent school report card[2]
  - Copy of birth certificate[2]
  - Two passport-sized photographs[2]
- **Important:** Parents must attend the appointment **with their child**[2]

**Campus Tours:**
Guided tours for interested 6th-grade students and parents are offered; the next scheduled tour is January 27, 2026 at 10:30 AM with Frau Schulterobben (contact: r.schulterobben@boell-schule.de)[2]

**Upper School (Oberstufe) Admissions:**
- **Registration Opens:** February 9, 2026[2]
- **Information Session:** February 25, 2026 at 18:30[2]
- **Enrollment Tracks:** Einführungsphase (grade 11) and Qualifikationsphase (grades 12-13) for school year 2026/27[2]

**Public School Status:**
As a public school (öffentliche Schule), the Heinrich-Böll-Oberschule is **tuition-free** with no enrollment fees or material charges[2].

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Heinrich-Mann-Schule's **Leitbild** (mission statement) emphasizes creating a peaceful, fair school environment free from exclusion and violence, promoting teamwork in interdisciplinary contexts, personal and mutual responsibility, future-oriented skills for independence, strengthened self-esteem, active school involvement, life planning post-graduation, appreciation of differences, openness to new ideas, and inclusive education.[1][2] This philosophy guides daily actions, decisions, and aspirations, fostering self-reliance and community.[1]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Originally a Gesamtschule, the school transitioned to an **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** with teilgebundem Unterricht starting in the 2010/11 school year as part of Berlin's school reform.[3] Located in Berlin-Neukölln (Buckow neighborhood), it serves approximately **450 students** across 4-5 classes per yeargang, led by Schulleiter Rudolf Kemmer (with recent mention of Jan Zimmermann in projects).[1][6][7] Facilities include a school garden, pond (with frogs and libellen), bee hotel, computer rooms, whiteboards, numerous computer workstations, and a mensa for communal lunches provided by Firma Schultze; recent projects involve redesigning the entrance and AUB (pausenraum) for a friendlier space with curved seating and monitors.[1][4][6][7]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As an **ISS**, the school offers education from **7th to 13th grade** (gymnasiale Oberstufe), enabling pathways to **Abitur** (in 13 years), MSA, or BBR, with leistungs- and abschluss-oriented instruction including inner differentiation in Deutsch, Englisch, and Mathematik.[1][4][5] Languages: **Englisch** (1st foreign language), **Französisch** (2nd as Wahlfach).[4][6] Special programs include teilgebundener Ganztagsbetrieb (TGB), duales Lernen (Betriebserkundungen, Praktika, Bewerbungstraining, Schülerfirmen), Produktives Lernen, Lerngruppen for new arrivals without Deutschkenntnisse, Projekttag (Wednesdays with topics like Cybermobbing and Klimaschutz), itslearning platform, AntonApp, and Wahlpflichtkurse (e.g., Luft- und Raumfahrt in some ISS).[1][4][6] All yeargangs complete Praktika via "Verantwortungsprojekt"; small 7th classes (20 students) grow to 26 by 10th.[6]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars include AGs (after-school): RAP, Parcour, Theater, Jazzdance (with Jugendamt and Show up); Arbeitsgemeinschaften like Schach, Planspiel Börse.[4][6] Trips and projects: Grüne Woche (career exploration, e.g., Gerüstbau, Prothesentechnik, Bagger operation), Demokratie-Mobil (mobile democracy education), Weihnachtspäckchenkonvoi (aid for Eastern Europe children), Young Arts, Projektwoche (Klimaschutz, e.g., Ernährung in 7th), bee/imker projects.[1][6] Partners support Umwelt (Grün macht Schule, Freilandlabor Britz), Berufsorientierung (Ayekoo, Berliner Netzwerk für Ausbildung), social learning (Heroes e.V., Scoring Girls*, Berliner Jungs); communal Mittagessen with classes.[1][4] Sports/social via partners; no specific exchanges noted here.[1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Certified as **Umweltschule and Klimaschule since 2011**, **Verbraucherschule in Bronze** (2019/20, 2021/22).[1] Strong enrollment demand (all 1st/2nd/3rd wish students admitted recently); focus on inclusion and Prüfungsergebnisse available via official portal.[4][6][10]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
**Public tuition-free** school; enrollment for 7th Klasse (e.g., 2026/27) via appointment booking with Sekretariat during Anmeldezeitraum (05.03.-12.03.2026), open to all wishes with small classes for transition support; **Tag der offenen Tür** on 24.01.2026 (10-14 Uhr).[1][2][6] No special requirements noted; currently hiring qualified teachers.[1]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Heinz-Brandt-Schule emphasizes **inclusive, collaborative learning** where knowledge grows and diversity is valued, fostering a welcoming environment for all students regardless of differences.[1][3] Core values include **diversity**, **engagement**, **individual support**, and **modern education**, with heterogeneous learning groups of 17-18 students promoting mutual support across varying ability levels, use of a **Logbuch** for goal-setting and progress tracking as a communication tool among students, parents, and teachers, and a focus on self-directed learning in prepared environments like the Lernbüro for subjects such as German, Math, and English.[1][3] The philosophy centers on **gebundene Ganztagsschule** (all-day schooling) from 8:20 to 16:00, balancing intensive phases with relaxation through midday activities, electives, and clubs, while integrating digital tools like tablets/iPads, itslearning platform, and media education.[1]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Originally a **Polytechnische Oberschule (POS) "Grete Walter"** in the GDR era, the school transitioned post-reunification, remaining a Hauptschule until Berlin's school reform; it was renamed **Heinz-Brandt-Schule in 2010**, expanding from a two-track to four-track **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** with internal reforms.[3] Located in **Berlin-Weißensee, Pankow district** (Langhansstr. 120, 13086 Berlin), it serves a challenging neighborhood with over 40% of students qualifying for financial aid and is part of the reform-oriented **Schulverbund "Blick über den Zaun"** network.[1][3][5] Facilities include **campus-wide fast WLAN**, digital devices for all students (tablets/iPads), specialized **Lernbüro rooms** for self-directed learning, and itslearning as the central learning management system for tasks, organization, and communication.[1][3]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As an **ISS starting from Grade 7**, the school offers pathways including **Abitur** (up to Grade 13 for university qualification), **MSA**, and vocational options like **BBR** or dual learning with early practical experiences from Grade 7.[1][3][5] Key features: heterogeneous groups, **Wahlpflichtkurse (WPU)** including second foreign language **Spanish**, digital integration via itslearning and media education, **Lernbüro** for differentiated instruction in Deutsch, Mathematik, and English, and bi-weekly **Global Goals project days** for grades 7-8 focusing on self-directed social, ecological, artistic-cultural, and interdisciplinary projects.[1][2][3] The all-day structure includes flexible midday bands for learning or relaxation.[1]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in **artistic and sports Arbeitsgemeinschaften (AGs)** (e.g., basketball, school band), cultural projects as a **Kulturagentenschule**, **Gedenkstättenfahrt** to Krakau/Auschwitz, "Herausforderung - Lernen am anderen Ort," service learning, and Global Goals initiatives for social engagement.[1][2] Extracurriculars emphasize **talent development** across abilities, class councils, school design projects, new school shirts initiative, and virtual open days like "follow me around"; digital tools enhance daily experiences, with options for creative midday activities.[1] As a member of international networks like Centropa, teachers incorporate history and social projects.[7]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
The school has received the **Deutscher Schulpreis** for its innovative concept and reforms, multiple awards for modernizing education, and recognition for engagement in networks; it was **Berlin's most sought-after ISS for 2023/24** with high enrollment demand and national prominence.[1][3] Achievements include overregional fame for reform-pedagogy, project successes like Global Goals, and consistent demand as one of Berlin's top secondary schools.[3]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **tuition-free public school (Öffentlich)**, enrollment is open with current calls for **Grade 7 intake for 2026/27** via "Anmeldung Klasse 7 2026/27," virtual open days, and "follow me around" tours; it attracts high demand, being Berlin's top ISS recently.[1][3] No tuition or fees mentioned; staff vacancies (e.g., Math, Deutsch/Sport teachers for 2026/27) indicate growth.[1]

description:
# Hemingway-Schule School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

The **Hemingway-Schule is committed to enabling every student to achieve the best possible school leaving qualification and to prepare them for future professional life.**[1] As an **Integrated Secondary School (ISS) in Berlin-Mitte**, the school operates as a **full-day school with a focus on career orientation and life path planning.**[1]

The school's core values emphasize **friendly and violence-free interaction**, **cultural diversity**, and **order and cleanliness.**[1] The educational approach integrates **individual promotion, value development, and democratic education** into its school mission, alongside preparation for vocational training.[3] The school views itself as both a **learning and living space**, implementing a **partially bound full-day operation** that creates free spaces during the school day through schedule rhythmization and cooperation with external partners.[6]

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Location and Access:** The Hemingway-Schule is located at **Gartenstraße 10-17, 10115 Berlin (Mitte district)** with **excellent central location and public transport connections.**[1][2] The school is operated by **gss Schulpartner GmbH** and has been active since August 2010.[2]

**Student Demographics:** The school serves approximately **441 students across 17 classes with 56 teachers.**[4] Over **80% of students have a migration background, with representation from 26 countries.**[1][3] More than **340 students qualify for educational support or "Education and Participation" benefits.**[3]

**Facilities and Equipment:** The school features **modern equipment including smartboards in all rooms, computer labs, a sewing studio, wood and metal workshops, a school kitchen, library, and cafeteria.**[7] The school maintains **high standards of order and cleanliness** and includes the **Jugendclub NewWay**, a youth club open to students aged 11-18.[1]

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**Grade Levels:** The school teaches **grades 7-10**, with grades typically organized in four parallel classes (five classes for grade 7 in 2021/2022).[3]

**Languages:** Students learn **English as the first foreign language and French as the second foreign language.**[7]

**Core Academic Structure:** 
- **Small group instruction** (13-15 students) in German, Mathematics, English, Biology, Chemistry, and Information Technology Basics (ITG).[4]
- **Performance-differentiated instruction** in German, Mathematics, and English from grade 7 onwards.[4]
- **ITG (Information Technology Basics)** is a standalone subject in grade 8.[4]

**Special Programs and Pathways:**

- **Dual Learning (Duales Lernen):** Includes internships, company visits, application training, trial internships, job coaching, and visits to trade associations.[7]
- **Practical Learning (Praxislernen):** One day per week of practice-oriented learning.[1]
- **Temporary Learning Groups (TLG):** Support for students developing learning and action strategies to manage school life, including a specialized small class (J) for students with intellectual development support needs.[3]
- **MyWay Project:** Support for students who no longer feel comfortable at their school.[1]
- **Guaranteed Abitur Pathway:** The school offers a **guaranteed school place for the Abitur at the OSZ Banken, Immobilien und Versicherung (vocational college for banking, real estate, and insurance).**[1]

**Graduation Pathways:** Students can achieve **MSA (Mittlerer Schulabschluss) after grade 10 and BBR (Berufsbildungsreife) after grade 9.**[7]

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**Extracurricular Activities:** The school offers **38 working groups (AGs) in sports, creative, and educational areas**, including theater, art, choir, basketball, volleyball, football, swimming, running, school newspaper, informatik, and conflict mediator training.[6]

**Sports Programs:** The school emphasizes sports with offerings for **sports enthusiasts up to 8 hours per week and more.**[1] Documented activities include girls' football tournaments and multiple sports clubs.[7]

**Support Services:**
- **School social work** for students needing support or facing difficulties[1]
- **Counseling team** available for students, parents, and teachers[1]
- **Trusted teachers** providing support for personal or school-related problems[1]
- **Training room supervision** and **morning band supervision**[6]
- **Active break supervision** with movement-focused activities[6]

**Community Engagement:** The school participates in social projects such as "Christmas in a Shoebox" and "Generations Connect" (visits to nursing homes).[1] The school collaborates with external cultural institutions, including the **KW Institute for Contemporary Art**, on artistic and community projects.[8]

**Dining:** The school provides **daily freshly prepared meals in the school kitchen.**[1]

**Youth Club:** The **Jugendclub NewWay** offers diverse leisure activities for students aged 11-18.[1]

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

The search results do not contain specific information about awards, rankings, or certifications. However, the school demonstrates commitment to **inclusive education development through new projects** and emphasizes **practical learning forms and individual promotion as cross-cutting tasks.**[6] Students report positive experiences with the school's teaching quality, facilities, and teacher relationships.[1]

## 6. ADMISSIONS

As a **public school (öffentlich), the Hemingway-Schule is tuition-free.** The school is part of the Berlin public school system and follows standard Berlin enrollment procedures for secondary schools. Interested families are invited to attend the **Open House Day (Tag der offenen Tür)** and explore the **virtual school tour** available on the school website.[1] The school also maintains an "always open virtual door" for prospective families to learn more about the institution.[1]

description:
# Herbert-Hoover-Schule School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

The Herbert-Hoover-Schule operates as a **cultural school** with a strong emphasis on arts and theater integration into the curriculum[2][4]. The school's educational approach centers on **"Duales Lernen" (Dual Learning)**, which combines academic instruction with practical work experience starting in grade 7[3][5]. The school is organized as a **bound all-day school (Gebundene Ganztagsschule)** using a rhythm-based principle that balances instruction with periods for rest and reflection[3].

The school's philosophy emphasizes collaborative decision-making, with the school program developed through workshops involving teachers, students, parents, and various working groups[6].

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

The Herbert-Hoover-Schule was **founded in 1956** and named after Herbert C. Hoover, with the naming ceremony attended by Hoover's son[3]. The school received the **German National Prize (Deutscher Nationalpreis) in 2006** for its community-driven decision to establish German as the primary language of communication throughout the school[3].

**Location:** Pankstraße 18-19, 13357 Berlin, in the Gesundbrunnen district (Ortsteil) of Berlin-Mitte[1][4][5]

**Facilities:** Since December 2020, the school is "chalk-free" with all classrooms equipped with modern smartboards for digital instruction[2]. The school includes two computer rooms and interactive whiteboards[5].

**Community:** In the 2022/23 school year, 469 students attended the school, with 89.1% being multilingual[3]. The school ranks among the ten most sought-after secondary schools in Berlin[3]. The school operates a **school network** in cooperation with three primary schools: Erika-Mann-Grundschule, Wedding Schule, and Humboldthain Grundschule[7].

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**School Type:** Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS) without a gymnasium upper level[4]

**Grades:** Serves grades 7-10 (secondary level)

**Languages:**
- First foreign language: **English** (mandatory for all students)[2][5]
- Second foreign language options: **Spanish or French** (elective in grades 7+)[2][5]

**Academic Profiles and Specializations:**
- **Cultural profile** with emphasis on arts and theater[2][4]
- **Career-oriented profile** (berufsorientiertes Profil)[4][5]
- **Language-focused profile** (sprachbetontes Profil)[4][5]
- **Theater-focused profile** (theaterbetontes Profil)[4][5]
- **Schwerpunktschule Kultur** (Priority school for culture)[5]
- **Schwerpunktschule für Deutsch und Darstellendes Spiel** (Priority school for German and Performing Arts)[4]

**Special Programs:**

*Dual Learning:* Beginning in grade 7, students participate in dual learning combining classroom instruction with practical experience, including:
- Internships and business explorations (Betriebserkundungen)[5]
- Application training (Bewerbungstraining)[5]
- Productive learning (Produktives Lernen)[5]
- Student enterprises (Schülerfirmen)[5]
- Participation in Berlin's in-depth career orientation program (Berliner Programm zur vertieften Berufsorientierung - BVBO)[3][5]

*Cultural Programs:* All 7th-grade students participate in five days of theater in autumn as part of the school profile[8]. The school offers:
- Theater weeks (Theaterwochen)[6]
- Cultural calendar and cultural agents program[6]
- Artistic final presentations during school breaks[6]

*All-Day School:* Structured with rhythm-based organization balancing instruction, recovery, and relaxation[3]

*Holiday School (Ferienschule):* A cooperative project during autumn and winter breaks where grades 8-10 students teach approximately 35 primary school students in mathematics, German, and English, with artistic presentations at week's end[6]

*School Social Work:* Dedicated school social work support services[6]

**Differentiation:** Internal and external differentiation in grade 7 English instruction[5]

**Graduation Pathways:** As an ISS without gymnasium upper level, students complete their secondary education at grade 10 with the option to pursue vocational training or transfer to other institutions for further education.

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**Theater and Arts:** Theater is central to the school experience, with mandatory participation for all 7th graders in autumn theater weeks[8]. The school regularly produces theater premieres, including "Theater 10" productions[6].

**Peer Teaching:** Older students (grades 8-10) teach younger primary school students during holiday school, developing leadership and teaching perspectives[6].

**Community Engagement:** Students organize and manage meal services through student enterprises and participate in school network activities[6].

**Reading Competitions:** The school hosts reading competitions (Vorlesewettbewerb)[6].

**School Trips:** Regular field trips and excursions are part of the program[6].

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

**Recognition:**
- **German National Prize (2006)** for community-driven language policy decision[3]
- Ranks among the **ten most sought-after secondary schools in Berlin**[3]

**Student Demographics:** 89.1% of students are multilingual, reflecting the school's diverse, international community[3]

**Enrollment Trend:** The school has maintained relatively stable enrollment, with 462 students in 2023/24[4]

## 6. ADMISSIONS

**Enrollment Process:** Students apply for admission to grade 7 through the standard Berlin secondary school application process[5][6]. The school website provides an application portal for grade 7 enrollment (Anmeldung 7. Klassen)[6].

**Admission Criteria:** Admissions are based on average grades from the school recommendation (Förderprognose) and other criteria during periods of high demand[5].

**Key Distinction:** Unlike gymnasium schools, there is no probationary year; students advance to the next grade level regardless of grades[7].

**Public School Status:** As a public school (öffentlich), the Herbert-Hoover-Schule is **tuition-free**. There are no enrollment fees or material fees[1][5].

**Meal Services:** The school provides lunch services through a student enterprise (Schülerfirma)[5].

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Hermann-von-Helmholtz-Schule is a **naturwissenschaftlich ausgerichtet** (science-oriented) integrated secondary school with a primary level (Integrierte Sekundarschule mit Grundstufe), emphasizing supported transitions from kindergarten through the end of schooling and beyond, language development (**Sprachbildung**), cultural education, and career orientation (**Berufsorientierung**).[Main Page][About Page][Curriculum Page]  
Core goals include imparting knowledge and competencies to prepare students for professions or studies, with a strong focus on comprehensive language skills across all subjects via a dedicated **Sprachförderkonzept** (language promotion concept), enabling understanding of their life world and broad education.[Curriculum Page][9]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Located in **Gropiusstadt**, Berlin-Neukölln district at **Wutzkyallee 68-78, 12353 Berlin**, this public (**Öffentlich**) tuition-free school serves the local community with programs like **Seniorpartner in School (SiS)**, involving 289 members across 58 Berlin schools for intergenerational engagement.[School Information][Main Page][About Page]  
No specific founding history or detailed facilities are mentioned; community ties are evident through events like open doors for kindergartens and parents, and partnerships such as with **ALBA BERLIN** for primary enrollment celebrations.[Main Page][About Page]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Serves **grades 1-10** (Grundstufe primary, then integrated secondary up to Sekundarstufe I), following Berlin's **Rahmenlehrpläne** with a science focus, extensive language competency in all subjects, and subjects like those in standard ISS curricula (e.g., Deutsch, Mathematik, Englisch, Naturwissenschaften, Wirtschaft-Arbeit-Technik/WAT).[School Information][Curriculum Page][9][1][6]  
Special emphasis on **Sprachbildung** (language education), cultural Bildung, and **Berufsorientierung** (career orientation); pathways align with ISS offerings like **MSA** (Mittlerer Schulabschluss), **BBR** (Berufsbezogener Bildungsabschluss), though not explicitly detailed.[Main Page][Curriculum Page][1]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars include holiday programs like **EföB auf Tour** (e.g., Britzer Garten visits, ATZE Musiktheater), arts (e.g., monthly student artwork such as Year 9's "Op-Art trifft Fluchtpunkt" drawing), career events (**Job- und Praktikumsmesse**, Brückenbauer, Talent-Company), sports/community partnerships (**ALBA BERLIN** team for primary starters), and open house parkours for transitions.[Main Page][About Page]  
Awards like **Helmholtz-Lichter** recognize outstanding student achievements; SiS offers senior mentoring.[Main Page][About Page]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Internal recognition via **Helmholtz-Lichter** awards for exemplary student performances, presented ceremonially (e.g., July 21, 2025).[Main Page][About Page]  
No external rankings, certifications, or graduation outcomes mentioned in the content.

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **public tuition-free school**, enrollment is open; key events include **Tag der offenen Tür** (open house days) for primary (e.g., September 25, 2025, 15:00-18:00) and secondary/middle levels (e.g., January 9, 2026), targeting parents of kindergarten children and future first-graders.[School Information][Main Page][About Page]  
Special primary enrollment event with ALBA BERLIN partnership (September 13, 2025); kindergarten/parent presentations via parkours.[Main Page][About Page]

description:
# Hufeland-Schule School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

The Hufeland-Schule operates as a **full-day integrated secondary school** with a pedagogical approach centered on comprehensive student support and practical learning integration[4]. The school's core mission emphasizes providing **guidance, protection, and participation** to ensure students achieve strong educational outcomes while addressing both academic and individual life challenges[2].

The school implements **inner and outer differentiation**, methodological training, and media-based learning through team-teaching models[1]. A distinctive feature is the **Produktives Lernen (Productive Learning)** program, which allows students to learn from practical experiences and integrate these into their formal education, available after 8th or 9th grade[4].

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Location and Community Integration**

The Hufeland-Schule is located in the Berlin-Buch district of Pankow in northern Berlin, situated within a residential neighborhood[2][4]. The school actively engages with its community through partnerships with local organizations, including the Helios Hospital, kindergartens, and nursing homes where students complete internships[4]. The school participates in neighborhood events such as "Hoffeste" (hope festivals) and engages in urban renewal conferences[4].

**Facilities and Infrastructure**

The school provides comprehensive facilities including[7]:
- Two computer labs
- Auditorium
- Teaching kitchen
- Workshops for woodworking, metalworking, and textile work
- Wheelchair-accessible facilities and accessible restrooms
- Parking for people with disabilities

**Current Development**

As of January 2026, the school website is undergoing renovation to create a modern, mobile-optimized platform[4].

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**Grade Levels and Structure**

The Hufeland-Schule serves grades 7–10 as an Integrated Secondary School (Integrierte Sekundarschule)[8]. The school offers **all school-leaving certificates, including the Abitur (university entrance qualification) in cooperation with partner institutions**[9].

**Academic Profile**

The school emphasizes a **MINT (mathematics, science, technology) and natural sciences profile**[1]. 

**Languages Offered**

- First foreign language: **English** (mandatory)
- Second foreign language: **French** (mandatory)
- Additional languages available as needed: **Russian** and **Spanish** (from school year 2017/18 onwards)[1]

**Differentiation and Support**

- Inner differentiation in mathematics and English in grade 7[7]
- Special support programs in German and mathematics[7]
- Sonderpädagogische Förderung (special education support) for students with identified learning needs[5]
- Training in communication and work techniques through multiple modules[7]
- Homework supervision and support[7]

**Dual Learning and Career Orientation**

The school provides extensive career preparation through[7]:
- Career selection passport (Berufswahlpass)
- Trial internships (Schnupperpraktika)
- Semester-long internships (Betriebspraktika)
- Student enterprises (Schülerfirmen)
- Company visits and explorations
- Application training
- Potential analysis assessments
- Productive Learning program

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**Extracurricular Activities**

The school offers working groups (AGs) in[7]:
- Basketball
- Handball
- Music
- Homepage/web design
- Comic creation

**Community Engagement**

Students engage in social responsibility through practical internships in healthcare, early childhood education, and senior care facilities[4]. The school participates in community events, including the Berlin Corporate Run at the Brandenburg Gate[4].

**Outdoor and Adventure Programs**

The school organizes expeditions combining hiking, cycling, and camping experiences, designed to build teamwork and challenge students to develop new skills[4].

**School Meals**

The school provides lunch service through Sunshine Catering as part of its full-day program[7].

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

**Student Population Growth**

The school has experienced significant enrollment growth, expanding from 312 students in 2011/12 to 697 students in 2023/24[6].

**School Classification**

The school is classified at **Belastungsstufe (stress level) 4**, indicating it serves a student population with higher support needs[6].

**Partnerships**

The school maintains cooperative relationships with multiple institutions including other secondary schools (03B09, 03G10, 03G24, 03G29, 10B02) and primary schools such as the Buggenhagen-Schule and Grundschule am Sandhaus, collaborating on projects including theater productions[4][7].

**Support Services**

School-based youth social work through djo-Bildungswerk Berlin gGmbH provides[2]:
- Support and counseling for all participants in the educational process
- Transition support from primary to secondary school
- Student mentor projects and anti-bullying coaching
- Crisis intervention services
- Organization of extracurricular activities

## 6. ADMISSIONS

**Enrollment Process**

The school welcomes applications from students transitioning to grade 7. Prospective students and parents are invited to the **Tag der offenen Tür (Open House Day)** scheduled for January 29, 2026, with separate programs for primary school classes (9:00–13:00) and parents/students (15:00–18:00)[4].

**Application and Contact**

Interested families can schedule personal conversations with school leadership regarding admission by contacting: **Anmeldung@hufelandschule.berlin**[4]

**Public School Status**

As a public school (öffentlich), the Hufeland-Schule is **tuition-free** with no enrollment fees[7].

description:
# Hugo-Gaudig-Schule School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

The **educational goal** of Hugo-Gaudig-Schule is to convey knowledge, skills, and behaviors that provide a solid foundation for students' further vocational training and personal development in family and society[2][3][4]. The school emphasizes **responsible independence**, educating students toward sound judgment, critical thinking, and creativity[8]. Teaching methods support self-directed and self-active learning in the spirit of Hugo Gaudig, transferring responsibility for learning to students themselves[8].

The school develops students' **social competencies**, teamwork abilities, and communication skills[2][3][4]. It operates as an **open all-day school** where instruction and extracurricular offerings are integral components of the educational program[1][2].

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

Hugo-Gaudig-Schule is located in the **Tempelhof-Schöneberg district** of Berlin at Boelckestraße 58-60, 12101 Berlin[6]. The school was converted from a Realschule to an Integrated Secondary School (ISS) in 2011[2].

**Facilities and Infrastructure:**

The school is **chalk-free** and equipped with modern technology[1]. Each classroom and most specialized rooms feature a personal computer, interactive smartboard, and whiteboard[1][2]. School-wide WLAN internet access is available throughout the building[1][2]. Each student has an individual desk that can be positioned flexibly, enabling varied social learning arrangements and cooperative learning formats[1][2].

Specialized facilities include[5]:
- 3 sports halls
- Teaching kitchen
- Textile workshop
- Woodworking workshop
- Landscape/garden area
- Music room
- Art workshop
- Physics lecture hall
- Computer labs
- Student lounge ("Wohnzimmer")

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**Grade Levels and Structure:**

Hugo-Gaudig-Schule is a **4-track Integrated Secondary School (ISS)** offering instruction in grades 7–10[1][2][6]. The school accepts students with various educational prognoses, including those designated for gymnasium/secondary school, secondary school, and students with special educational needs[1].

**Graduation Pathways:**

Students can achieve all possible school-leaving certificates available in Berlin[1]:
- **Berufsbildungsreife (BBR)** – basic vocational qualification
- **Erweiterte Berufsbildungsreife (eBBR)** – extended basic vocational qualification
- **Berufsorientierender Abschluss (BOA)** – career-oriented certificate
- **Mittlerer Schulabschluss (MSA)** – intermediate school-leaving certificate
- **MSA with transition to upper secondary level (MSA GO)** – pathway to Abitur[1][2]

**Vocational Preparation:**

**Dual Learning** is fundamental to the ISS and a core component of the school's program[3][4]. It is implemented through mandatory modules and largely through project-based learning[3][4].

**Specialized Instruction:**

The school offers **elective science courses** (grades 7–10) taught through inquiry-based, action-oriented, and project-based methods[2]. Students learn microscopy techniques, specimen preparation, model creation, and engage in bioethical discussions integrated with ethics instruction[2].

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

The school hosts **open house days** and **discovery days** for prospective students[7][9]. 

**Sports and Physical Education:**

Students receive **coeducational physical education** instruction, with the goal of building endurance, developing motor and coordinative body awareness, and promoting health consciousness[4]. Three school staff members supervise swimming instruction at a facility on Götzstraße, funded by the Senate administration[4].

**Support Services:**

The school offers **bullying counseling services** and **individual student coaching**[5]. **Deescalation training** is provided as part of the school's approach to creating a safe learning environment[5].

**Career Orientation:**

The school emphasizes **active breaks** and maintains partnerships for practical experience and workshop days related to career preparation[9].

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

Hugo-Gaudig-Schule received the **"Quality Seal Berlin for Excellent Career Orientation" (2025–2029)** on August 23, 2025[7]. This award recognizes the school's engaged, creative, and practical approach to career guidance, demonstrating that vocational orientation is an integral part of quality education rather than an add-on[7].

The school also holds the designation of **"Watchful School"** (Siegel "wachsame Schule"), reflecting its commitment to student safety and well-being[5].

## 6. ADMISSIONS

As a **public school**, Hugo-Gaudig-Schule is **tuition-free**. The school is open to all students transitioning to secondary level I[1]. Students are accepted regardless of their educational prognosis designation (gymnasium/secondary school track, secondary school track, or special educational needs)[1].

**School Registration:**

Online school registration for 2026 is available through the school's website[9].

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The school emphasizes a welcoming environment where teaching, learning, and living occur joyfully, positioning itself as a public institution with reform pedagogical approaches and ongoing development.[1][8] It describes itself using adjectives like **innovativ**, **beweglich**, **kooperativ**, **aufgeschlossen**, and **projektorientiert**, reflecting a flexible, cooperative, and project-based ethos.[2] As a Kulturagenten-Schule, it fosters diverse learning offers in a full-day school setting to enable interest-oriented development through participatory artistic projects connected to students' lives.[2]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Opened in August 2019 as the first school in Berlin's Schulbauoffensive, built in innovative Holzmodulbauweise (wood modular construction) with a capacity for 550 students; it received the Holzbaupreis 2019 for ecology and architecture.[1][3][4] Located in Berlin-Marzahn-Hellersdorf (Mahlsdorf district) at An der Schule 47 (or 41-59), 12623 Berlin, serving a growing community addressing school place shortages.[1][6] Facilities include a 3-5 story main building (9,090 m² gross floor area), integrated sports hall, central foyer with open wooden staircase, 32 flexible classrooms, art and music rooms with outdoor access, divisible multi-purpose room/speisesaal with kitchen, administration areas, makerspace, school garden, and bee hives; designed inclusively per DIN 18024 with accessibility features.[1][3][4][5] Student numbers grew from 200 in 2019/20 to 589 in 2023/24, with 59 teachers.[1]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
An **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** covering grades 7-13 (Mittelstufe 7-10, Oberstufe 11-13), with gymnasialer Oberstufe enabling pathways like Abitur; offers berufsorientiertes Profil and gesundheitsbetontes Profil.[1][8] Fremdsprachen include English and Spanish.[1] Reformpädagogisch oriented with year-group-spanning instruction, full-day gebundene Ganztagsschule structure, and profiles in vocational orientation and health focus.[1][2][7]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars emphasize arts, culture, and projects: theater productions, art exhibitions/excursions (e.g., Dessau, Leipzig Book Fair), makerspace, school garden (competition winners), student company "ISS-Mein-Essen", junior elections, reading day, prevention day, and Kulturagenten artistic programs.[2][8] Sports include volleyball tournaments (victors), team sport festivals, and integrated sports hall.[5][8] Events feature open doors, future fairs (Zukunftsmesse), parents' evenings, and visits like from Bildungssenatorin Katharina Günther-Wünsch.[8]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Won Holzbaupreis 2019 for sustainable building.[3] School garden competition victory.[8] Sports success: Sieg beim Oster-Volleyballturnier.[8] Positive outcomes in student firm and bee integration; featured in Oberschulbroschüre and visited by officials.[1][8] Enrollment growth indicates demand; Belastungsstufe 4 per STYPS classification.[1]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
Public tuition-free school with specific enrollment for Mittelstufe (grades 7-9) and Oberstufe (11-13), promoted via Oberschulmesse, Oberschulbroschüre, open doors (e.g., Dec 2024), and flyers detailing school concept and daily life.[1][8] No tuition or fees mentioned; process announced periodically (e.g., Jan 2024 post).[8]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The school emphasizes a **MINT-focused** (Mathematics, Informatics, Natural Sciences, Technology) education alongside strong sports programs in its teilgebundene Ganztagsschule (partially mandatory all-day school) model, serving approximately 350 students with priorities on natural sciences, sports, democracy education, reading promotion, career orientation, and social work.[7][2][3] Core values include fostering responsibility through projects like Schülerfirma (student company), Leseförderung (reading promotion), and Schulsozialarbeit (school social work) based on voluntariness and confidentiality, supporting student counseling, conflict management, and integration.[1][7] The approach integrates professional social-pedagogical support, berufsorientierung (career guidance), and community partnerships to prevent school disengagement and promote holistic development.[1][3]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Built between 1910-1912 as the Realgymnasium Schmargendorf (designed by Alfred Solbach), the building served various schools post-WWII, including the 8. Grundschule and from 1957 the 3. Oberschule Technischer Zweig, before becoming the current Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS) without gymnasiale Oberstufe.[5] Located in Berlin's Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf district (Schmargendorf neighborhood) at Kranzer Str. 3, 14199 Berlin, it features a three-story twin-wing structure with a rear schoolyard for sports and play, computer rooms, media/classrooms with notebooks and smartboards, and relaxation areas.[2][4][5][6] Community ties include partnerships with OSZ KIM, Louise-Schroeder-Schule, local businesses, Lehrreich Wilmersdorf, and organizations like Terre des Femmes and Maneo e.V. for projects on human rights and diversity; leadership by Schulleiterin Martina Schult and deputy Jeanette Damiano.[3][4][6][7][8] Current enrollment is ~350 students and 35 teachers (2023/24), down from peaks near 950 in 2011/12.[2]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As an **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** for grades 7-10 (without upper secondary gymnasiale Oberstufe), it offers a broad Wahlpflichtunterricht (elective compulsory courses) in double year-groups (7/8 and 9/10, except French), covering subjects like NaWi (natural sciences), Sprachen (languages), Informatik, WAT (economy/work/technology), Kreativ, Sport, and more, with core foreign languages English (1st) and French (2nd).[2][3][4][7] Special programs include **MINT emphasis** (projects, competitions, girls in MINT, Azubitag), dual learning forms (Praxislerngruppen), support for new arrivals without German skills, special education small classes, student company, and career orientation/BSO.[1][3][4][7] No explicit graduation pathways like Abitur are mentioned (focus on MSA/BBR typical for ISS); reading promotion, ethics, and democracy projects integrate across curriculum.[3][7]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars feature extensive **sports** (basketball in ALBA-Oberschulliga, minimarathon, Uber Arena trips, football, volleyball, table tennis, gymnastics/dance, cycling AG), **arts** (Christmas concerts, creative writing, chemical experiments as "Weihnachtsbaum", band, pop choir, guitar, keyboard, drama, art workshop, media design), and clubs (cooking, computer games, Mofa course, writing workshop).[4][7] Experiences include author visits (e.g., Mawil on Kinderland), field trips (Technical Museum, basketball games), service learning (soup kitchen), human rights campaigns (STOP FRÜHEHEN! with Terre des Femmes), democracy projects (Grundgesetz day, U18-Wahl), biology AG greening the school, and excursions/wandertage supported by social workers.[1][7] All-day operations include midday meals and social training.[1][7]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific awards, rankings, or certifications are detailed in the content; highlights include successful sports participation (ALBA league, minimarathon), cultural events (Weihnachtskonzert, Rotes Rathaus rainbow cake event with mayor), and project engagements (author visits, human rights days), with enrollment stability at ~350 students.[2][7] Prüfungsergebnisse (exam results) and management details are referenced but not quantified.[4]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **public tuition-free school** (Öffentlich), admission prioritizes average grade from primary school Förderprognose (promotion prognosis) and other criteria if oversubscribed; open to grades 7+ with supports for language learners and special needs.[2][4][7] Contact sekretariat@sekundarschule-wilmersdorf.schule.berlin.de for inquiries; Tag der offenen Tür (open house) and intro films provide overviews, with new 7th graders welcomed via events like museum trips.[4][7]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Isaac-Newton-Schule emphasizes **Vielfalt (diversity), Respekt (respect), and Freude am Lernen (joy of learning)**, aiming to be a place of education and life joy where no child leaves having lost their innate love for learning, as quoted from Prof. Dr. Gerald Hüther[5][1]. It fosters **transparency, cooperation, and participation** among students, parents, and staff to overcome challenges, with a focus on individual support, creativity, practical orientation, and openness to new ideas[5][1]. Core pillars include building strong foundations through experiments and projects, promoting problem-solving, solution-oriented thinking, self-confidence, and preparation for future challenges in a diverse, tolerant community[3][5][6].

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Located in **Berlin-Oberschöneweide, Treptow-Köpenick district** at Zeppelinstr. 80-88 (or 76-80), the school occupies a **historical building from the Wilhelminian era** near the former AEG factories and Volkspark Wuhlheide[1][2][4]. It serves around **389-451 students** primarily from the local area, with one in five having a migration background, supported by **37 teachers, 2 educators, 2 social workers**, school social services, administration, a janitor, and a new workshop master[1][2][4]. The school acts as a **learning, life, and experience space** for positive development, including multiprofessional teams and projects like "Cool Learning" with the youth office for at-risk students[2][4].

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As an **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** for **grades 7-10** (no upper secondary on-site), it offers profiles in **art, music, languages, career orientation, and health**, with **English as first foreign language** (extra profile hour) and **French as second**; further Abitur possible at partner schools like Merian-Schule or Hermann-Scheer-Schule[1][2][6]. Curriculum focuses on **practical, project-based learning** with experiments, real-world problem-solving, competence development (e.g., source analysis in history, political education, art techniques like printing and ceramics), Wahlpflichtunterricht (electives), Förderunterricht (remedial), and **Praxislernen** (practical dual learning for grades 9-10 with KIDS&Co for challenged students leading to school completion)[3][6]. Emphasis on **MSA (Mittlerer Schulabschluss)** preparation, vocational transition, and closing learning gaps via small-group, year-spanning "Cool Learning" sessions[2][3].

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in **extracurriculars** like Theater AG (e.g., adapted "Drei Haselnüsse für Aschenbrödel"), Winterball, Weihnachtschallenge, Projekttage/-wochen, Hoffest, class trips/excursions, team-building, and erlebnispädagogische (experiential) offers[main page][2][4]. **Arts and culture** include music, visual arts (painting, ceramics, film, street art), and presentations; **sports/events** via projects and social work; **career/vocational** through Praxislerngruppe, berufsbildungsangebote (vocational training offers), and Ausbilder support[3][4][6]. Community events promote **inclusion, motivation, and parent involvement**, with daily small-group learning for fun and success[2][main page].

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific awards, rankings, or certifications are mentioned; outcomes focus on **student development** toward school completion (e.g., MSA), vocational integration, closing learning gaps, and building motivation/self-confidence via targeted programs like Cool Learning and Praxislernen[2][3]. Enrollment grew from 391 (2022/23) to 451 (2023/24)[1]. Prüfungsergebnisse (exam results) are referenced but not detailed[7].

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **public, tuition-free Öffentlich school**, enrollment is open without fees; prospective grade 7 students and parents can explore via website info, "Ein erster Blick" section, and events like Tag der offenen Tür (open day)[main page][1][7][8]. Contact: sekretariat@isaac-newton-schule.de; prioritizes local Treptow-Köpenick students, with support for transitions and individual needs[2][9].

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Janusz-Korczak-Schule emphasizes **nachhaltige Bildung** (sustainable education) as its core focus, promoting praxisbezogene Bildung (practice-oriented education), awareness of health and sustainable consumption, the importance of human dignity, and societal responsibility[5][6]. It prioritizes zukunftsorientierte Kompetenzen (future-oriented competencies), including social, emotional, and value-based skills through lebensnahes Lernen (life-relevant learning) and social orientation[5][6].

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
The school is housed in a historic building originally constructed as the Jewish Orphanage Berlin-Pankow in 1882 for refugee children, rebuilt in 1913 after a fire, closed in 1940, and restored by the Dr. Walter and Margarete Cajewitz Foundation in 1999; it has served as the school's location since around 2007[1][4]. Located in Berlin-Pankow district at Dolomitenstr. 94, 13187 Berlin, it serves the local community in a green area, with no specific modern facilities detailed beyond a virtual tour option[3][5]. Enrollment has remained stable at around 365-370 students with 30-38 teachers from 2011-2022[3].

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As an **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** without gymnasiale Oberstufe, it offers secondary education (typically grades 7-10), with pathways including MSA (Mittlerer Schulabschluss), possibly BBR (vocational), and Abitur via cooperations with Oberstufenzentren like Oberstufenzentrum Unternehmenssoftware, Büromanagement und Sport, and TIEM (Technische Informatik, Industrieelektronik, Energie, Management)[3][5][6][7]. Key programs include berufs- und studienorientierung from grade 7, schwimmunterricht (swimming instruction), and sustainability-integrated teaching[5][6].

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in active partizipation in school life design, lernen mit allen Sinnen (learning with all senses), and methods-, team-, and communication training[5][6]. Sustainability-focused experiences promote health awareness and societal responsibility, with career orientation starting early[5][6].

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific awards, rankings, or certifications are mentioned in the available content. Historical enrollment stability indicates consistent operation, but no outcome data like prüfungsergebnisse (exam results) is detailed beyond general statistics[3][7].

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a public (öffentlich) tuition-free school, enrollment follows standard Berlin public secondary school processes with no fees or special requirements noted; contact via sekretariat@jks-pankow.de or phone +49 30 47909940[7][9].

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Jean-Krämer-Schule emphasizes a welcoming environment focused on learning, personal growth, talent development, and community building, positioning itself as a place for students to discover new skills, form friendships, and receive support from teachers.[1][5][7] It promotes comprehensive career orientation and personal development for young people.[5] No explicit mission statement is provided, but the school's approach highlights openness, supportiveness, and opportunities for individual unfolding.[7]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Built between 1928 and 1931 by architects Jean Krämer and Hans Krecke as a Volksschule in a distinctive curved design with rounded corners; renamed Jean-Krämer-Schule in 1972.[2] Located in Berlin-Reinickendorf (Wittenau district) at Alt-Wittenau 8-12, 13437 Berlin, near S&U Wittenau station and buses M21/120; accessible via public transport.[1][3] Facilities include holzwerkstatt (wood workshop), Lehrküche (teaching kitchen), Keramikwerkstatt (ceramics workshop), Textilwerkstatt (textile workshop), rollstuhlgerechter Aufzug (wheelchair-accessible elevator), rollstuhlgerechtes WC (wheelchair-accessible toilet), and parking for disabled persons.[6] Recent renovations (2016-2021) replaced blue facade panels with yellow ones; a modular extension building (MEB) added in 2019 for ~190 more students.[2] Community ties include schulgarten (school garden) for growing produce used in kitchens/cafeteria, and partnerships like Jugendsozialarbeit (youth social work).[2][8] Enrollment stable around 500-570 students with 60-65 teachers (latest 2023/24: 524 students, 62 teachers).[1]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
**Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** without gymnasialer Oberstufe, serving grades 7-10 (focus on new 7th graders).[1][6] Profiles: berufsorientiertes Profil (career-oriented), gesundheitsbetontes Profil (health-focused), Mathematisch-naturwissenschaftliches Profil (math/science), sprachbetontes Profil (language-emphasized), Wirtschaft-Arbeit-Technik (WAT).[1][6] Languages: 1st foreign language English, 2nd French; bilingual offers Deutsch/Englisch.[1][6] Special programs: Lerngruppen für Neuzugänge ohne Deutschkenntnisse (learning groups for new arrivals without German skills), Praxislerngruppe (practical learning group), temporäre Lerngruppe (temporary learning group, e.g., Klasse Füchse for school-distant students), Tablettklassen (tablet classes), Teilgebundener Ganztagsbetrieb (partial all-day program), Schulstation.[6][8] Activities: Betriebspraktikum (internships for 9th grade), Intensivwoche (intensive week), Präsentationsprüfungen (presentations for 10th grade), VERA 8 (standardized tests for 8th grade), mündliche Fremdsprachenprüfung (oral foreign language exams for 10th).[website] Graduation pathways not detailed, but ISS typically leads to Mittlerer Schulabschluss (MSA) or vocational paths (BBR); no Abitur mentioned.[1]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars and AGs (Arbeitsgemeinschaften): Wahlpflichtkurse (electives) in Mathematik-Naturwissenschaften (math/science), WAT, Französisch, Fußball (soccer), Fahrradwerkstatt (bike workshop), Keramik (ceramics), Selbstverteidigung (self-defense), Mediation, Klassensprechercoaching (class representative coaching), Schulsanitätsdienst (school health service).[6] Events: Tag der offenen Tür (open house, next 16.01.2026), Fitnesstag (fitness day), Infoveranstaltungen (info evenings), Lernentwicklungsgespräche (learning development talks), Oberschulmesse (secondary school fair).[website][5] Digital tools: WebUntis for absences, Teams access support, online student ID creation (Cardy app: digital €1.95, card €3.95, or free via teacher).[website] Practical experiences via workshops, garden, kitchen, internships, and social work integration.[2][6][8]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific awards, rankings, or certifications mentioned in available content. Prüfungsergebnisse (exam results) referenced but not detailed.[6] Stable enrollment and teacher numbers indicate consistent operation; Belastungsstufe 6 (stress level) noted.[1][6] Leadership: Dr. Steffi Rosen.[6]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
Public tuition-free school; enrollment for new 7th grade via Anmeldezeitraum (05.03.2026-12.03.2026).[website] Open house on 16.01.2026 (15-18 Uhr) for prospective 7th graders.[website] Aufnahmekriterien bei Übernachfrage (admission criteria if oversubscribed): Durchschnittsnote der Förderprognose (average grade from primary school recommendation), others.[6] Contact: 030-22503360, sekretariat@12k07.schule.berlin.de.[website][3]

description:
# Johanna-Eck-Schule School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

The Johanna-Eck-Schule is named after **Johanna Eck, Righteous Among the Nations**, and is guided by her philosophy: "Die Menschen, so will es mir scheinen, bilden eine große Einheit, und wo sie einander unrecht tun, schlagen sie sich selbst und allen ins Gesicht" (People form a great unity, and when they wrong each other, they strike themselves and everyone in the face).[3]

The school's core educational approach emphasizes **learning together and from one another**.[2] Students in the same learning group learn collaboratively regardless of individual performance levels, valuing social learning through intensive pedagogical support and individualized learning opportunities.[2] The school prioritizes **integration, democracy, and history** as central themes, with annual class trips organized under the motto "Democracy and History."[6]

The school operates as a **partially bound all-day school** (Teilgebundener Ganztagsbetrieb) with an innovative, agile school program that continuously evolves to meet contemporary educational challenges.[3]

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Location and District:**
Johanna-Eck-Schule is located in **Berlin-Tempelhof**, Tempelhof-Schöneberg district, at Ringstraße 103-106, 12105 Berlin.[1][5] The school also operates a branch location at Alt-Tempelhof 53, 12103 Berlin.[5]

**Historical Context:**
The school was formerly known as **Dag-Hammarskjöld-Schule** (named after the first UN Secretary-General) before being renamed to honor Johanna Eck.[6]

**Current Enrollment:**
As of 2023/24, the school serves **591 students** with **72 teachers**.[1] The school currently educates grades 7–10, with plans to expand to a full community school (Gemeinschaftsschule) serving grades 1–13.[3][9]

**Facilities:**
The school includes an aula (assembly hall), computer labs, an imbiss-station (snack bar), and a multipurpose building housing the cafeteria.[5] **Sonnenkäfer-Catering** provides contracted meal services under standards set by the Tempelhof-Schöneberg district.[3]

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**Grades and School Type:**
The school is an **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** without a gymnasium upper level, currently serving grades 7–10.[1][3]

**Languages Offered:**
Students learn **English** (first foreign language), with choices of **French or Spanish** as second foreign languages.[1]

**Core Academic Programs:**

- **Differentiated courses** in Mathematics and English for grades 9–10, tailored to different performance levels and graduation goals[2]
- **Dual learning model** with practical learning groups (Praxislerngruppen)[5]
- **Welcome classes** (AB-Klassen) for newly arrived students without German language skills, with age-appropriate cross-grade instruction[2][5]
- **Special profile subjects** (ungraded, year-long courses): "Glück" (Happiness), "Verantwortung" (Responsibility), "Haltung" (Attitude), and "Zukunft" (Future), which can be combined with core subjects like Deutsch, Politik, and Geschichte[3][6]
- **WAT (Wirtschaft-Arbeit-Technik)** – Economics, Work, and Technology – a subject specific to integrated secondary schools[6]

**Subject Offerings:**
Physics, Chemistry, Biology, Mathematics, Informatics, German, History, Ethics, English, French, Spanish, Music, Art, Theater, and Woodworking.[3]

**Graduation Pathways:**
The school prepares students for **MSA (Mittlerer Schulabschluss)** and **BBR (Berufsbildungsreife)** certifications.[5] Career orientation is emphasized through annual career fairs and work-study programs.[3]

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**Extracurricular Activities and Clubs:**
The school organizes diverse working groups (Arbeitsgemeinschaften) in sports, arts/creative fields, and MINT (Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics).[4]

**Sports and Physical Education:**
- Cricket workshops[3]
- Gender-inclusive swimming programs, including dedicated girls' swimming sessions in welcome classes[6]
- Sports-focused activities emphasizing community building[3]

**Arts and Culture:**
- Musical evenings (four annual events)[3]
- Theater programs[3]
- Art workshops and exhibitions[3]
- Woodworking classes[3]

**Student Participation and Democracy:**
- Class council hours (Klassenratsstunden)[4]
- Student representative body (GSV – Gesamtschülervertretung)[4]
- Student speaker elections and leadership roles[6]
- Participatory projects strengthening student voice[4]

**International Engagement:**
- Student exchanges and visits from international guests (e.g., USA visitors)[6]
- Integration support for newly arrived students from abroad[2]

**Pause and Wellness:**
- Supervised break activities offering movement, games, and relaxation[4]
- Thematic project days and weeks[4]

**Career and Professional Development:**
- Annual career fairs with diverse industry representatives[3]
- Apprenticeship pathway guidance[3]
- Work-study programs and practical learning groups[5]

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

The search results do not provide specific information about standardized test scores, awards, or external rankings. However, the school demonstrates commitment to quality through:

- **Stress level classification**: The school is rated at **Belastungsstufe 5** (stress level 5) on the school typification scale (STYPS), indicating high student need and socioeconomic challenges in the catchment area.[1]
- **Continuous improvement**: The school maintains an agile, evolving school program with documented development priorities for 2024–2026, including literacy promotion and digital production integration.[3]
- **Community recognition**: The school has attracted professional filmmakers and maintains partnerships with external organizations and associations.[6]

## 6. ADMISSIONS

**Enrollment Process:**
Admission to grade 7 occurs through the **Schulamt Tempelhof-Schöneberg** (district school office). Admission notices are issued in late June for the following school year.[6] The school received admission requests as first-choice preferences from families in 2025.[6]

**Admission Criteria:**
Admission criteria at over-capacity include consideration of the **average grade of the support prognosis** (Durchschnittsnote der Förderprognose) and other factors.[5]

**Trial Classes (Schnupperunterricht):**
The school offers **trial lessons** for prospective 6th-grade students (and occasionally 5th graders with special circumstances) from January 5–23 annually.[3][6] Students can sample classes in core subjects (Mathematics, German, English), WAT, and special profile courses. Early registration is encouraged to accommodate subject preferences.[3]

**School Contact:**
- **Phone:** +49 30 902772676[5]
- **Email:** sekretariat@johanna-eck.schule.berlin.de[5]
- **Website:** johanna-eck-schule.de[5]
- **Principal:** Robert Heinrich[5]

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Johann-Julius-Hecker-Schule emphasizes a **praxis-oriented education** modeled after Johann Julius Hecker, the founder of Berlin's practical Realschule, with strengthened instruction in core subjects (**Deutsch, Mathematik, Englisch**) and a focus on practical learning, career orientation, elective courses in school workshops, and off-site learning locations.[1][2][6] It promotes a **gewaltfreie und freundliche Schulatmosphäre** (violence-free and friendly school atmosphere) in a quiet, green location at Berlin's city edge, providing optimal learning conditions post-renovation, while fostering learning readiness to master challenges.[6] As a teilgebundene Ganztagsschule (partially extended-day school), it integrates social-pedagogical support from partners like tandem BTL for education and leisure offers.[1]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Named after **Johann Julius Hecker**, the school is an **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** in Berlin's Marzahn-Hellersdorf district, serving around **580 students** in a friendly atmosphere with berufs- and sport-oriented profiles.[1][2][4] Located at **Hohenwalder Str. 2, 12689 Berlin** in a calm, green suburban area, it features post-sanierung optimal facilities including two computer rooms (16 workstations each), media islands in classrooms, projectors, smartboards, teaching kitchen, wood and metal workshops, wheelchair-accessible elevator, WC, and ramps.[4][6] Community highlights include a **Schulgarten** that won the 2020 "Gemeinsam Gärtnern für die Vielfalt in Marzahn-Hellersdorf" competition, school merchandise, and partnerships like Strahlemann Talent Company for career support (opened ~2023).[3][5] Leadership: Direktorin **Jana Harwardt**, with deputies Herr Wedde and Herr Neidel.[6][8]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As an **ISS (grades 7-10)**, it offers reinforced core subjects, practical vocational training via workshops and external sites, dual learning practice groups, sports emphasis, and inclusive education.[1][2][4][5] Languages: **Englisch** (1st), **Französisch or Russisch** (2nd).[4] Graduation pathways aim for the best possible Abschluss (e.g., MSA, BBR, potentially Abitur via further paths; detailed options listed on site).[5] Features teilgebundener Ganztagsbetrieb, homework supervision, Nachhilfe, and berufsorientiertes Profil.[1][4]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Practical focus includes **Berufsorientierung** (career orientation), Wahlpflichtunterricht in workshops, außerschulische Lernorte, and Strahlemann Talent Company exchanges with companies.[2][3] Extracurriculars: Arbeitsgemeinschaften (AGs) via partners, Hausaufgabenbetreuung, Nachhilfe, sports programs, Schulgarten activities, ganztags leisure offers, and events like Hecker-Shirt days honoring school history.[1][4][5] Accessibility supports inklusion; transport: S7, buses X69/197/390, trams M8/16 to Barnimplatz.[6]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Won **2020 Schulgarten competition** ("Gemeinsam Gärtnern für die Vielfalt in Marzahn-Hellersdorf").[5] Selected as a **Strahlemann Talent Company school** (opened ~2023) for enhanced vocational transitions.[3] No specific awards, rankings, or exam outcomes detailed in content; prüfungsergebnisse available via official portal.[4]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
Public tuition-free school; enrollment for new 7th grades: **March 5-12, 2025**, with appointment booking soon on website; includes info on ISS vs. Gymnasium, forecasts, languages.[6][9] At overdemand, criteria: Durchschnittsnote der Förderprognose and others.[4] Events: **Tag der offenen Tür January 28, 2026 (18-20 Uhr)**; first parent meeting for new 7th graders July 10, 2025 (17:00).[6][9] Contact: sekretariat@hecker.schule.berlin.de, +49 30 930209-10.[6]
