description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
John F. Kennedy School (JFKS) is dedicated to providing a **German-American bicultural, bilingual education of the highest quality** in Berlin, creating community in Berlin and around the world since 1960.[1] Learning emphasizes making meaningful connections, building student creativity, and lifelong learning, with teachers as facilitators using bicultural, bilingual, and problem-based methods.[1] The school develops students' entire personalities through a future-oriented program that fosters shared understanding and high standards.[3][4]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in **1960**, JFKS serves over **1600 German, American, and international students** from Entrance Class through Grade 12.[1][2][4] Located at **Teltower Damm 87-93, 14167 Berlin, Germany** in the Steglitz-Zehlendorf district, the campus includes separate facilities like the Hort Building.[1][2] It builds a diverse community of German and American students in a tuition-free public setting.[1]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
JFKS offers programs from **Entrance Class through Grade 12**, divided into Elementary School (Grades 1-6 with sub-levels Grades 1-2, 3-4, 5-6), Entrance Class, and High School (Grades 7-8, 9-10, 11-12).[1] The bilingual curriculum includes **ES/HS Language Programs**, Religion & All Together, and departments leading to **Abitur & Diploma** as college-preparatory pathways.[1][4] It supports German and American curricula in a bicultural format.[3]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars include **Fine Arts** (music, drama, art, performances; member of International School Theatre Association (ISTA) and Association for Music in International Schools (AMIS)), **Sports** (swim, lacrosse (JFKS Rams), basketball, tennis; focusing on sportsmanship, teamwork, discipline, competition, skill, and character), **Model United Nations (BERMUN/Jr-MUN)** for grades 7-12 (research, debate on global issues), drama productions (Shakespeare, Grease), choir, ski trips, and events like Reading Series, Open Mic Night, ISTA Trivia Night.[1] **Student Support Services** feature counselors, Abitur coordinators, special education teachers, pedagogical assistants, registrar, learning support, and college prep/career counseling.[1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
JFKS is described as one of Berlin's **most sought-after and renowned schools** with consistently high standards and results, attracting global applications; it is a college-preparatory public school.[3][4] No specific awards, rankings, or certifications are detailed in the content.[1]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **tuition-free public school**, admission is competitive and selective; contact **welcome@jfksberlin.org** for questions and **admissions@jfksberlin.org** to submit applications.[1][5] Office hours are 08:00-15:00, with phone lines for ES/HS German and American secretaries.[1]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Jüdisches Gymnasium Moses Mendelssohn (JGMM) and Rabbinerin-Regina-Jonas-Schule (JONAS) are state-recognized private schools of the Jüdische Gemeinde zu Berlin, emphasizing lived Judaism through mandatory Hebrew and Jewish religious studies for all students, regardless of faith. They serve as places of learning and communal life, fostering tolerance, acceptance, and integration amid differences in belief, origin, and ability, with a values framework rooted in Jewish tradition (Massoret), human dignity (Kwod haBrijot), and lifelong learning (Limmud), opposing antisemitism, racism, and violence while promoting differentiated instruction.[2][6]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 1862 at Große Hamburger Str. 27 in Berlin-Mitte, the school merged boys' and girls' institutions in 1931, was closed by Nazis in 1942, and reopened in 1993 in its historic listed building (modernized with a glass tunnel attic by architects Kay Zareh and Ruth Golan), renamed JGMM in 2012, with JONAS added in 2023/2024. It enrolls about 550 Jewish (70%) and non-Jewish (30%, mostly Christian or non-confessional) students in classes capped at 24 (fewer at JONAS), offering kosher meals daily (meat or vegetarian, supervised by Rav Ehrenberg, included in fees), modern digital boards in all classrooms, Microsoft 365 licenses since 2020, stationary computers/laptops, and a sekretariat open weekdays (until 15:45, Fridays 14:00).[1][2][3][4]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
JGMM is an ISS-Gymnasium (integrated secondary school) from grade 5 (gymnasial track) through Oberstufe (qualification phase to Abitur), with JONAS as integrated Sekundarschule grades 7-10; Hebrew is obligatory grades 5-10 (modern Hebrew to B/C levels, with catch-up for transfers), plus Jewish religion/philosophy (mandatory Grundkurs in Oberstufe, electable as Leistungskurs). Other languages: English (S1), French (2nd/3rd from grade 7, DELF certifiable), Russian (AG, Oberstufe Leistungskurs/S3); Leistungskurse include art, biology, German, English, geography, history, Jewish philosophy, math, Russian. Features teilgebundener Ganztagsbetrieb, digital integration, and state recognition as Ersatzschule; graduation via Abitur (Gymnasium), MSA possible.[1][2][5][7][8]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
All students participate in Jewish holidays, kosher lunches, and religion/Hebrew classes; extracurriculars include Schnellschach team (recent win), "Quiz der Religionen," Gedenken events, Schulsanitätsteam, Moses Journal (Winter/Pessach 2025), Oberstufe trips, Konflikt AG, Mediation/Streitschlichter, Russisch AG. Digital tools (Teams, Promethean boards) and computer curriculum enhance learning.[2][7]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Schnellschach team success highlighted; first post-Holocaust Jewish Gymnasium in Germany leading to Abitur; no specific rankings, awards, or test outcomes detailed in content.[2][9]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
**Enrollment Process**: Annual intake: one grade 5 class (max 24 students), two grade 7 Gymnasium classes (max 24 each, requires grade sum ≤14 in German/Math/English from grades 5-6 or probe class), max 14 for Sekundarschule grades 7-10. Open Day: 18 January 2026 (11-15 Uhr). Required docs: application form, school reports/prognosis, motivation letter (handwritten by student), child ID/vaccination/Berlinpass. Quereinstieg possible if places available (esp. grades 7-8 with French/Russian; Hebrew catch-up required); Oberstufe needs MSA. Apply via online form to info@jgmm.de or Jugendförderung (for contracts).[2]

**Fees**: No exact EUR amounts listed on scraped pages (Fees/Tuition page empty, no PDFs with fee info). School contract and payments via Jüdische Gemeinde's Dezernat für Bildung und Jugend (Oranienburger Str. 28-31, tel. 030/880 28-133); kosher meals included in Schulgeld. No registration/material/after-school/scholarship details provided.[2]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As an **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** (integrated secondary school without upper gymnasium level, school code 12K01), it serves grades 7-10, enabling the Mittlerer Schulabschluss (MSA) for all qualified students, with pathways to higher qualifications via partnerships with schools offering gymnasium upper levels; no Abitur or BBR directly mentioned, but strong vocational orientation.[2][4][5][8] Languages include English (1st) and Spanish (2nd).[2][8] Programs feature reinforced core subjects (German, math, sciences, social studies), practical workshops, daily reading, differentiated electives, 3-week internships in 9th/10th grades, company visits, career counseling from grade 7, sports emphasis, and all-day operations with school station.[1][5][8]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in extracurriculars like voluntary all-day Arbeitsgemeinschaften (working groups), guided meditation breaks (Tuesdays/Thursdays), Nachhilfe-Café tutoring (Mon-Thu 15:00-16:00), school discos, reading competitions (grades 7-8), charity runs (e.g., for Deutsche Kinderkrebsstiftung with Team Rynkeby), and schoolyard redesign projects.[1][3] Social initiatives include human rights projects (Amnesty letter marathon, role-playing, posters), SV workshops with Friedrich-Ebert-Stiftung on rights and projects, proRespekt for democratic culture, and class representative training.[1][3] Sports profile supports physical activities; multiprofessional support enhances daily experiences.[7][8]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific awards, rankings, or certifications are detailed, but successes include successful events like the 2025 reading competition, first school disco with positive atmosphere, charity runs, human rights engagements, and schoolyard improvements via volunteer efforts.[1][3] Enrollment stable around 400-416 students with 42-45 teachers (2023/24); inspection highlights strengths in vocational prep, social climate, school social work, and reform adaptation.[2][5]

As a tuition-free public school (Öffentlich), enrollment is open; interested 6th-grade students and guardians can attend open house events or additional info sessions (e.g., 20.01.2026, 16:00-17:00 for personalized school tours).[1] No fees or selective requirements specified; focuses on welcoming families to assess fit.[1]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
Katholische Schule Sankt Franziskus (KSSF) is a Catholic school under the sponsorship of the Archdiocese of Berlin, emphasizing Christian values, community, and spiritual integration in education. The school begins each day with a morning school prayer and holds regular Gottesdienste (services), fostering a sense of togetherness and reflection, as seen in events like the Patronatstag with a festive service at St. Matthias Church.[1][2][10] It promotes an integrated, differentiated approach in an Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS) with gymnasialer Oberstufe, using binnendifferenzierung (internal differentiation) in subjects like Math, German, English (Grundniveau/Erweiterungsniveau), and sciences from grade 9, while keeping class units intact to support diverse learners without grade repetition risks.[1][6][7] The philosophy highlights practical competencies through open learning, project-based work, Lerntechniken, and berufsorientierung (career orientation), alongside Montessori principles in the Grundschule.[3][7]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 1889 as a "Katholische Höhere Mädchenschule" in Berlin-Schöneberg, the school was taken over by the Franziskanerinnen order in 1918, closed by Nazis in 1940, and reopened post-war; the main building, a listed monument designed by Margot Zech-Weymann, was inaugurated in 1959 by Cardinal Döpfner.[2] It became co-educational gradually (boys from 1959, full in Grundschule by 1967) and adopted its current ISS form in 2008 with gymnasial Oberstufe reopened.[2][5] Located at Hohenstaufenstr. 1-2, 10781 Berlin (Tempelhof-Schöneberg district), it serves a diverse community of ~750 students from over 25 nations, promoting peaceful Christian coexistence.[6] Facilities include a school building with Schulhof (playground), offene Kapelle (open chapel), Schulpastoral, Textilwerkstatt (textile workshop on 6th floor), Mensa (catered meals), Schulkiosk, and proximity to St. Matthias Church and local parks like Gleisdreieckpark.[1][scraped Main Page][scraped About Page]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a private **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** with Grundschule (two-stream, Montessori-influenced Verlässliche Halbtagsgrundschule) and gymnasialer Oberstufe up to grade 13, it covers grades JÜL (Eingangstufe) to 13, with classes like 1Md-6Md, 7a-d, 8a-d, 9a-d, 10a-d, 11a-c, 12, 13.[1][3][5][7][scraped Admissions Page] Differentiation occurs in core subjects from grade 7 (Math/Deutsch/Englisch: GR/ER levels; sciences from 9/10); Wahlpflichtfächer (electives) include Französisch/Spanisch (grade 7), then Art and Design/Drama/Histographie/Informatik/Naturwissenschaft/Sport/WAT (grade 9).[7][9] Languages: Englisch (S1), 2./3. Fremdsprachen (Latein/Spanisch/Französisch).[9] Unterrichtszeiten span up to 18:05, with structured pauses and grade-specific Essenspausen (meals for grades 5/6/7/8/9/10).[scraped Curriculum Page] Graduation pathways: **BBR** (Berufsbildungsreife), **eBBR** (erweiterte), **MSA** (Mittlerer Schulabschluss) after grade 9/10; transition to Oberstufe for **Abitur/Fachabitur** after 13 years.[5][7][8] Career prep includes Praktika, BIZ visits, Girls/Boys-Day from grade 7-10.[1]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in rich extracurriculars emphasizing arts, sports, projects, and outings: Adventskonzert (joint Grund-/Oberschule event at St. Matthias); Patronatstag; Sport Wahlpflicht excursions across Berlin; Gemeinschaftsspiele in Gleisdreieckpark; music projects like "Mythen und Legenden" with RIAS-Kammerchor at UdK; "Franziskus Voices" choir at Berliner Schulchorwettbewerb (FEZ Berlin); Upcycling Nähen (Bauchtasche); Goldschmiedin workshop (Art/Design); Känguru-Mathematikwettbewerb; history trips (AlliiertenMuseum, Marienfelde Notaufnahmelager, Barberini Museum Potsdam, Afro-Germans walking tour Schöneberg); bilingual AI short stories (9a); VR sexuality education ("Virtual S*Exploration"); Sozialpraktikum (grade 11, Suppenküche).[scraped Main Page][1] Weekly Box-AG, Schüler:innenparlament, diverse electives, and community service like Weihnachtsmarkt for social projects enhance experiences.[1][scraped Fees Page]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific awards, rankings, or certifications are detailed in the content, though participation in competitions like Känguru-Mathematikwettbewerb (31st edition, ~880,000 participants nationwide) and Berliner Schulchorwettbewerb indicates active involvement; first Abitur in 2011 after Oberstufe reopening.[scraped Main Page][2] The school supports progression to Abitur, with ongoing Leistungsstand feedback.[7]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
Admissions involve an Aufnahmeantrag flyer (PDF available); process references Förderverein (support association) with homepage www.foerderverein-kssf.de.[scraped Fees Page] No exact fee amounts (monthly/annual tuition, registration, materials, meals, after-school) are stated on scraped pages; all Schulgeld info directs to https://schulen-erzbistumberlin.de/schultraeger/schulgeld (Archdiocese site) or the 2019 Aufnahmeantrag PDF: http://kssf-berlin.de/neu/wp-content/uploads/2019/07/Flyer_mit_Beileger_2019_Endversion16.1.2019.pdf (may contain details).[scraped Fees Page] No scholarship or income-based options mentioned. Mensa meals are catered (no costs specified).[1]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
Katholische Schule Sankt Marien emphasizes education in a **Christian spirit**, promoting modern teaching, independent work, and talent development for every student.[5][1] The school supports parents in **christlich orientierte Wertebildung** (Christian-oriented values education), maintains a religions-sensitive approach to all students' beliefs, and fosters ethical standards based on a Christian view of humanity that respects individuality and aims for maturity and responsibility.[10][4] It unites an Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS) and Gymnasium under one roof, focusing on holistic development through teaching, learning, and community life.[5]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
The Integrierte Sekundarschule has operated as a three-track secondary school since the 2011/12 school year.[7] Located in **Berlin-Neukölln** at Donaustr. 58, 12043 Berlin, it is a private Catholic school under the Archdiocese of Berlin, led by **Schulzentrumsleiterin Frau Rahner (Theresa Rahner)**.[1][5][6] Facilities include a **cafeteria**, two computer rooms, laptops, whiteboards, and a 360° virtual tour; a kiosk offers snacks.[1][6][5] It features bound full-day operations (GGB), serves as a state-recognized substitute school, and includes a primary school section (Grundschulteil) in the school center.[1][6] Community ties involve the Förderverein, Bildungsspender support, and the Archdiocese's online network.[5]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The school offers **ISS (Integrierte Sekundarschule, grades ~5-10)** and **Gymnasium (grades 5-12/13, including Oberstufe)**, leading to **Abitur** via Gymnasium pathways; it supports Zweiter Bildungsweg (second educational path).[1][2][5][6] **Languages**: English (S1/S4), second foreign languages (French/Polish/Latin from grade 7), third (Latin/Spanish/French from grade 8); DELF prep, language trips (France, Spain, Poland), English/Polish competitions.[1][2][6] Special profiles: **kunstbetontes Profil** (art-focused, split classes in middle school), **theaterbetontes Profil**, **MINT-classes** (Math, IT, Science, Tech; e.g., 7/8Ga Mint-Forscherprojekt).[1][2][6] Differentiation from grade 7 (Math/English), grade 8 (German), grade 9 (sciences); remedial in German/Math, homework supervision.[1] Full-day ISS (mandatory, ends 15:30, Thursdays 13:45); detailed schedule from 8:00-17:00.[Curriculum Page] Leistungskurse in Oberstufe: Art, Biology, Chemistry, German, English, Geography, French, History, Math, Music, Political Education.[6] Grade 10 course choices cover linguistic-artistic, social sciences, MINT fields.[2]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
**Extracurriculars** include choir (e.g., Christmas performances), guitar lessons, chess, Catholic Student Youth, student company (e.g., Advent market), school newspaper (NIF insights), social projects (MISEREOR, soup kitchens, Third World aid).[1][2][5] **Sports**: Rugby, volleyball, basketball, soccer, strength training.[1][6] **Social/Community**: Mediators, school medical service, **Sozialpraktikum** (3-week in grade 10/11), class trips (e.g., 10Gb), Advent wreath/festivals.[2][5] Arts/theater emphasis; political education, career orientation.[1][6]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Student **Aanmihan Colin Cowdry** won a prize from the Deutsche Physikalische Gesellschaft.[Main Page] MINT research projects and competitions in languages noted; no broader rankings or certifications detailed.[1][2][5]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
**Process**: Open to all children/youth; decision by school leadership after interview with student/parents.[10][Admissions Page] For **grade 7 (ISS/Gymnasium, 2026/27)**: Schedule interview from 13.10.2025 (tel: 030-39881620) or open day 11.10.2025; use online application.[Admissions Page] For **grade 11**: Informal written application (email, fax 030/398816230, mail) with last 2 report copies.[Admissions Page] Downloadable docs: Rahmenschulordnung, contract termination, fee suspension request.[Admissions Page]  
**Fees**: No exact amounts listed on pages; refer to Archdiocese link (https://www.erzbistumberlin.de/bildung/katholische-schulen/schulgeld-und-ordnungen) with Vorab-Schulgeldrechner (preliminary fee calculator); PDFs available: "INFO Schulgeld Eltern Infoblatt.pdf" (175 KB), "Elterninformation Online-Anträge_Schulgeld.pdf" (134 KB), fee suspension form.[Admissions Page] Possible income-based options/suspensions implied; no specifics on registration, materials, meals, aftercare, scholarships.

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Kepler-Schule aims to provide students from grades 7 to 10 with a personal and appreciative learning atmosphere, enabling all to achieve the best possible school qualifications, particularly the Mittlerer Schulabschluss (MSA), and secure strong post-school prospects.[1][3] It emphasizes innovative instruction tailored to individual talents, interests, and learning styles through creative arrangements, fostering personal development and readiness for future challenges, while viewing digitalization as an opportunity.[1] Core values include participation between teachers and students, respect, curiosity, courage, and community engagement, as highlighted by testimonials from students, teachers, and parents praising development leaps, fun, and collaborative decision-making.[web:0][web:3]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Established around 2006 as an Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS) without upper secondary level, the school serves approximately 390 students, primarily from Neukölln's High-Deck-Siedlung area, a region with high social challenges including child poverty and youth unemployment.[1][5][6] Located at Zwillingestr. 21-29, 12057 Berlin-Neukölln near S-Bahn Köllnische Heide, it plans to relocate to Rudow in the second half of the school year due to renovations.[web:0][4] Facilities include a redesigned schoolyard (completed 2019), digital classrooms with WLAN and smartboards, a computer room, CNC milling machine, woodworking shop with student firm "Holzwurm," and a Lerninsel for independent learning.[1][5] Staff comprises 45 teachers, 5 school social workers, and 4 additional colleagues; leadership includes Schulleiterin Nadine Düppe (since 2022).[1][3][4][5]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Serves grades 7-10 as a four-track ISS, offering English as the first foreign language and French as the second.[1][2][5] Students can earn the MSA; Abitur is available via partner schools like OSZ Wirtschaft und Sozialversicherung or Otto-Hahn-Schule.[1][5] Special programs include student coaching by Lehramtsstudierende for personal and social development (supported by Ohne-Stiftung and school association), dual learning offers for vocational preparation, Hip-Hop profile class starting 2025/2026 for grades 7-10, and unique diving in elective curriculum.[1][3][5][7] High-quality instruction prepares for exams in a supportive environment.[5]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars feature project weeks (e.g., 8th graders exploring Berlin's history and politics), year trips (e.g., 7th grade team-building at Groß Väter See), reading days with kindergarten visits, market stands at Christmas markets, and volunteer appreciation events.[web:0] Unique offerings include diving projects, Hip-Hop class, Tauchen in Wahlpflichtunterricht (Germany's only school), woodworking in student firm, and Lerninsel coaching.[web:0][3][5] Community involvement via parent groups like "Keplers Aktiven Eltern," Teach First, and mentoring with Bürgerstiftung Neukölln; events like info evenings, open days, and Lesewettbewerb with prizes (e.g., Leipzig Book Fair trip).[web:0][5][8]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific awards or rankings are detailed, but the school notes exceptional student development leaps, redesigned schoolyard improving stay quality (2019), and unique national distinction as Germany's only school offering diving in curriculum.[web:0][5] Enrollment stable around 370 students with growing local intake (17% from immediate area in 2022); high teacher engagement supports MSA attainment and vocational prep in challenging context (Belastungsstufe 5).[1][2][5]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a tuition-free public school (Öffentlich), enrollment is open; info evenings and open doors introduce new 7th graders and parents (e.g., July 2025 for 2025/2026).[web:0][9] Contact sekretariat@kepler.schule.berlin.de or 030 221 99 44 00 (08:00-09:30, no anonymous calls); accessible by S-Bahn S45/S46/S47 or buses M41/246.[4]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
Kerschensteiner-Schule (K-ISS) is an integrated secondary school (Integrierte Sekundarschule, ISS) emphasizing a supportive community, individual student support, sport, and career orientation to prepare students for professional life.[1][2][3] Core values include fostering a familial atmosphere among students, teachers, and school social workers; encouraging student responsibility and active participation; and prioritizing social cohesion, success experiences for all, and transition to the workforce through internships, counseling, and projects starting in grade 7.[1][4]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Located in Berlin's Marzahn-Hellersdorf district (Golliner Str. 2, 12689 Berlin), the school serves around 300-318 students, making it the smallest ISS in the district, with 26 teachers.[1][2][4] It features a new modular wooden extension building opened for the 2025/26 school year, housing grades 9 and 10 to expand capacity and opportunities.[1] Community involvement includes school social work by DRK since 2006, supporting inclusion for students with special needs in language, learning, motor, and emotional areas; partnerships with youth services, job counseling, and local organizations; and student-led initiatives like a newspaper and podcast.[1][4]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The school offers grades 7-10 as a full-day operation (Ganztagsschule) without upper secondary (gymnasiale Oberstufe), focusing on integrated secondary education with daily sports in regular classes, electives, lunch programs, and AGs (e.g., soccer, volleyball, table tennis, parcours).[1][2][5] Career orientation is a key pillar, including in-house counseling, company internships in grades 9-10, and projects from grade 7; languages are English (1st) and French (2nd).[1][5] Assessments include MSA/eBBR exams, presentation exams in grade 10, and comparable works; it participates in inclusion trials and partial full-day operations.[1][5]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students prepare their own breakfast and lunch, promoting independence.[1] Extracurriculars encompass sports AGs, student newspaper (two issues published, led by teacher Frau Ditler), school podcast "K-ISS Check" (e.g., Christmas special), image film, Instagram updates, and events like open house (Jan 19, 2026), awards ceremonies, excursions, and summer festivals.[1][4] Social learning includes violence prevention, democracy projects, participation AGs, training room coordination, and 7th-grade integration days; full-day offers feature fixed class structures, two class leaders, and a dedicated social worker per 7th-grade class.[1][4]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific awards, rankings, or certifications are detailed, though student outputs like the newspaper and podcast highlight engagement.[1] Enrollment grew slightly from 313 (2022/23) to 318 (2023/24); the school is noted for its small size, internal values, and new facilities enabling pedagogical expansion.[1][2]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a public tuition-free school (öffentlich), enrollment follows Berlin public criteria; at oversubscription, priority uses average grade from primary school promotion prognosis.[2][5] Interested families can apply via the website's "Schüler:in werden" link; open house scheduled for January 19, 2026, from 4 PM.[1]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Königin-Luise-Stiftung's mission is encapsulated in its leitbild: **"Selbst sein - Miteinander - Weiterkommen"** (Be oneself - Together - Progress forward), emphasizing individuality, respectful social interaction, and fostering competence through demanding yet supportive education.[1][7] It promotes individual strengths and diversity ("Selbst sein"), a positive school climate through respect, dialogue, conflict resolution, and responsibility ("Miteinander"), and performance-oriented learning with self-directed methods, creativity, physical activity, and technology integration ("Weiterkommen").[1] The foundation blends traditional values from its classical roots with modern facilities, aiming for a culture of peace, environmental protection, sustainability, and global equity as a UNESCO-Projektschule.[1][3]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in **1811** as a "living monument" to Queen Luise, influenced by reformers like Delbrück and Humboldt, it evolved from a girls' institute into a co-educational campus offering schools and boarding for over 200 years.[1][3] The community includes **ca. 950 students**, **170 staff**, and **33 boarding places** (noted as 56 in some external sources) in a family-like environment across Grundschule, Sekundarschule, Gymnasium, and Jugendhilfe-Internat.[1][4] The campus in Berlin-Steglitz-Zehlendorf (Podbielskiallee 78, opposite the Botanical Garden) features school buildings, boarding areas, sports fields, playground, park, bio-certified kitchen, and specialized rooms (e.g., science labs, music/art rooms, workshops, computer labs).[1][3]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The school offers a continuous pathway from primary to upper secondary: **Grundschule** (grades 1-6, homogeneous classes plus mixed-age Montessori 1-4); **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS-Gymnasium)** (grades 7-12, enabling all Berlin qualifications: Berufsbildungsreife, Mittlerer Schulabschluss/MSA, Fachgebundene Hochschulreife, or **Abitur** after grade 12/13); **Gymnasium** (grades 5-12, performance-oriented with low class sizes of 18-23).[1][2][3][5][6][7] Languages follow **E-F-Sp** (English from grade 3/5, French, Spanish); differentiated instruction in core subjects; religions (Protestant/Catholic); broad upper-secondary courses including Philosophy, Film, Digitale Welten; no BBR mentioned.[2][3][4][6] Open all-day program with projects, homework help, electives.[2]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Experiences emphasize small classes (18-22 students), personal greetings by principals, prevention programs for social skills, inclusion via internal/external support, and family-like boarding in three homes.[1][2][6] Extracurriculars include AGs (working groups) in music, theater, sports, social learning, Hauptstadt-Aktivitäten (capital city projects), foreign partnerships, tandem projects; ganztagsbetreuung (all-day care) with homework, projects, electives; bio-meals with daily raw veggies/fruit, feedback options, special diets at no extra cost.[1][2][3][4]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Certified as **UNESCO-Projektschule** for peace, environment, sustainability, and equity projects; **Internationale Nachhaltigkeitsschule - Umweltschule in Europa** for "Bildung für nachhaltige Entwicklung."[1][3] Gymnasium Abitur average slightly above Berlin average; supports solid to top performers via broad courses/small groups.[6] State-recognized private school with ongoing quality focus.[4][9]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
Admissions require an online form selecting school type (Grundschule, ISS, Gymnasium, Internat), with child's name/grade, parents' details, desired entry time, cover letter, last two report cards, behavior assessments (PDF/DOC up to 10MB); contacts per section (e.g., Cornelia Otto for Grundschule).[1] Income-based tuition via annual application (by July for new year, effective next month; max rate without application); sibling discounts; reductions for social aid (e.g., §7 Lernmittelverordnung: welfare, housing aid); contacts: Michelle Zurek, Daniel Russau.[1] **No exact tuition amounts listed on pages**; external source notes **extern: income-dependent, max €360/month**; **intern: €1,787.43/month** plus extras (trips, sports); meals included in operations but bio-certified DGE-standard at no extra for specials.[1][4] No registration/material/after-school fees specified; scholarships via income scale.[1]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Konrad-Duden-Schule positions itself as a **weltoffene (open to the world), tolerante (tolerant), and praxisorientierte (practice-oriented)** Integrierte Sekundarschule (integrated secondary school), emphasizing respect, tolerance, and a fear-free, friendly atmosphere among students and staff.[1][2] It aims to provide comprehensive general education, foster individual strengths in areas like languages, sports, sciences, and social studies, and prepare students for optimal entry into professional life or upper secondary education through binnendifferenzierung (internal differentiation) in heterogeneous groups.[1][2] Berufs- und Studienorientierung (career and study orientation) holds significant importance, with daily commitment to promoting competencies tailored to each student's potential.[1][2]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Located in **Berlin-Niederschönhausen, Pankow district**, at Rolandstr. 35, 13156 Berlin, near Schlosspark, the school has grown significantly, taking in six classes per 7th grade since 2018/2019, making it one of Pankow's largest ISS.[1][3] Enrollment has increased from around 400 students in 2011-2017 to 637 in 2023/24, with 63 teachers.[3] Facilities include modern classrooms, a cafeteria for warm meals (new caterer nobis gGmbH since 2025/2026), and support for ganztägige Betreuung (all-day care) two days a week via Modul e.V.[2][4] Community ties are evident in partnerships like Siemens visits and local recycling initiatives.[8]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Serving **grades 7-10** as an ISS without gymnasialer Oberstufe, the school offers **schwerpunkte (focus areas)** in Sprachen (languages: English, French, Spanish, Chinese), Sport, and Wirtschaft/Arbeit/Technik (WAT, economy/work/technology).[1][2][3][6] Additional profiles include berufsorientiertes Profil, mathematisch-naturwissenschaftliches Profil, Informationstechnik, and sportbetontes Profil.[3][6] Instruction uses block scheduling (95-minute blocks Monday-Thursday, shorter on Fridays), internal differentiation in classes, groups, projects, and varied learning settings, with Mittagsband (midday band) for self-study, class advisor time, and meals.[Curriculum Page][2][4] Pathways prepare for individual Abschlüsse (e.g., MSA, BBR), Abitur via cooperation with Oberstufenzentren, and vocational entry through praktika (internships), Betriebspraktikum (class 9), Schnupperpraktikum (class 8), and JobDetektive tours.[1][2][5][6] Ganztag is teilgebunden (partially binding), mandatory AGs (working groups) for grades 7-9 unless exempted for external activities.[4]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars feature a wide range of **Arbeitsgemeinschaften (AGs)** via Modul e.V., mandatory for grades 7-9 (two 45-minute sessions), including Tischtennis, Graffiti, Tanzen, Theater, Kochen, Fahrradwerkstatt, Web Design, Stop Motion, Fotografie, Schülerband, Fußball, Allgemeine Spiele, Kreativ, Bewegung/Basteln/Bauen, Parkour, Volleyball, Schulgarten, Kraftsport.[4][6] Sports highlights include Fußballschulmannschaft competing in Berlin championships, Skifahrt to Zell am See, and school team projects.[Main Page] Trips encompass Gedenkstätte Sachsenhausen (mandatory for 10th grade), Siemens Schnuppertag, JobDetektive berufserkundung, and Pfandsammelaktion for recycling.[Main Page][5][8] Other experiences: Juniorwahlen 2025 (50.92% turnout), schulinterne Ausbildungsmesse.[Main Page]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific awards, rankings, or certifications are detailed, but successes include steady enrollment growth to 637 students (2023/24), successful Juniorwahlen 2025 with strong participation, Fußballschulmannschaft reviving participation in Berlin Schulmeisterschaften, and consistent events like Siemens visits and historical site trips as program staples.[3][Main Page][8] Prüfungsergebnisse and Förderprognose data are referenced but not quantified.[6]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **tuition-free public Öffentlich school**, admission for new 7th classes (six classes of ~26 students) involves a Tag der offenen Tür (open house, e.g., 29 January 2026, 17:00-19:30) for presentations and Anmeldung for 2026/2027.[Main Page][1][2] Aufnahmekriterien apply bei Übernachfrage (overdemand); contact Sekretariat@03K03.schule.berlin.de or +49 30 4450959.[6][8]

description:
# Konrad-Wachsmann-Schule School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

The Konrad-Wachsmann-Schule operates under the philosophy that "the grass does not grow faster if you pull on it," emphasizing individualized pathways for each student to achieve their best possible graduation outcome.[5] The school views itself as **a place of encounter for people of different ages, diverse characters, and varied backgrounds**, prioritizing respectful interaction and appreciative community.[5]

The school's pedagogical approach centers on three key pillars:

- **Knowledge and competency development** through subject instruction
- **Relationship-based work** via school social services that provide individual counseling for students and families
- **Self-directed learning methods**, including cross-grade learning studios and digital integration in classrooms[5]

The school emphasizes creating a friendly atmosphere where students can engage with new learning more easily, with particular attention to supporting students who have become distanced from regular school attendance through the "Coole Schule II" project.[1]

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Location and District:** The Konrad-Wachsmann-Schule is located at Geithainer Straße 12, 12627 Berlin, in the Hellersdorf district of Marzahn-Hellersdorf, on Berlin's eastern city edge.[1][2]

**School Type and Organization:** This is an **Integrated Secondary School (Integrierte Sekundarschule) without a gymnasium upper level**.[2] The school operates on a **part-time all-day schedule (Teilgebundener Ganztag)** with approximately 550 students.[1]

**Enrollment Growth:** The school has experienced steady enrollment growth, from 330 students in 2011/12 to 527 students in 2023/24.[2]

**Facilities and Infrastructure:**
- Two computer rooms with 36 total workstations[3]
- Classrooms equipped with digital whiteboards, laptops, and beamers[3]
- iPads available for student use[5]
- Accessibility features: wheelchair-accessible elevator and restroom, parking for people with disabilities[3]
- Meal service provided by Sunshine Catering[3]

**School Leadership:**
- Schulleiterin (Principal): Susanne Reich
- Stellvertretende Schulleiterin (Vice Principal): Rica Nitschke
- Mittelstufen-koordinatorin (Middle School Coordinator): Stephanie Schacke[8]

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**Grades and Structure:** The school serves grades 7-10, with students organized into year groups 7 & 8 and 9 & 10.[6]

**Languages Offered:**
- First foreign language: **English**
- Second foreign language: **French**[2][3]

**Educational Profile:** The school features a **career-oriented profile (berufsorientiertes Profil)** with special emphasis on dual learning pathways.[2][3]

**Special Programs and Offerings:**
- **Dual Learning (Duales Lernen):** Includes practical learning groups (Praxislerngruppen), business visits, internship programs, application training, and assessment centers[3]
- **Inclusion School Program:** Participates in the school trial for inclusion with 16 reserved places for students with special educational needs[3][7]
- **Differentiated Instruction:** External differentiation in mathematics and English for grade 7; specialized support courses in mathematics, English, and German; homework supervision[3]
- **Career Guidance:** Career passport program, business explorations, trial internships, and job application preparation[3]
- **Self-Organized Learning:** Implementation of cross-grade learning studios[5]

**Graduation Pathways:** Students can pursue various graduation certificates (MSA - Mittlerer Schulabschluss, BBR - Berufsbildungsreife) through the school's differentiated pathways.[5]

**Examination Schedule:** The school administers standardized exams in German, English, and Mathematics, with presentation exams as part of the assessment process.[9]

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**School Social Services:** The school employs school social workers who serve as trusted contacts for students, parents, and teachers, designing afternoon programming and supporting students with school attendance challenges.[1]

**Projects and Enrichment:** The school offers various projects in the areas of career orientation, school-based social work, and "School Without Racism" initiatives, along with subject-specific projects.[9]

**Support Programs:**
- Individual counseling with students and families regarding needs and wishes[5]
- Family and educational counseling in cooperation with SOS-Familienzentrum (available the first school Monday of each month)[9]
- Support for students distanced from school through the "Coole Schule II" project[1]

**School Schedule:** The school operates on a block schedule system with regular and shortened schedule options, with classes running from 8:30 AM to 3:50 PM (regular schedule) or 8:00 AM to 1:00 PM (shortened schedule).[6]

**Partnerships:** The school collaborates closely with primary schools, other secondary schools, vocational training centers, and external cooperation partners.[6]

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

The search results do not provide specific information about awards, rankings, certifications, or quantified achievement data. The school's **stress level classification (Belastungsstufe) is 5**, indicating the highest level of social and economic challenges in the school's catchment area.[2]

## 6. ADMISSIONS

**Enrollment Process:** For the 2025/26 school year, the registration period for new 7th-grade students is **March 5-12, 2026**. All registrations received during this period are recorded alphabetically, regardless of registration time.[7]

**Capacity:** The school accepts **104 seventh-grade students** for the 2025/26 school year when applications are submitted during the designated registration period.[7]

**Special Needs Accommodation:** 
- **16 places are reserved for students with special educational needs (sonderpädagogischer Förderbedarf)**[7]
- If more than 16 students with special needs apply, admission is determined through a lottery process under notarial supervision, with previously requested hardship cases receiving special consideration[7]
- A copy of the current special education assessment (Förderbescheid) is required for students with identified special needs[7]

**Excess Applications:** If the school receives more applications than available spaces, admission follows a specific procedure prioritizing students with special needs, followed by lottery selection for remaining spots.[7]

**Contact Information:**
- Phone: +49 30 9918101
- Fax: +49 30 99283875
- Email: sekretariat@konrad-wachsmann.schule[8]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The school's motto is **"Das Undenkbare denken"** (Think the Unthinkable), inspired by Nikolaus Kopernikus, emphasizing individualized promotion for all students regardless of background, abilities, or performance levels.[3][4] It operates as an **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS) with gymnasialer Oberstufe** since 2011 (previously a Gesamtschule for over 50 years), fostering a learning environment where students with diverse prerequisites, talents, interests, and goals work together in fixed class communities from grade 7, supported by two teachers per class and differentiated courses.[1][4] Core values include social competencies like performance willingness, tolerance, teamwork, reliability, empathy, and curiosity; it prioritizes productive class communities, respectful interactions, inclusion, and preparation for successful school qualifications.[3][4]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded around 1910 as the Evangelische Gemeindeschule für Knaben (Nr. 3) and Mädchen (Nr. 4) in the former Jahnstraße 29 (now Lepsiusstraße), with buildings including a historic Altbau (~1910), Fachräume from the 1960s/1970s, and a 2017 extension (Haus A).[2][3] Located in Berlin-Steglitz (Bezirk Steglitz-Zehlendorf) at Lepsiusstr. 24-28, 12163 Berlin, it shares a campus and new sports hall (completed 2022, with two competition-standard fields) with the neighboring Dunant-Grundschule.[3] Facilities include a modern multifunctional Mensa (2017/2018) with stage for events, NaWi-Fachräume (natural sciences), music and art rooms, workshops, leisure spaces, cafeteria, five computer rooms, beamers, laptops, smartboards, and parking for disabled persons.[3][5] Enrollment grew from 788 students in 2013/14 to 1011 in 2023/24, with 101 teachers.[1] Community efforts include neighborhood cleanups (e.g., Zero-Waste Month) and a Förderverein for support.[7]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Serves **Sekundarschule with gymnasialer Oberstufe** (grades 7-13, including introduction phase in grade 11 and qualification phase with reduced course requirements compared to full Gymnasium).[1][4] Profiles: **mathematisch-naturwissenschaftliches Profil** (math-natural sciences emphasis) and **sprachbetontes Profil** (language-focused); foreign languages: English, French, Latin, Spanish.[1][5] Leistungskurse (advanced courses): Bildende Kunst/Kunst, Biologie, Deutsch, Englisch, Erdkunde, Mathematik, Politische Weltkunde.[1][5] Offers gebundener Ganztagbetrieb (all-day program), schools in inclusion trial, duales Lernen (vocational elements like internships, company visits, application training), and excellent vocational orientation (IHK seal since 2014).[4][5] Graduation pathways include Abitur via gymnasiale Oberstufe, plus MSA and BBR equivalents through integrated secondary structure; second educational pathway and Grundschulteil mentioned.[5]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars (AGs): Chor, Band, Tanz, Segelflug, Schach, Gitarre, Werkstatt, Ballsport, Billard, Schülerzeitung, Mediation, Hausaufgaben, Frankreich-AG (Nouveaux Fous Berlinois project), Astronomie, Informatik.[5][7] Activities include theater workshops (e.g., mask theater for grade 9 DS elective), theater visits (e.g., "Krug.Zerbrochen!" for Deutsch Leistungskurse), grade 11 trips to Brandenburg, open days with department demos, and service learning like neighborhood cleanups.[7] Sports via new hall; arts via music stage, workshops; meals by 3 on-site cooks.[5][7]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Received **„Exzellente Berufliche Orientierung“** seal from IHK since 2014 for vocational guidance efforts.[4] Steady enrollment growth to over 1000 students; no specific awards, rankings, or test outcomes detailed in sources.[1]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **public tuition-free school**, enrollment is open; Anmeldung (registration) via booking Gespräche (advisory interviews) from 30.01.2026; Tag der offenen Tür (open house) held annually (e.g., Jan 10, 2026).[7] First school day for 2025/26 was Sep 8, 2025; general process follows Berlin public secondary school guidelines with no fees.[1][5][7]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Kosmos-Schule emphasizes a collaborative learning environment where **learning, discovery, and community** are central, guided by curiosity, enthusiasm, and the unfolding of individual potentials.[1][4] It fosters a vibrant, diverse learning space where students, teachers, parents, and staff feel welcome and valued, prioritizing **respect, openness, and creativity** in interactions.[1][4] The school aspires to be a "Lern- und Lebensraum" (learning and living space) that supports personal growth, inspiration, and community engagement.[3]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
The Kosmos-Schule (11K15), an **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS) with gymnasialer Oberstufe**, is a new public school founded for the 2024/25 school year as part of the ADKosmos campus, which includes a co-located Gymnasium (11Y12).[1][2][4] The modern school complex at **Allee der Kosmonauten 20-22, 10315 Berlin-Lichtenberg** was constructed between 2021-2024 in a wood-hybrid building method on a 38,000 m² site near Landschaftspark Herzberge, featuring indoor and outdoor facilities.[2][7] Leadership includes **Dr. Michael Albertus** (Schulleiter) and **Carsten Treuber** (stellvertretender Schulleiter); contact: +49 30 2639760000, sekretariat@11K15.schule.berlin.de.[1][5] The school serves the Lichtenberg community, welcoming neighbors, students, parents, and staff.[1][4]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

# Kurt-Schwitters-Schule School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

Kurt-Schwitters-Schule is a **Staatliche Europaschule Portugiesisch-Deutsch** (State European School with German-Portuguese bilingual instruction)[3], characterized by four core values: **vielfältig** (diverse), **kreativ** (creative), **interkulturell** (intercultural), and **praktisch** (practical)[3]. The school emphasizes **individuelles Montessori-Lernen** (individual Montessori-based learning)[3].

The school's mission centers on collaborative development, with its school program created through cooperation between teachers, students, and parents[3]. The school maintains a comprehensive **Leitbild** (mission statement) that anchors the school's self-understanding and cultural foundations[3].

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Location and Structure:**
Kurt-Schwitters-Schule is located in the Pankow district of Berlin at Greifswalder Str. 25, 10405 Berlin[3]. The school operates multiple locations, including a second building at Bötzowstraße 11, 10407 Berlin[3].

**Community Engagement:**
The school fosters active participation through student representation (Schülervertretung), parent representation (Elternvertretung), teacher representation (Lehrervertretung), and a school conference (Schulkonferenz)[3]. The school supports a **Förderverein** (parent support association) and maintains a **Lernmittelfonds** (learning materials fund) to help families access educational resources[3].

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**Grade Levels and Structure:**
The school serves **Sekundarstufe I** (Secondary School I, grades 7-10) and **Sekundarstufe II** (Secondary School II, grades 11-13)[3], offering pathways to the **Abitur** (German university entrance qualification)[3].

**Languages and Bilingual Education:**
As a State European School, Kurt-Schwitters-Schule offers **continuous bilingual instruction in German and Portuguese**[1][3]. The curriculum includes additional languages: English, Spanish, and French[3].

**Core Academic Subjects:**
The school's internal curriculum covers Mathematics, German, English, Portuguese, Spanish, French, Geography, History, Political Education, Ethics, Biology, Chemistry, Physics, and Music[3].

**Profile Subjects (Schwerpunkte):**
- **Art (Kunst)** – a major focus with advanced courses (Leistungskurse) in grades 12-13[3]
- **Music (Musik)**[3]
- **Portuguese (Portugiesisch)**[3]

**Specialized Programs:**
- **WAT (Wirtschaft – Arbeit – Technik)** – Economics, Work, and Technology as an elective subject[3]
- **Darstellendes Spiel** (Performing Arts) in upper secondary[3]
- **Career and Study Orientation (Berufs- & Studienorientierung)** with structured internships in grades 8, 9, and 10[3]
- **Self-Organized Learning (Selbstorganisiertes Lernen)** with methodology support and free work periods[3]

**All-Day School Concept:**
The school operates as a **Ganztagskonzept** (all-day school) with mandatory and elective courses, plus extracurricular activities (AGs)[3].

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**Extracurricular Activities:**
The school maintains diverse student-led initiatives, including **qurt news**, an independent student newspaper (online and cross-media)[3], and **Schwitters-Arts**, a student business that sells student artwork[3]. Drum courses and concerts are also offered[3].

**Arts and Culture:**
The school regularly hosts exhibitions and cultural events, including art exhibitions by advanced course students and a Christmas festival[3].

**Internship Programs:**
Structured work experience is integrated into the curriculum, with internships (Betriebspraktikum) in grades 8, 9, and 10, and transition planning after grade 10[3].

**Safeguarding and Well-being:**
The school implements comprehensive **child protection** (Kinderschutz) measures, including prevention and education in the curriculum, violence prevention, substance abuse prevention, and support for sexual diversity[3]. **School social work** (Schulsozialarbeit) is available[3].

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

The search results do not provide specific information about awards, rankings, or certifications. However, the school's designation as a **Staatliche Europaschule** (State European School) represents official recognition of its bilingual and bicultural educational model[1].

## 6. ADMISSIONS

**Public School Status:**
Kurt-Schwitters-Schule is a **public school** (Öffentlich) with **no tuition fees**[1]. As a public school in Berlin, proximity to the school is an important enrollment criterion, though children from other residential areas may be accepted if capacity exists[1].

**Registration Process:**
Students must be registered either at their assigned school based on residential area or at a school of choice[1]. Required documents for school registration in Berlin include:
- Proof of residence registration[1]
- Child's birth certificate[1]
- Residence visa (not required for EU citizens)[1]
- Documentation of previous performance and accomplishments[1]

**Enrollment for Grade 7:**
The school provides information sessions and open house events for prospective students entering grade 7[3]. An open house (Tag der Offenen Tür) was scheduled for January 15, 2026, from 4-7 PM at the Bötzowstraße location[3].

**Upper Secondary Admission:**
Separate admissions information is available for students entering the upper secondary level (grades 11-13)[3].

**Lateral Entry:**
The school accepts **Quereinstieg** (lateral entry) students at various points[3].

description:
# Kurt-Tucholsky-Oberschule Berlin - School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

The Kurt-Tucholsky-Oberschule is **a school with a democratic mission and musical profile**[1][2]. The institution emphasizes democratic education beginning with classroom councils (Klassenrat) and integrates cultural learning throughout its curriculum[2]. The school's core educational approach combines academic rigor with artistic expression, positioning itself as a center for creative and cultural development in Berlin-Pankow.

The school participates in the Berlin-wide "Hybrid Learning" pilot program since the 2021-22 school year, with all students in grades 7 and 11 automatically enrolled in this initiative[2].

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Location and Structure**
The Kurt-Tucholsky-Oberschule is located at Neumannstraße 9-11, 13189 Berlin in the Pankow district (Pankow Süd)[1][3]. The school is a **five-stream Integrated Secondary School (ISS) with a gymnasium upper level** and partial full-day programming on two days per week[1].

**Student and Staff Population**
The school serves more than 900 students supported by approximately 90 teachers, trainees, social pedagogues, and staff members[1]. As of the 2023-24 school year, enrollment was 960 students with 83 teachers[3].

**Facilities and Technology**
All classrooms are equipped with smartboards and most include document cameras[1]. The school provides three networked computer labs, with laptops and tablets available for classroom instruction[1].

**Cultural Partnerships**
The school maintains partnerships with the **Hamburger Bahnhof - Museum for Contemporary Art Berlin** and the **MAXIM Children's and Youth Cultural Center**[2]. The school participates in the **Erasmus Program** and was certified as a **Reference School for Culture** in 2018 under the "Cultural Agents for Creative Schools Berlin" program[2].

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**Grade Structure and Graduation Pathways**
The school offers all graduation levels from **Berufsbildungsreife (vocational qualification) through Abitur**[2]. The Abitur is offered after 13 years[2]. Students are admitted to grade 7 according to their educational prognosis, while grade 11 primarily accepts students from partner schools[2].

**Languages Offered**
English is the first foreign language and continues from primary school[1]. A second foreign language is not mandatory at an ISS but can be selected as an elective. Students can begin **French or Latin in grades 7 and 11, and Russian in grade 9 or 11**[1]. A second foreign language is required for Abitur admission[1].

**Bilingual Program**
The school offers **bilingual instruction in English** beginning in grade 7[2]. Students in grade 7 receive an additional English hour to prepare for bilingual learning[1]. Grade 8 students take a mandatory preparatory course in either history or geography and biology to qualify for the bilingual program in grades 9-10[2]. This structure allows students to test the bilingual option before making a binding commitment[2].

**Musical and Artistic Profile**
The curriculum features **strengthened art and music instruction in secondary level I**, extensive elective courses in cultural education (art, music, dramatic arts, and cultural education), advanced courses in art and music, and basic courses in art, music, theater, and artistic photography[2]. The school offers project-based learning in cultural education through its open full-day program[2].

**Additional Academic Areas**
The curriculum integrates **dual learning, career and study orientation across all grades**, alongside language, sports, and natural sciences[2].

**Advanced Courses (Leistungskurse)**
Available advanced courses include Bildende Kunst/Kunst (Visual Art), Biologie (Biology), Chemie (Chemistry), Deutsch (German), Englisch (English), Erdkunde (Geography), Geschichte (History), Mathematik (Mathematics), Musik (Music), and Physik (Physics)[3].

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**Cultural and Artistic Activities**
Project weeks, theatrical performances, school band concerts, exhibitions, and a summer festival characterize the cultural community life[2]. Professional artists support the school's diverse offerings[2].

**Extracurricular Engagement**
The school operates an open full-day program with project-based learning opportunities in cultural education[2]. Students participate in democratic governance through classroom councils beginning in the lower grades[2].

**Practical Learning**
The school emphasizes dual learning and career/study orientation integrated throughout all grade levels[2].

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

The school was **certified as a Reference School for Culture in 2018** as part of the "Cultural Agents for Creative Schools Berlin" program[2]. The school participates in multiple **Erasmus Program projects**[2].

The school has demonstrated sustained growth, with enrollment increasing from 765 students in 2011-12 to 960 students in 2023-24[3].

## 6. ADMISSIONS

**Enrollment Process**
The school admits 5 classes with 26 students each (130 students total) per school year[1]. In years of high demand, placement is determined by a selection process[1].

**Grade 7 Admission**
Students are admitted to grade 7 according to their educational prognosis (Förderprognose)[2].

**Grade 11 Admission**
Grade 11 primarily accepts students from partner schools[2].

**Hybrid Learning Program**
All students entering grades 7 and 11 automatically participate in the Berlin-wide "Hybrid Learning" pilot program as part of their enrollment[2].

description:
# Martin-Buber-Oberschule School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

The Martin-Buber-Oberschule operates under the motto **"Schule der Möglichkeiten"** (School of Possibilities), with a core philosophy centered on recognizing and developing individual student strengths while providing targeted support for areas of need[1]. The school's pedagogical approach is built on the conviction that students with varying abilities succeed academically when learning in an environment with high performance expectations[1].

The school implements **individualized instruction** through inner and outer differentiation, tailored support modules for both high-achieving and struggling students, homework assistance, and peer tutoring programs ("Schüler helfen Schülern")[1]. A distinctive feature is the absence of probationary periods and grade retention, reflecting the belief that "fear makes people stupid"[3].

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Location and Campus**

Martin-Buber-Oberschule is located in the Falkenhagener Feld area of Berlin's Spandau district[1][2]. The school is part of the **"Campus im Spektefeld,"** a shared educational campus that includes both the secondary school and the Grundschule im Beerwinkel (primary school)[6]. The campus consists of eight building sections constructed in the 1960s arranged in a cross formation across the grounds[6].

**School Structure**

The school is a **six-form entry integrated secondary school** (sechszügig) with a gymnasial upper school[6]. Current enrollment stands at approximately 1,043-1,100 students across 39 classes, with an average class size of 28.2 students[1][2].

**Facilities and Resources**

The school offers comprehensive facilities including four computer labs, fitness room, school garden, school radio, and accessible spaces for wheelchair users (accessible via ramp or ground level)[5]. The school provides midday meal service[5].

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**Grade Structure and Pathways**

The school serves students from grade 7 through grade 13, offering the **Abitur after either 12 or 13 years**[3]. Students can pursue the **Mittlerer Schulabschluss (MSA)** or continue to the Abitur through the gymnasial upper school.

**Language Program**

- **First Foreign Language:** English (mandatory)[2]
- **Second Foreign Language:** French, Spanish, or Latin[2]
- **Third Foreign Language:** Latin, French, or Spanish[2]

The school offers a **language profile class in each year group** where students can study French, Spanish, and Latin alongside English[1]. Language exchange trips for modern foreign languages (French, Spanish, English) occur in the 9th grade[8].

**Profile Classes and Specializations**

Students apply to the school based on their interests and existing competencies in one of **seven profile areas**[1][3]:

1. **Art** (künstlerisch)
2. **Technology and Mathematics** (technisch-mathematisch)
3. **Music** (musikalisch)
4. **Sport** (sportlich)
5. **Natural Sciences** (naturwissenschaftlich)
6. **Languages** (Sprachen)
7. **Theater** (Darstellendes Spiel)

Profile classes are limited to a maximum of 26 students[3]. Over 70% of students enter with a gymnasium recommendation[3].

**Academic Differentiation**

Performance differentiation begins in grade 7 or 9 depending on the subject, with students divided into E-courses (advanced) and G-courses (standard)[3]. The school offers an **E+ level** specifically designed to prepare high-performing students for the demands of the upper school[3].

**Advanced Courses (Leistungskurse)**

Available advanced courses include: Art, Biology, Chemistry, German, English, Geography, French, History, Mathematics, Music, Physics, and Political Education[5].

**Elective Subjects (Wahlpflichtfächer)**

Students can choose from: Art, Music, Spanish/French, Mathematics, Natural Sciences, Economics-Work-Technology (WAT), Sport, and Theater[1][5].

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**Extracurricular Activities**

The school offers numerous working groups (AGs) including sports AGs and specialized modules[1]. Recent activities include:

- **Duke of Edinburgh's International Award** program (newly implemented)[7]
- **Language trips** to French, Spanish, and English-speaking countries[8]
- **Media and journalism projects** (e.g., visits to the taz newspaper)[8]
- **Music performances** (e.g., music advanced course concerts)[8]
- **Art exhibitions and studio visits**[8]
- **First Aid modules** with community service components[8]
- **Block instruction weeks** featuring specialized courses like "Text Ton Technik" (audio production with Ableton software)[7]

**Talent Development**

The school provides **gifted and talented modules** (Begabungsmodule) as part of its enrichment offerings[1]. These programs specifically support highly motivated and exceptionally able students through differentiated instruction and specialized opportunities[1].

**Social Support**

The school employs school social workers (Schulsozialarbeit) and maintains partnerships with external organizations including the Society for Sports and Youth Social Work (GSJ), Max Delbrück Center for Molecular Medicine, and Youth and Addiction Counseling Spandau[8].

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

**Academic Results**

The school reports exceptional outcomes: **98% of all students achieve the Mittlerer Schulabschluss (MSA)**, with **87% leaving the school with the Abitur**[1]. This success rate is attributed to the school's mixed-ability learning environment where stronger students support weaker peers[1].

**Recognitions and Certifications**

- **Duke of Edinburgh's International Award** school[7]
- **"Exzellente digitale Schule"** (Excellent Digital School) designation[1]
- **Samartschule** (federal award for digital excellence)[1]
- **Showcase School** for digital initiatives[1]

## 6. ADMISSIONS

**Admission Process**

Admission to Martin-Buber-Oberschule follows a **selection process based on student interests and aptitudes** (Auswahlverfahren nach Neigung), with **no lottery system**[2]. Students apply based on their demonstrated interests and competencies in one of the seven profile areas[1][3].

**Enrollment Requirements**

- Students must be transitioning from primary school (grade 6)
- Application requires indication of preferred profile class
- Virtual counseling sessions are available to discuss the admission process and profile options[9]
- Admission is competitive based on profile fit rather than academic achievement alone

**Key Dates**

The school publishes an annual admission schedule (Terminplan für das Aufnahmeverfahren 7) with specific deadlines for applications and counseling sessions[9].

**Public School Status**

As a public school (öffentliche Schule), Martin-Buber-Oberschule is **tuition-free** and coeducational[1][2]. It operates as a partially bound full-day school (teilgebundene Ganztagsschule)[1].

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Max-von-Laue-Oberschule is an **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS) with gymnasialer Oberstufe**, emphasizing individualized education that allows students to achieve qualifications matching their talents and goals, from basic to advanced levels including an 11th grade (E-Phase) as a pathway to Abitur at a partner gymnasium.[5][1] It promotes a dynamic approach by retaining proven methods while integrating innovations, with profiles in **mathematisch-naturwissenschaftlich** (math-science), **informationstechnik**, **sprachlich-künstlerisch** (language-artistic), and as a **Staatliche Europa-Schule Berlin (SESB)** offering a German-Greek branch from grades 7-10.[3][4][6][9] Core values include practical, hands-on learning (e.g., Weinbau project), global education, social orientation (e.g., SOL-Stunden for charity), and support via school social workers for conflict resolution.[1][3][5]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in **1912** as seminar exercise classes evolving into a nine-grade girls' mittelschule in Groß-Lichterfelde, it restructured from Realschule to ISS around 2010-2016 with a €7.5 million expansion including new buildings, specialized rooms, sports field, Weingarten with Weinlabor, and digitalization (kreidefrei).[1][2] Located in **Berlin-Steglitz-Zehlendorf (Lichterfelde)** at Dürerstr. 27, 12203 Berlin, it serves ~510 students (2022/23) in a community-focused environment with ganztagsbetrieb (full-day operation), social workers per class, and initiatives like Begabtes Berlin and Leistung macht Schule for gifted students.[1][2][4] Facilities include computer rooms, smartboards, Lehrküche, Schülercafé, textilwerkstatt, werkstätten, and modern sports areas.[1][2][4]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Serves **secondary levels** (likely grades 7-10/11) as ISS with gymnasialer Oberstufe, offering **Abitur pathway** via 11th grade and transfer to Hermann-Ehlers-Gymnasium, plus MSA and options for BBR/berufliche Ausbildung.[1][5] **Languages**: Englisch (1st), Französisch/Spanisch (2nd/3rd), Griechisch (SESB Deutsch-Griechisch branch).[1][4][5][6] **Subjects** include Berufs- und Studienorientierung, Arbeitslehre, Ethik, Geografie, Geschichte, Informatik, Kunst, Mathematik, Musik, Politische Bildung, Psychologie, Sport, Theater/Literatur, WAT.[1] Special programs: mathematisch-naturwissenschaftlich (e.g., Weinbau), Informationstechnik, duales Lernen with Praxislerngruppen, inner/outer differentiation in Deutsch/Mathe/Englisch from grade 7, zusatzmodule, ge bundener Ganztagsbetrieb.[1][4][5][6][7] Participation in Zweiter Bildungsweg and Grundschulteil elements noted.[4]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
**Extracurriculars and excursions** feature prominently: DLR visit (Wirtschaftsverkehr/CO2), Berlin Zoo (Klimawandel), Plenarsitzung (Haushaltsgesetz with chancellor), Wittenberg Reformationstag, Hawaii guest speaker on culture/school system.[scraped] **AGs**: TAT-AG (global learning "sprechender globale Schrank"), Diversity-AG, Kreatives Schreiben, Weinbau, Sozial-AG.[1][3] Arts/culture via Kunst/Musik/Theater, SOL-Stunden (charity Weihnachtspäckchen), global projects.[scraped][3] Sports via new facilities; exchanges/international via SESB and guest programs.[5][scraped]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific awards or rankings detailed; growth from 407 students (2011/12) to 487 (2021/22) reflects expansion.[6] Part of **Begabtes Berlin** (gifted promotion) and **Leistung macht Schule** (federal-state excellence initiative).[1] Modernized infrastructure (2010-2016) and leadership under Schulleiterin **Irina Bothmann** (since 2015) advanced Oberstufe and profiles.[2][4][7] Active in global learning projects like "globaler Schrank."[3]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **public tuition-free school**, enrollment prioritizes **Förderprognose (average grade from primary recommendation)** in case of overdemand.[1][4][7] Open Day: **January 16, 2026, 16-19 Uhr** with brochure and events.[scraped] Contact: sekretariat@mvl.schule.berlin.de, Tel 030-902991747.[scraped]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The May-Ayim-Schule is an **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** focused on **project-oriented learning** (projektorientiertes Lernen), emphasizing hands-on, discovery-based education where students learn "on eye level" (Lernen auf Augenhöhe) and "self-discover" through practical projects.[1][2][3] Core values include qualified teachers (all with Bundesjugendspiele certificates), diverse travel and excursions, and preparing students for real-world challenges via interdisciplinary projects like healthy nutrition, craftsmanship, and career exploration.[1][4]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in the school year **2022/23** as a new ISS in Berlin-Lichtenberg (Bezirk Lichtenberg), the school originated from a 2021 building renovation of a former DDR-era Polytechnische Oberschule site, with delays pushing occupancy to June 2023; it temporarily used another school during construction.[2][3] Located at **Storkower Str. 209B, 10369 Berlin (Fennpfuhl)**, it serves the local community with modern facilities including a renovated steel-concrete structure, new ventilation, extension for cafeteria (Mensa), auditorium (Aula), administration, and workshops; a virtual tour is available (in English).[1][3][5][8] Leadership: Dr. Alexander Westphal (kommissarisch).[5]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Serves **grades 7-10** with **English** as the first foreign language; German curriculum emphasizes individualized reading, writing, speaking, and critical thinking, assessed via LAL 7 and VERA 8.[2][5][6] Project-oriented focus includes: grade 7 (healthy, climate-friendly nutrition, economics, travel prep); grade 8 (handcrafts like sewing, sawing); grades 9-10 (praktika, career projects with Jugendberufsagentur).[4][6] Graduation pathways: **BBR** (end of 9th), **eBBR, MSA, MSA GO, BOA** (end of 10th).[6] Practice for 9th/10th grade exams is offered.[1]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Rich extracurriculars emphasize **travel and projects**: ski trips (with Vincent-van-Gogh-Schule), Alpenüberquerung, London/Dubai/Turkey/NY trips (listed), class trips (Fahrtenkonzept), excursions (fire station, Charité museum, Humboldt-Forum, Sana Klinikum Care Day).[1] Arts/sports: music workshops (Matondo Castlo, Mete Eksi project), art (design, collages, monster plushies, Alpen exhibition), sports (Bundesjugendspiele, Talente Check Berlin, bike challenges), school dog Rudi.[1] Other: girls' tech congress, grave maintenance group, Schulsanitätsdienst, potato action against food waste.[1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Won the **Mete Ekşi Preis 2025** for "Laut.Mut.May" project.[1] Feierliche Eröffnung in 2023; active in challenges like Alpenüberquerung and Stadt-Land-Meer.[1][3] No rankings or broad outcome data in content, but emphasizes project successes and community events like Tag der offenen Tür.[1][7]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
**Public tuition-free** school; open enrollment (Anmeldung) for 2026/27 school year encouraged, with Schnuppertage (trial days) for 6th graders, Tag der offenen Tür (e.g., Jan 13, 2026, 16:00), and virtual tour.[1][7] Parents invited to register children; info from school leadership includes books lists and parent-teacher conferences.[1][7]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Merian-Schule is named after the naturalist and artist Maria Sibylla Merian (1647-1717), reflecting a commitment to environmental awareness and artistic education.[1] It aims to integrate aesthetic education with forward-looking, natural science-based training, tested in practical contexts.[1]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 1962 as the Polytechnische Oberschule "Edwin Hoernle," the school evolved through reforms: in 1991, it became a Gesamtschule with gymnasialer Oberstufe; renamed Merian-Oberschule in 1992; and redesignated Merian-Schule in 2008 due to Berlin's school reform.[1] It serves approximately 950 students in Berlin's Treptow-Köpenick district with partly bound all-day operations.[4] Facilities include modernized natural science rooms (1991), a second site at Mittelheide 49 from 2000, and a multifunctional sports facility inaugurated in 2010 at the main Hoernlestr. 80 location.[1]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As an Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS) with gymnasialer Oberstufe, it offers integrated secondary education from lower to upper secondary levels, including pathways toward Abitur.[1][4]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
No specific extracurriculars, sports, arts, or exchanges are detailed in the available content beyond the new sports facility supporting physical activities.[1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
The school is recognized for climate and environmental protection engagement in Treptow-Köpenick.[4] No awards, rankings, or certifications are specified.

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a public (öffentlich) tuition-free school, enrollment follows Berlin's standard public school processes with no tuition fees; children turning six by December 31 must attend, with early entry possible for those turning six by March 31 upon guardian application.[5]

The school's motto is **"Brücken bauen, Horizonte erweitern - deine Schule im Herzen Berlins"** (Building bridges, expanding horizons - your school in the heart of Berlin), emphasizing an inclusive environment where every student feels welcome and contributes.[1] The Leitbild (mission statement, from 01/2019) outlines three core pillars: (1) valuing individual abilities and creating an **inclusive school** with tailored support and full-day programs; (2) promoting **tolerance, diversity, and anti-racism** through projects on acceptance, including gender/sexual identity, human rights, anti-mobbing days, and democratic values; (3) leveraging **digitalization** for modern learning conditions amid technological change.[1]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Named after Mildred Harnack, a historical figure associated with resistance against National Socialism (taught English literature post-1933 dismissal).[4][5] Located in **Berlin-Lichtenberg district** at Schulze-Boysen-Str. 12, 10365 Berlin, as a public, tuition-free **integrated secondary school (ISS)** with upper secondary level.[1] No specific founding date or facilities details provided; community focus includes diverse backgrounds (teachers, students, parents, staff) and cooperation partners for career orientation.[1]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
**Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** with gymnasialer Oberstufe and **Staatliche Europa-Schule Berlin (Russian/German bilingual)**, offering continuous bilingual instruction.[1][2] Covers secondary levels with flexible graduation pathways based on performance: **Abitur** (after 12/13 years), **Mittlerer Schulabschluss (MSA)** (after 10th grade), **erweiterte Berufsbildungsreife** (after 10th), **Berufsbildungsreife (BBR)** (after 9th).[1] Includes **Berufs- und Studienorientierung (BSO)** for career/study guidance (apprenticeships, vocational schools, voluntary service).[1] Additional profiles like German-Spanish noted externally.[2]

Recent activities highlight arts (winter art despite storm chaos, poetry slam, recitation competition), human rights (MOVE IT! Filmfestival, memorial event on Nov 9, 2025), journalism (students as youth reporters), and politics (Politik-LK with Ines Schwerdtner).[1] Upcoming info event on "Übergang zur Sek I" (Jan 20, 2026, 18:00-20:00).[1] Emphasizes projects for tolerance, diversity, and full-day individualized offers.[1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No awards, rankings, or specific outcomes mentioned in the content. Recognized as **Staatliche Europa-Schule** for bilingual (Russian/German) program.[1][3]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
Public **Öffentlich** school (tuition-free); features **Anmeldung** (enrollment) section on website. Info event for transition to Sek I on Jan 20, 2026 (18:00-20:00); books list and catering available.[1]
