description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Eckener-Gymnasium positions itself as a modern **MINT-Gymnasium** (focusing on mathematics, IT, natural sciences, and technology) in a historic building, emphasizing a supportive school climate shaped by an open-minded faculty and active student involvement.[1][2][3] It offers diverse profiles including the altsprachlicher Zweig (classical languages from grade 5), MINT-Zweig (from grade 5 or 7), and Zweig „Kultur- und Gesellschaft“ (from grade 7), alongside broad project and extracurricular offerings to foster competencies in STEM and cultural areas.[1][2][3]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in **1907** as an Oberrealschule by the then-independent Mariendorf community, the school moved in 1911 to its current historic building at Kaiserstr. 17-21 (corner Rathausstraße) in Berlin-Mariendorf, designed by architects Heinrich Reinhardt and Georg Süßenguth, featuring an aula with an organ by Albert Kienscherf.[1][2][3][6] It serves approximately **740-750 students** from grades 5/7 to Abitur in the Tempelhof-Schöneberg district, with a community-oriented environment including partnerships, a friends' circle (Freundeskreis) founded in 1994 from earlier parent initiatives, and nearby facilities like a sports hall.[1][3][5] The school promotes a friendly climate through its committed staff and student activities.[3]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a **grundständiges Gymnasium**, it offers education from **grade 5 (two classes per yeargang) or grade 7 (two classes)** leading to **Abitur**, with English as the first foreign language for all.[1][2][3] Profiles include: altsprachlicher Zweig (Latin from grade 5, French from grade 7); MINT-Zweig (STEM focus from grade 5/7, with a third 7m3 class starting 2025/26 replacing a prior band class); Kultur- und Gesellschaft from grade 7; and a former music-focused band class in cooperation with Musikschule Tempelhof-Schöneberg (instrumental training plus regular music).[1][2] No specific mentions of MSA or BBR pathways.[1][2]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in extensive **extracurriculars** (Arbeitsgemeinschaften/AGs) such as Schulgarten, basketball, football, Jugend forscht, orchestra, robotics, school newspaper, bands, musicals (every two years), theater, choir, and performances.[1][3] Events include author readings (e.g., Anna Ruhe, 2025), reading competitions, museum visits (e.g., C/O Berlin photography), sports trips (SV-Fahrt), music trips, central hiking days, math olympiads, Känguru competition, and book fairs.[2] Participation in Mathe-Olympiade (grades 5-13) and music ensembles open to all.[1][2]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Awarded **MINT-freundliche Schule** for the third consecutive time, recognized in a ceremony with officials like Staatssekretär Torsten Kühne and Prof. Christoph Meinel.[2] Hosts stages of the 65th Mathematik-Olympiade and internal reading competitions with prizes like author visits; strong in music/theater productions and STEM projects like Jugend forscht.[1][2][3]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **tuition-free public school**, admission for 60% of grade 7 places prioritizes the **average grade of the Förderprognose** (per Sek I-VO Berlin §6).[2] Open houses include Tag der offenen Tür on **15.01.2026 (16-19 Uhr)**, school flyer, and enrollment info for 2026/27 (MINT grade 5 selection noted).[2] Contact: Sekretariat (Mon-Fri 8-14 Uhr, tel. 030-90277-74-48, info@eckener-gymnasium.de); illness reports 7:15-8:30 Uhr.[2]

description:
# Emmy-Noether-Gymnasium School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

The Emmy-Noether-Gymnasium is committed to fostering **self-directed, globally-minded, and creative student personalities** through a combination of high academic standards and personal responsibility[1]. The school emphasizes **comprehensive general education** through interdisciplinary and project-based instruction, ensuring that acquired knowledge is networked and applicable[1].

Core values include:
- **Respectful interaction, consideration, helpfulness, and tolerance** as fundamental to daily school life[1]
- **Sustainable development awareness**, with a focus on strengthening students' competencies to address societal challenges and environmental questions[1]
- **Violence-free coexistence**, reflected in the school's designation as "Schule ohne Rassismus – Schule mit Courage" (School Without Racism – School with Courage)[1]
- **Continuous quality development** of instruction, responsible education, and vibrant school life[1]

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Founding and Development**

The Emmy-Noether-Gymnasium was established in **2006 through the merger of two secondary schools: "Linus Pauling" and "Nelly Sachs"**[1][2]. The school is named after mathematician Emmy Noether[2]. Since 2015, it has been designated as one of five Berlin gymnasiums with special pedagogical emphasis on a **natural sciences profile**[1].

**Location and District**

- **Address:** Pablo-Neruda-Straße 6-7, 12559 Berlin (Köpenick)[1][3]
- **District:** Treptow-Köpenick[1]
- **Enrollment:** Approximately 798 students with 74 teachers[3]

**Facilities and Infrastructure**

The school is well-equipped with[4]:
- 120 m² library
- Four computer rooms
- Internet café
- Photography lab
- Ceramic workshop
- Teaching kitchen
- Aula (assembly hall)
- Accessible facilities including wheelchair-accessible elevator and restrooms
- Parking for people with disabilities
- Covered schoolyard
- Two outdoor green classrooms

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**Graduation Pathway**

Students acquire the **Abitur (German university entrance qualification) after 12 years of schooling** in both regular and profile classes[1].

**Languages Offered**

- **First foreign language options:** English/French (S1) or English/Spanish (S4)[3][4]
- **Third foreign languages:** Latin or Spanish[3][4]

**Advanced Courses (Leistungskurse)**

Students can choose from nine advanced courses[2]:
- Deutsch (German)
- Englisch (English)
- Französisch (French)
- Geschichte (History)
- Geographie (Geography)
- Mathematik (Mathematics)
- Physik (Physics)
- Biologie (Biology)
- Chemie (Chemistry)

**Upper-Level Electives (Grundkurse)**

Additional courses available in the upper school include[2]:
- Informatik (Computer Science)
- Darstellendes Spiel (Drama)
- Ensemblemusik (Ensemble Music)
- COMENIUS course on "Challenges of Sustainability"
- English supplementary course
- Politikwissenschaften (Political Science)
- Philosophie (Philosophy)
- Sporttheorie (Sport Theory)
- Windsurfing course
- Alpine skiing course

**Natural Sciences Profile Program**

Beginning in **grade 5 and grade 7**, scientifically-interested and gifted students receive targeted support through a modified curriculum with **additional instruction in mathematics and natural sciences**[1]. This specialized track provides enhanced opportunities for STEM-focused learners.

**Pedagogical Approach**

The school implements "Pädagogische Schulentwicklung" (Pedagogical School Development), which includes[2]:
- Multi-day training units for systematic development of methodological competence
- Team work and communication skills development
- Preparation for the Mittlerer Schulabschluss (MSA – intermediate school certificate)

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**Working Groups and Extracurriculars**

The school offers diverse activities across academic, artistic, and athletic domains[4]:

**Arts and Culture:**
- Drama/Theater
- Choir
- Photography
- Ceramic workshop
- Event planning

**Science and Environment:**
- Chemistry
- Zoo/Animal care
- Reptile care (exotic animals including chameleons, iguanas, and stick insects)
- Sustainability and energy
- Garden/Horticulture

**Languages:**
- Russian

**Sports:**
- Football
- Basketball
- Tennis
- Volleyball
- Badminton/Table tennis
- Diving/Underwater activities
- Yoga and relaxation

**Special Initiatives:**
- "School Without Racism – School with Courage" project
- Homepage/IT projects
- Student-led projects

**Field Experiences**

The school organizes **student course trips**, including international experiences such as trips to Greece[1].

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

**MINT Certification**

The school was recognized as a **"MINT-freundliche Schule" (MINT-friendly School) on November 15, 2023**, under the patronage of the Kultusministerkonferenz (KMK), with certification valid for three years[1].

**Environmental Recognition**

The school received the **Biodiversity Prize from Berlin Zoo**, reflecting its commitment to environmental education and sustainability[1].

**Academic Performance**

Historical Abitur results demonstrate consistent achievement[3]:

| Year | Average Grade | Success Rate |
|------|---|---|
| 2013 | 2.25 | 98% |
| 2014 | 2.24 | 93% |
| 2015 | 2.31 | 98% |
| 2016 | 2.29 | 96% |
| 2017 | 2.42 | 97% |

**Media Recognition**

The school received positive coverage in the Tagesspiegel (August 23, 2023), highlighting its practical orientation and student responsibility programs, particularly noting the reptile care activities as an example of hands-on learning[1].

## 6. ADMISSIONS

As a **public school (öffentlich)**, the Emmy-Noether-Gymnasium is **tuition-free** and operates under Berlin's public education system. Specific admission details are referenced on the school's website under "Informationen zur Anmeldung" (Admission Information)[1], though detailed requirements are not provided in the available content.

Students interested in the **natural sciences profile program** beginning in grade 5 or 7 should demonstrate interest and aptitude in STEM subjects, as the school provides targeted support for scientifically-gifted students[1].

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The school embodies a peaceful, open Europe in daily life, inspired by Bertha von Suttner, who advocated for understanding, humanity, and education as paths to peace.[5] It emphasizes a European and international orientation through partnerships with ten schools in Europe and China, bilingual offerings, and programs like "Europa & die Welt," begabungsförderung (gifted education), medienkompetenzrahmen (media literacy framework), and bilingualer Unterricht (bilingual instruction).[1][4][5] Core values include fostering gifted students via the "Leistung macht Schule – LemaS" pilot (2018–2023, now a multiplier school), self-directed learning, career orientation (BSO), and MINT (STEM) focus.[1][3]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded on April 22, 1908, as a private Höhere Schule für Mädchen in Reinickendorf, it became state-run Lyceum Reinickendorf in 1913, was renamed Bertha-von-Suttner-Oberschule in 1946/47, admitted boys in 1952 under coeducation, and moved to Reginhardstr. 172 in 1960 (expanded 1981).[4] Renamed Europäisches Gymnasium Bertha-von-Suttner in 2011 to highlight European unity.[4] Community includes schulleitung, kollegium, eltern/GEV, Verein der Freunde, and partners/sponsors; facilities feature two sport halls, modern fachräume, mensa, cafeteria, aula with stage/lighting, schülerbibliothek, mediothek, makerspace, schließfächer, two school buildings on a large campus with sportplatz, three computer rooms, and schulhof.[3][6] Enrollment: ~1182 students, ~106 teachers (recent data).[2]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a Gymnasium (grades 5–13, leading to Abitur), it offers altsprachliche Züge (Latin from grade 5, Greek from 7), neusprachliche Züge (from 7), bilingual Deutsch/Englisch, erweitertes Fremdsprachenangebot (English/Französisch S1, English/Latein S2, 3rd/4th languages: Greek, French, Latin, Spanish, Chinese), and subjects like MU-KU-DS (music/art/drama), NW-MA-IN (sciences/math/IT/MINT), GE-PW-EK (history/geography/ethics/philosophy/religion/sport).[1][2][3][5][6] Upper school (Oberstufe) includes broad Leistungskurse (nearly all combinations), Grundkurse (Studium&Beruf/Schülerfirma, Darstellendes Spiel, Cambridge/DELF certs, bilingual PW), Seminarkurse, laufbahnplanung, umwahlen, Abitur results/prüfungen.[5][6][7] Offene Ganztagbetreuung (OGB), schulinternes curriculum, unterrichtszeiten, werkstattwoche.[5]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars include arbeitsgemeinschaften (AGs) in arts/music (theater from grade 5, Big Band, choir, orchestra), history/politics (Gedenk-arbeit), sports (football, judo, wandertag, bewegte Pause), sciences (math, bio/chemie, umwelt, SimTech-Wettbewerb), and others (homepage, Hebräisch-AG).[3][5][6] Exchanges: Erasmus+ in Athen, auslandsaufenthalten, international partners.[1][5] Other: lernstudio, Rest&Play/Rest&Read, makerspace, bibliothek/bücherfonds, career day, jugend debattiert, kindergartenkunstakademie, kooperation with firms/universities, kinderKunstAkademie.[1][5][6]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
**MINT-freundliche Schule** (5th time, Nov 14, 2025; valid 3 years, KMK patronage; since 2013).[3][5] CertiLingua-Schule, Qualitätssiegel für Exzellente Berufliche Orientierung (since 2014).[1][4] LemaS pilot/multiplier school (2018–2027).[1] Wins: Student Julia R. Landes- und Bundeswettbewerb Philosophischer Essay (Leistungskurs Philosophie); SimTech-Wettbewerb Bundesfinale qualifikation; Jugend debattiert, school contests.[5] Schulinspektion 2019, wettbewerbe.[5] Abitur pathways with strong results tracking.[5]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
Public tuition-free Gymnasium; anmeldung for grades 5/7 via online beratungsgespräche (from Dec 6, 2025, 18:00) and Tage der offenen Tür (e.g., Jan 17, 2026, 10:00–13:00 for new 5./7. Klassen).[5] Fourzügig (one 5th, three 7th classes); open to local district via standard Berlin public process.[3]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Evangelische Schule Frohnau emphasizes a continuous educational pathway from primary school through gymnasium, rooted in evangelical Christian values, fostering a familial atmosphere, responsibility for others, and an open worldview. Evangelical religious education is a regular subject, with traditions of Bible interpretation promoting love, freedom, and community life; the school aims to make students "weltkundig" (worldly aware) by understanding global realities, competing values, and Christian life models.[1][2][4][6] Key elements include morning circles, class councils, devotions, services, and festivals as integral to school life, alongside active anti-mobbing efforts through the "Faire Schule" initiative and social internships.[4]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in the tradition of 1948 evangelical schools emerging from the Confessing Church's resistance to National Socialism, the school is owned by the Evangelische Schulstiftung EKBO (Evangelische Kirche Berlin-Brandenburg-schlesische Oberlausitz) and serves over 700 students in a green, idyllic setting in Berlin-Frohnau, Reinickendorf district.[1][2][3][4][6] It operates as a state-recognized substitute school (Ersatzschule) with qualifications equivalent to public schools, led by Christine Behnken, featuring two parallel classes per year (zweizügig) from grades 1-12 for stability.[2][3][4][7] Facilities include accessibility features like parking for disabled persons, wheelchair-accessible elevator and WC, and after-school care (Hort); it supports a democratic culture with conflict mediators, active student representation, and ecological initiatives like a bicycle check day.[1][7]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The school offers a continuous track from Grundschule (grades 1-4, or up to 6 in some structures) to Gymnasium (up to grade 12/Abitur), with two streams per yeargang, year-group learning in early grades, and fachlehrkraft principle (subject teachers) from grades 5-6.[4][5][7][9] Languages include English from grade 1, optional strings from grade 2, and third languages French or Latin; elective profiles like S1 English/French, S2 English/Latin.[5][7] Evangelical religion is mandatory; upper levels include gymnasiale Oberstufe leading to Abitur, with no mention of MSA or BBR pathways.[2][4][7]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in diverse extracurriculars including Arbeitsgemeinschaften (AGs) like strings class, junior orchestra, choir, dance, theater, handball, Champions-Runner, upcycling, and sports courses; grade 5-6 projects in brass, choir, art, and performing arts culminate in joint performances.[1][4][5] Additional experiences feature class trips (grades 3 and 6), school partnerships, social internships, Klassenrat, morning circles, festivals, and anti-mobbing programs; after-school Hort couples support hours with play and learning.[4][5]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
The school has received the Fobizz-Siegel (renewed for 2026) for exceptional commitment to digital education and teacher training.[4] It promotes a democratic school culture with high student satisfaction, conflict mediators, active student council supported by communication seminars, ecological responsibility (e.g., Umweltamt, Fahrradcheck-Tag), and global learning initiatives.[1]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
Admissions for grade 1 start after summer holidays of the prior year (e.g., 9-19 September 2025, 8-12 Uhr), with phone appointments for interviews; grades 5/7 after winter holidays.[4] Required documents: application form, last two report cards (original/copy), birth certificate copy, measles vaccination proof (original), baptism certificate if applicable, and school assessment for grades 5/7; waitlist requires updated certificates by 15 September.[4] As a private school in free sponsorship, monthly tuition (Schulgeld) is income-based per the EKBO Schulstiftung table (details on carrier's site); no exact EUR amounts listed on pages, but PDFs may contain forms (not fee-related per metadata).[4] No specifics on registration, material, meal, or after-school fees; contact Lisa Gutjahr for queries.[4] Newsletter and parent contributions mentioned, but no scholarship details.[4]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Evangelische Schule Köpenick (ESK) is committed to a **Christian worldview** that values, accepts, and respects the individuality of each person, providing education and upbringing grounded in the Gospel.[1][3] It emphasizes being **engaged, social, and creative**, fostering holistic learning through "heart and hand" – thinking, feeling, and acting – with high value on creativity, curiosity about other cultures, solidarity, empathy, tolerance, and environmental stewardship.[1][3] The school's leitbild promotes Christian life via worship, rituals, and festivals; individualized learning paths; preparation for self-determined lives; and close parent partnerships.[3]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in **2005** by an initiative of engaged parents, ESK is a state-recognized substitute school (Ersatzschule) owned by the **Schulstiftung der Evangelischen Kirche Berlin-Brandenburg-schlesische Oberlausitz (EKBO)**, led by Michael Tiedje since 2017.[1][2][3] Located at **Grüne Trift 169, 12557 Berlin** in the quiet Wendenschloss area of Treptow-Köpenick district, between the Dahme, Spree rivers, and Müggelberge, it serves **611 students** (2021/2022) with **61 teachers**.[1][2][3] The campus features DDR-era buildings (1953-1963), including a three-story main building with basement, art rooms in a northern wing, and a gym hall; a digital school tour is available.[1][2]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a **Gymnasium** (grades 5-12/13), ESK follows Berlin's state curricula and frameworks, offering a musisch-künstlerisches (arts-focused) profile with subjects including **Religion, Deutsch, Darstellendes Spiel, Mathematik, Chemie, Biologie, Physik, Musik, Kunst, Spanisch, Französisch, Latein, Englisch, Geschichte/Politik, Sport, Geografie, Informatik/ITG**.[1][6][9] Languages: English/Französisch from S1, Englisch/Spanisch from S4, Latein as 3rd.[6] Upper school (Oberstufe) leads to **full Abitur** after 12 years, with Leistungskurse options like Deutsch, Englisch, Französisch, Spanisch, Mathematik, Biologie, Physik, Kunst, Musik, etc.[4] Leistungsbewertung applies grades 5-10; open to evangelical primary transitions and others.[1][3][4]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
ESK promotes holistic, creative learning with arts emphasis (kunstbetontes and musikbetontes Profil), including Darstellendes Spiel, Musik, Kunst.[1][3][6][9] Extracurriculars include Freiwilligendienste (e.g., FSJ), Schülervertretung, Jahrgangsteams, Berufsberatung, Oberstufenberatung, Vertretungsplan.[1] Community initiatives: "Schule ohne Rassismus – Schule mit Courage" (since 2011), "Faire Schule" certification (2013) for global justice and sustainability ("global denken – lokal handeln").[4][5] Partnerships, Gottesdienste, rituals, and environmental efforts enhance social experiences.[3]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
ESK reports strong **Abitur results**: 2024 average **2.0**, 2023 **1.9**, 2022 **1.8**, 2021 **1.9**, 2020 **1.8**, 2019 **2.01** – all above Berlin Gymnasium average and top in Köpenick district.[5] Certifications: "Faire Schule" (epiz, 2013); "Schule ohne Rassismus – Schule mit Courage" (since 2011, part of Germany's largest school network with 1000+ schools).[4][5] State-recognized Ersatzschule with equivalent qualifications.[3]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
**Admissions**: Open for Quereinstieg in grades 9-11 (contact Sekretariat).[1] For grade 5/7: Submit copies by **01.12.2025** (application form, Zeugnisse from grade 1/3, Geburtsurkunde, Taufurkunde if available, Passfoto, engagement certificates); by **31.01.2026** (Halbjahreszeugnis grade 4/6, Förderprognose, Sec I Anmeldung).[1] Grade 5: Optional info talks **10.01.2026, 11-15 Uhr**; grade 7: Mandatory tests **14.01.2026 & 20.01.2026, 16-17 Uhr** (register from 10.11.2025).[1] If Förderprognose <2.2 average, Senate probeunterricht required.[1] Forms: https://esk.schulstiftung-ekbo.net/wp-content/uploads/sites/13/2021/12/Anmeldebogen_200325.pdf.[1]

**Fees**: No specific amounts listed on pages; "Elternbeitrag" mentioned but undefined.[1] A PDF form may contain details (likely fee-related: False per scrape, but check for updates).[1]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The school's mission centers on a **humanist and evangelical educational program** that integrates classical and modern foreign languages, natural sciences, music, art, and sports, fostering comprehensive personal development in the tradition of its 1574 founding.[1][2][3] Core values include independence, freedom, tolerance, respect, responsibility, and Christian ethics, with education viewed as more than knowledge transmission—emphasizing ancient languages for deeper understanding of modern culture, history, and Europe, alongside democracy education through history, politics, religion, and philosophy.[1][3] As an evangelical institution, it promotes Religionsunterricht, student-led Gottesdienste and Andachten, and social engagement like the 10th-grade Sozialpraktikum to encourage ethical action and community involvement.[1][3]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 1574 as the Berlinisches Gymnasium zum Grauen Kloster in a former Franciscan monastery, it is Berlin's oldest Gymnasium; the modern Evangelisches Gymnasium was established in 1949 in Tempelhof, moved to Schmargendorf in 1954, and adopted its current name in 1963, continuing the historical tradition.[1][2][7] Located in Berlin's Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf district (Schmargendorf) at Salzbrunner Str. 41, 14193 Berlin, it operates under the Evangelische Schulstiftung EKBO as a state-recognized private Ersatzschule.[1][2][6] Facilities include new 2023 naturwissenschaftliche Fachräume for physics, biology, and chemistry experiments, supporting digitalization; the community features weekly Andachten, partnerships, and events like Holocaustgedenktag.[1][2]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
A **Gymnasium** offering grundständiger Bildungsgang from 5th grade (Sexta, three classes) and a new one from 7th grade (Quarta, starting 2025/26 with 28 places), leading to Abitur after 12 years; no mentions of MSA or BBR.[1][2][4] Languages: From 5th—English + Latin, 8th—Ancient Greek, 9th—French (elective), 10th—Hebrew (leading to Hebraicum in 12th); Latinum by 10th, Graecum by 11th.[1][2] Other features: Teilungsgruppen in middle school for sciences/math/languages; Profilkurse in 10th; project-based art/music in 8th (e.g., photography, dance, songwriting); subjects span Religion, languages, math-sciences (Math, Physics, Chemistry, Biology/NaWi), social sciences (History, Politics, Geography, Philosophy), and arts-sports (Art, Drama, Music, Dance, Sport).[1]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars include AGs (e.g., Debattier AG, Jugend debattiert, Juniorwahlen), Altsprachenfest with ancient texts via music/art/drama, Religionsprojekttag, Orchester/Chöre, Weihnachtskonzert, music/tanzfahrten, theater/tanz/musik projects, Klosterspiele (athletics/team challenges), and top regional Rudern/Hockey.[1] Sports emphasize teams like Fußball/Volleyball for community; arts foster holistic Bildung; social experiences via Sozialpraktikum (10th grade), Silentium, Schülerberatung.[1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Landesweit top in Rudern and Hockey; 2024 Doppeljubiläum (450 years Gymnasium zum Grauen Kloster, 75 years Evangelisches Gymnasium); new Bildungsgang ab 7th grade expands to fourzügig school with diverse Oberstufe offerings; Altsprachenfest 2024 highlighted vibrant classics teaching; no specific awards/rankings or exam outcomes detailed.[1][2][4][7]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
Admissions for 2026/27: Applications from Dec 2025; for 5th grade—submit Dec-Jan: grades 1-3 (incl. behavior), birth/baptism certificates, religion/engagement proofs; for 7th—by Feb 13, 2026: grades 1-6, Förderprognose, same certificates; personal interview with leadership/Schulpfarrerin required (starts Dec 4, 2025).[1] Criteria: Good grades across subjects (not sole decider), school-child fit, prior Religionsunterricht preferred; church membership desirable but not required; open to other faiths; submit by post/sekretariat (Tel: 030-8254011, 8:30-15:00); Anmeldeformular available.[1] **No tuition/fee amounts specified** in provided content; mentions Elternbeitrag, Elternhilfswerk, but details likely in PDF: https://eggk.schulstiftung-ekbo.net/wp-content/uploads/form_anmeldung.pdf (not confirmed fee-related).[1]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Felix-Mendelssohn-Bartholdy-Gymnasium aims to open manifold perspectives for students' school and professional development, enabling individual profiling based on their interests and abilities through a versatile course offering, with special emphasis on musical-artistic and social science education.[1][4][5] Core values include mutual respect, consideration, openness, and tolerance; promotion of social competence, activity, creativity, and independence; and a performance-oriented learning process using modern methods like interdisciplinary projects, method training, communication training, and team training.[4] The school identifies as a learning space and living environment fostering joint engagement among students, teachers, and parents in school and extracurricular activities.[4]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Located in Prenzlauer Berg, Pankow district, at Pasteurstr. 7, 10407 Berlin, the school serves approximately 1,309 students and 109 teachers as of 2023/24, with steady enrollment growth from 767 students in 2012/13.[3] It is a public gymnasium owned by Bezirksamt Pankow, led by Hr. Tschiche, with no founding date specified in available content.[2][3] Facilities include two computer rooms, a library, an aula for concerts, and spaces across three floors for open house events; a tablet class is in testing.[4][6] Community ties involve partnerships with local elementary schools like Homer-Grundschule and external organizations such as Musikschule Hugo Distler e.V. and Sportverein Pfefferwerk e.V.[6]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a standard German Gymnasium, it covers Sekundarstufe I (grades 7-10) and II (grades 11-13), offering Abitur as the primary graduation pathway, with strong focus on musical-artistic (music, visual arts) and social sciences (history, geography, politics, ethics, psychology) disciplines from grade 7 via mandatory elective courses (Wahlpflichtangebote).[1][3][4][5] Languages include English (from S1), French, Spanish, Latin, and Japanese as third options; Leistungskurse (advanced courses) in Art, Biology, German, English, Geography, History, Mathematics, Music, Spanish.[3][6] Additional features: career orientation integrated across subjects (e.g., job applications in German class, Gläsernes Labor visits, student firm "Cafe Tholdy"), digital tablet use in select classes, and berufsorientierte elements like internships (Praktika).[2][8]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars emphasize music with ensembles (Big Band, choirs like Mittelstufenchor, flute trio), pre-Christmas and summer concerts, and performances at local schools; other AGs (working groups) include instrumental groups, sculpture, history workshop, student club, Norwegian, and politics AG (e.g., "Denkraum Klima" project on climate-neutral urban living).[1][6] Exchanges and partnerships with schools in Ecuador (first exchange 2025 to Cuenca), France (Aix-en-Provence, Paris), Spain (Picassent), Japan (Tokyo), Norway (Oslo, Nannestad); sports via Pfefferwerk e.V.; theater like "Der Sandmann"; open house days showcasing all subjects.[6][9] Student testimonials highlight community, music passion, and teacher support during events.[9]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Abitur averages are consistently strong: 2.31 (2024), 2.29 (2023), 2.13 (2022), with success rates 98-100% in recent years; ranks 7th among Pankow gymnasiums with 2.31 average.[3][7] Enrollment demand high (133% first-choice placement rate).[7] Active in cultural events (e.g., 2025 Christmas concerts, Ecuador exchange) and projects (e.g., Politik-AG climate initiative featured in F.A.Z.).[3][9] No specific awards or certifications noted.

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a tuition-free public school, enrollment for grade 7 involves booking an appointment during the period March 6-14, 2025, via the school's Termin FMBG system; open house (Tag der offenen Tür) allows parents and prospective students to tour three floors, meet students/teachers, and learn about Sekundarstufe I/II offerings and extracurriculars.[2][9] High demand with 298 first-choice applications for 224 places.[7]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Fichtenberg-Oberschule emphasizes an **inclusive, socially competent school environment** with a positive school climate and respectful interactions among students, staff, and parents.[3][4] Its educational approach, outlined in the **Schulprogramm** developed collaboratively by teachers, parents, and students since 2006 (revised in 2018 and updated through 2025), focuses on organizational principles, performance standards, extracurricular learning sites, student participation, and digital learning.[Curriculum Page] The school promotes problem-oriented instruction, team-based development, and continuous improvement, including support networks like the "Fichte-Netz" for student challenges.[3][4]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 1904 as a higher girls' school in Steglitz, the institution moved to its current protected historic building at Rothenburgstr. 18 in 1912 (as Kaiserin Auguste Victoria-Lyzeum), was renamed Auguste-Viktoria-Schule in 1924, adopted its present name in 1956, and opened to boys in 1964.[1][4] Serving approximately **775-809 students** with 76-80 teachers in Berlin's Steglitz-Zehlendorf district, it integrates blind and visually impaired students since 1979 in mainstream classes with accommodations like extra support in sciences and exams.[1][2][4] Facilities include modernized classrooms via a recent sanierung (renovation) with cluster learning spaces, science labs, wandelhalle (circulation halls), aula, turnhalle (gym), library, cafeteria run by parents and a provider, and a mensa.[3][5] Located in a green residential area near the city center, it attracts students from surrounding districts.[4]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a **Gymnasium** (grades 7-12/13), it leads to **Abitur** (university entrance qualification), with strong historical Abitur results (e.g., averages 2.35-2.51, success rates 92-100% from 2013-2017).[1][2][7] Core subjects include Deutsch, Mathematik (4 hours/week each in grades 7-10), Englisch, plus options like Französisch, Spanisch, Latein as second/third languages (S1: Englisch/Französisch; S4: Englisch/Spanisch).[2][7] Leistungskurse (advanced courses) offered: Bildende Kunst, Biologie, Chemie, Deutsch, Englisch, Erdkunde, Französisch, Geschichte, Mathematik, Musik, Physik, Politische Weltkunde, Sozialwissenschaften.[2] Special features: Inclusion for visual impairments, Willkommensklasse (welcome class for newcomers), digital learning principles, außerschulische Lernorte (off-site learning), and profiles in Gesellschaftswissenschaften/Sozialwissenschaften.[Curriculum Page][6] Weekly hours: 33-35 in lower grades, rising in upper levels.[7]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in theater trips (e.g., "Minus 16" at Deutsche Oper, "An die Wand" on antisemitism), arts projects on world religions and theater, Spanish cultural events (paella), and publications like **Fichtenblatt** newsletter.[Main Page] Extracurriculars include AGs (working groups), sports, and cultural outings; school initiatives like "Schule ohne Rassismus" (school without racism), Förderverein (parent association), MGB Welcome², Streitschlichter (peer mediators), and Krisenteams.[Main Page][6] Problem-oriented lessons encourage discussion and creativity; additional hours in geography/social studies for grade 7, project courses in grade 9; full participation for inclusive students except adapted sports/art.[1][3]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
**Schulinspektion evaluation (March 2024)**: Rated **A** (top grade) in 12 of 15 quality areas, B in 3; strengths include positive climate, inclusion success, leadership, team development, learning conditions, and partnerships.[Curriculum Page][3][4] Annual student-led teacher evaluations for over 10 years show trends in teaching quality.[Curriculum Page] Historic Abitur excellence (e.g., 100% pass rate in 2015).[2] Recognized for pioneering visual impairment integration since 1979 and first protected building renovated under Berlin's school construction offensive.[1][5]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **tuition-free public Gymnasium**, admission for four new 7th-grade classes (128 students/year) is based solely on the **Grundschule Förderprognose** (primary school recommendation/prognosis); enrollment info provided for new 7th graders.[School Information][9] Open house (Tag der offenen Tür) on January 22, 2026, and trial classes (Schnupperunterricht) on January 23, 2026, available.[Main Page]

description:
# Französisches Gymnasium Berlin - School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

The **Französisches Gymnasium** is dedicated to providing **multilingual education in a multicultural environment** to discover and foster students' talents, enabling them to become engaged and enlightened global citizens.[2] The school's core values are **openness, respect, collaboration, and critical thinking**.[2]

The institution emphasizes **academic excellence** guaranteed through its status as an EGD (Etablissement en Gestion Directe) of the AEFE network, ensuring curriculum compliance with French educational standards.[7] The school offers **general education** that enables students to develop a critical mind and achieve excellent skills across languages, natural sciences, and humanities.[7]

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Founding and Historical Significance**

The Französisches Gymnasium is **the oldest public school in Berlin**, founded in **1689 by Elector Frederick III of Brandenburg** for children of Huguenot families fleeing religious persecution in France.[1][3] Originally called the *Collège français*, it was established under the leadership of French jurist Charles Ancillon in a rented building on Stralauer Straße.[1]

**Location and Campus**

The school is located at **Derfflingerstraße 7, 10785 Berlin**, in the Tiergarten district (Ortsteil) of Mitte borough (Bezirk).[1][6] The school moved to its current building in 1972 after being located in Berlin-Reinickendorf.[3] The campus comprises three facilities: the main gymnasium, the Voltaire School, and a preschool.[2]

**Student Population and Community**

The school serves approximately **810 students** from **50 different nations**, with most students of German or French heritage.[1][2] The student body includes francophone learners from approximately 25-50 nations.[1][4] The school employs **115 teachers**.[2]

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**Grade Structure and Language of Instruction**

The school offers **continuous high-quality education from grades 5 through 12**.[7] Students are admitted in grades 5 and 7 into separate German and bilingual (German/French) classes.[9] **From grade 7 onwards, French becomes the language of instruction** across all subjects.[1][4][6]

**Language Programs**

- **Intensive French instruction** beginning in grade 5[5]
- **English** from grade 6[5]
- **Spanish** as an elective option from grade 8[5]
- **Latin and Ancient Greek** offered[3][5]

**Academic Specializations**

The curriculum includes:[5]

- **Modern languages gymnasium** advantages with comprehensive foreign language offerings
- **Advanced science instruction** with options for specialized science tracks (Mathematics, Physics-Chemistry, Natural Sciences)
- **Humanities and social sciences** subjects offered more broadly than at other Berlin gymnasiums, including Philosophy in grade 12 and Economics as a multi-track advanced course
- **History and Geography** with enhanced representation in middle school
- **MINT-Profile** (Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics)[2]

**Graduation Pathways**

Students can earn **five different diplomas**:[2]

1. **Diplôme National du Brevet** (French middle school diploma)
2. **Mittlerer Schulabschluss (MSA)** (German middle school certificate)
3. **Baccalauréat** (French high school diploma)
4. **Abitur** (German high school diploma)
5. **AbiBac** (combined German-French diploma)

Students can complete both the Abitur and Baccalauréat in French or German language.[1] The school reports **excellent results** in both examinations, with Abitur averages that are **outstanding in Berlin comparison**.[5]

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**Extracurricular Activities**

The school offers **working groups in theater, writing workshops, and science programs**.[7] Additional activities include:[2]

- **Theater, arts, and music** cultural activities
- **Exchange trips and language journeys** to European destinations
- **Erasmus+ program participation** since 2019, providing enriching international experiences to develop student independence, language skills, and intercultural competence[2]
- **"Maths en Jeans"** long-term mathematics project with international congress participation
- **Bullying awareness programs** developed by students
- **Sustainability initiatives** engaged since 2018 as part of a global sustainable development project[2]

**Field Studies and Exchanges**

Notable programs include geology study trips to the Alps, exchanges to Spain, and study trips to destinations such as Cherbourg and Dublin.[2]

**Sports and Community Engagement**

The school participates in events such as the **Mini Marathon Berlin**.[2]

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

**Certifications and Labels**

The school holds the following designations:[2]

- **AEFE** (Agence pour l'Enseignement Français à l'Etranger) - one of 14 schools in Germany with this accreditation
- **MINT-Profile** (Science and technology focus)
- **PASCH** (Schools: Partners for the Future)
- **Erasmus+** program participant
- **Sustainable development school**

**Academic Performance**

The school reports **excellent examination results** across both German and French diplomas, with **outstanding Abitur averages compared to other Berlin gymnasiums**.[5]

**Historical Recognition**

The Französisches Gymnasium is recognized as **the world's oldest French gymnasium in the AEFE network** and maintains a **long tradition of excellence**.[2]

## 6. ADMISSIONS

**Enrollment Process**

Students are admitted in **grades 5 and 7**.[4][9] The school offers **trial days (Schnuppertage)** for prospective students to experience a day at the gymnasium.[2]

**Admission Requirements**

Prospective students should:[5]

- Be **open, eager to learn, and enthusiastic**
- Have an **appropriate promotion prognosis (gymnasium recommendation)**
- Be **willing to intensively engage with French language and culture**

**Contact Information**

Families can contact the school through the German or French secretariat to arrange a visit and discuss enrollment.[2]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Freie Schule Anne-Sophie (FSAS) aims to create responsible, open-minded individuals who shape the future through an innovative, bilingual learning environment that fosters mindfulness, tolerance, critical thinking, self-responsibility, and sustainable, equitable action in a changing world.[2][5] Its philosophy emphasizes holistic qualification via individualized coaching, social competence development, self-organized and goal-oriented learning, media literacy, and four core attitudes: appreciation (Wertschätzung), mindfulness (Achtsamkeit), willingness to learn (Lernbereitschaft), and confidence (Zuversicht).[2][3][5] The school positions itself as a state-recognized substitute school in private ownership, prioritizing personality development so "every child leaves as a winner," with a focus on bilingualism, digital responsibility, and social skills in a modern, learning-friendly setting.[1][2][5][7]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 2011 in Berlin with a bilingual primary school and gymnasium; expanded in 2022 to include an entry class (Eingangsklasse).[5] Located at Clayallee 328-334, 14169 Berlin (Steglitz-Zehlendorf district), in a quiet residential area near Monroe-Park, it operates as a full-day school (Ganztagsschule) with modern, aesthetically designed learning spaces, excellent IT equipment, and facilities supporting self-organized learning.[1][2][3][4][9] The community includes "learning partners" (students), coaches/teachers, and emphasizes trust-based relationships, small classes, personal coaching, school festivals, clubs, excursions, and class trips to build community and respect.[1][2][4][5] A second campus exists in Künzelsau, but this profile focuses on Berlin.[6]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Offers bilingual (German-English) full-day education from **Eingangsklasse** (entry class), **Grundschule** (grades 1-6), to **Gymnasium** (grades 7-12), culminating in **Abitur** (recent 2025 graduates noted).[2][4][5][6][10] Half of subjects are taught in German, half in English from grade 1, integrating standard curriculum with self-organized learning, project work, interdisciplinary projects, media competence, digital tools, elective subjects (Wahlpflichtfächer), and personal coaching.[1][2][4][8] State-recognized as a substitute school (Ersatzschule), it emphasizes goal-oriented learning in a structured environment, with full-day operations mandatory from grade 3.[2][5] No mentions of MSA or BBR pathways.[3]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars include school festivals, working groups (AGs), clubs, excursions, class trips, and a bound full-day program with after-school care (Hort), promoting community and social skills.[1][2][4][5] Digital focus features responsible, creative media use with top IT infrastructure; bilingual immersion from entry level; personalized coaching and feedback for self-directed learning.[1][2][4][7] Recent events: Halloween ball, school start activities, guest visits (e.g., Staatsminister Gunther Krichbaum).[2] Sports, arts, or exchanges not explicitly detailed, but broad electives and projects support talent development.[3][4]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
State-recognized substitute school; awarded **"Exzellente digitale Schule"** (Excellent Digital School), **"Smart School"**, and **"Apple Distinguished School"** for innovative methods and technology integration.[2][4] Described as "outstanding" private primary and gymnasium with excellent bilingual curriculum; 2025 Abitur graduates celebrated.[1][2][4] Parent review notes rapid language progress (English speaker fluent in German in 6 months).[7] No specific rankings, test scores, or university placement data provided.[1][2]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
Admissions involve info events (next open day: 14.02.2026, weather-dependent), with a "Zur Anmeldung" (to registration) call-to-action and contact form (GDPR-compliant).[2] No detailed requirements listed. Fees are **income-dependent (einkommensabhängiges Schulgeld) starting from 100 Euro** (exact period unspecified); no specifics on registration, materials, meals, or after-school care. No PDFs with fee details found.[2] Scholarships not mentioned.

poi_primary_school_01_name: Finkenkrug school funding priority Spiritual Development
description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The school's mission emphasizes a democratic tradition inspired by Friedrich Ebert, the first President of the Weimar Republic, fostering a community committed to humanistic values, diversity, mindfulness, tolerance, digital learning, and sustainable action.[2] Core values include openness to over 40 nations, respect for all, curiosity, and preparing students for a humane future through 21st-century competencies like mindful interaction and planetary stewardship.[2] The educational approach prioritizes a tolerant, world-open environment where diverse learners and 75 educators collaborate to maximize potential.[2]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
The main building dates to 1906, with a new building added in 2005; the campus includes a protected former director's villa and sports facilities, all surrounded by greenery near the idyllic Fennsee in Wilmersdorf, creating a friendly, sheltered atmosphere.[2] The community comprises over 800 students from Wilmersdorf and other Berlin districts, representing over 40 nations, alongside 75 teachers focused on respectful, inclusive interactions.[2] A photographic tour of the campus is available on the website.[2]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a Gymnasium, it serves Sekundarstufe I (grades 7-10) and Sekundarstufe II (grades 11-12), leading to Abitur; detailed schedules include Stundentafeln, Wahlpflichtfächer, Leistungskurse, Zusatzkurse, and an Abitur-Broschüre for each stage, plus berufsorientierung (career orientation).[2] Subjects span Bildende Kunst, Biologie, Chemie, Darstellendes Spiel, Deutsch, Ethik/Religion, Fremdsprachen, Gesellschaftswissenschaften, Informatik, Mathematik, Musik, Philosophie, Physik, Sport, and Willkommensklassen.[2] Daily schedule runs from 8:00 to 17:35 with breaks and double periods.[2] Pioneers in digital learning since the 1980s, certified kreidefrei since 2009, eEducationMasterplan Modellschule in 2008, and updated via Digitalpakt Schule since 2020.[2]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars include theater projects (e.g., "TIRADEN" January 27-29), Tag der offenen Tür (January 17), Weihnachtskonzerte (December 17/18), English-Leistungskurs cinema trips, Klimaparlament events with Luisa Neubauer and Antarctic links, boys' Sek I basketball in Alba Oberschulliga, and blog updates on school life.[2] Arts and culture feature Shakespeare Players partnership (Berlin) and music events.[2] Sports include on-campus facilities and team successes.[2] Exchanges: Erasmus-accredited since 2022 for teacher training and student programs; annual swap with Jämtlands Gymnasium (Östersund, Sweden) since 1994 for 11th graders, involving winter sports and Swedish school insights.[2] Additional supports: over 10 years of mindfulness training, 3 school social workers for stress/family issues, berufsorientierung with Jugendberufsagentur, Nachhaltigkeits-Projekte (solar panels, Klima-Check, Grünes Klassenzimmer since April 2023), fächerübergreifender Unterricht, Medienkompetenz, Patenprojekt, and teacher training.[2]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Erasmus accreditation since 2022; digital pioneers (vernetzte systems 1980s, eEducationMasterplan Modellschule 2008, kreidefrei 2009, Digitalpakt 2020); Nachhaltigkeits-Kurs initiatives since April 2023; sports success in Alba Oberschulliga; no specific Abitur pass rates, rankings, or awards detailed.[2]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
Public tuition-free school; prospective students (Erstwunsch-Schule) invited to personal Kennenlerngespräch (get-to-know-you meeting), with direct online sign-up; Tag der offenen Tür on January 17 for visits.[2]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The school's motto, **"Miteinander leben – voneinander lernen"** (Live together – learn from each other), emphasizes diversity as a driver for appreciative openness, learning readiness, and social community skills, with students from 36 nations.[2] It promotes a modern languages focus, bilingual teaching in Spanish and English often by native speakers, and individual support for high-achieving and struggling students through workshops and extracurriculars.[2][5] As an open all-day school since 2011/12, it offers voluntary extended programs (up to 4 days/week, 7 hours/day) including creative, sports, science, and language activities to foster holistic development.[2][5]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded on April 6, 1905, as Höhere Gemeinde-Knabenschule (later Reform-Realgymnasium in 1911), it evolved through name changes (e.g., Oberschule für Jungen in 1937, Manfred-von-Richthofen-Oberschule in 1938) and post-1951 as 1. OWZ with neusprachlich and mathematisch-naturwissenschaftlich tracks; Spanish teaching began in the 1930s.[3] Located in Berlin-Reinickendorf near the Weiße Stadt and Breitkopfbecken, it serves ~920-971 students (932 in one source) with 87 teachers in a 5-track (5-zügiges) setup, including a listed Neorenaissance main building (1905) and recent Neubau extension with 12 classrooms, common rooms, refectory, skylights, and integrated study areas.[1][2][3][4] Facilities include aula, theater workshop, film studio, keyboard room, sports hall, outdoor sports area with tartan track and climbing, cafeteria/mensa, group rooms, and theaterwerkstatt for assemblies.[6] Community support comes from the Förderverein (contact: foerderverein@feg.berlin) for ideal and financial aid where public funds lack.[scraped]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
A **Gymnasium** (grades 5-12/13, leading to **Abitur**), it admits two classes from grade 5 with Spanish emphasis and three profiled classes from grade 7; most classes are bilingual (subjects in Spanish/English).[1][5][6] Languages: S1 English/French, S4 English/Spanish, 3rd foreign: French/Latin/Spanish; Leistungskurse in Art, Biology, Chemistry, German, English, Geography, French, History, Math, Music, Physics, Political Science, Spanish.[1][6] Profiles cover Fremdsprachen, Deutsch/Kulturfächer, Mathe/Informatik, Naturwissenschaften, Gesellschaft, Sport, Gesundheit; bilingual Deutsch-Spanish (~330 students), enhanced Biology in English (grades 8-10).[1][2][3][8] Profil-courses (2 hours each, versetzungsrelevant) in Deutsch/Fremdsprachen, Naturwissenschaft/Mathe, Gesellschaftswissenschaften, Musik/Kunst.[7] Open all-day with Lernwerkstätten; Berufsorientierung via BvBO program, internships (grade 9), workshops (grades 9/11), Wirtschaftsführerschein.[3][6] Strong Abitur outcomes: averages 2.37-2.45 (2013-2017), success rates 94-100%.[1]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars (Arbeitsgemeinschaften) include ball sports, film music, Jugend forscht, cooking, keyboard, reading club, Model European Parliament, sewing, dance, sound technology.[6] Special trips: annual Krakau Auschwitz-Birkenau memorial (since 2016, 53 students in 2025) via "Jüdisches Leben" course; Schnuppertag (trial day, e.g., 24.01.2025 with Spanish, NaWi, Soziales).[scraped] Ganztag offers creative/sports/science/language AGs, dual learning, Komm auf Tour Parcour (grades 7/8); meal ordering available.[scraped][6] Events: Infoabend for grades 5/7 (27.11.2025), Tag der offenen Tür, semester starts with schedules in theaterwerkstatt.[scraped]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Recognized as a **"Schule besonderer pädagogischer Prägung"** due to grade 5/7 intakes; consistent strong Abitur results (e.g., 2.39 average/100% success in 2017).[1][2] Hosts reading competition (6th grades, 09.12.2025), Lesewettbewerb.[scraped] No recent awards or rankings specified beyond historical Abitur data (up to 2017).[1]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
Public tuition-free school; enrolls two grade 5 classes (Spanish focus) and additional grade 7 intakes; info/anmeldung via link on site.[scraped][2][5] Events: Schnuppertag (e.g., 24.01.2025, 8:50am Neubau), Infoabend (27.11.2025, 18:30 Aula) for 2025/26; parents pick up at 12pm.[scraped] Lernmittelfonds for materials; transition info from primary via external link.[scraped] Contact: feg@freenet.de, Tel +49 30 4955056, Schulleitung Dr. Hellmuth.[5]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Gabriele-von-Bülow-Gymnasium emphasizes a bilingual profile in **Deutsch-Englisch**, positioning itself as a **Europäische Begegnungsschule mit Polen** to foster international exchanges and cultural understanding.[1][2][4][9] It promotes talent development, individual interests, and **digital education**, alongside a strong focus on **MINT** (Mathematik, Informatik, Naturwissenschaften, Technik) subjects, as evidenced by its repeated "MINT-freundliche Schule" certification.[1][scraped content] The school's approach integrates rigorous gymnasial education with extracurricular support for gifted students (e.g., Begabungsförderung or BEgabi) and digital competencies, aiming to provide broad opportunities in STEM while nurturing practical music engagement and critical thinking.[1][4][5][scraped content]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in **1890** as a private girls' school, it became public in **1902** with two classes and 28 students, evolving into a Höhere Töchterschule by 1908 and moving to its current location in Tegel by 1911.[4] Located at **Tile-Brügge-Weg 63, 13509 Berlin (Reinickendorf district, Tegel neighborhood)**, it serves a community of approximately **988 students** and **89 teachers** (2023/24 data), with steady enrollment growth from 913 in 2012/13.[1][6] Facilities include an **Aula**, **Bibliothek**, **Cafeteria** (seeking volunteers), **Computerräume**, and **Aufenthaltsraum/Schülerclub**.[5][6][scraped content] It supports community initiatives like hospice fundraisers (e.g., €2000 raised in 2025) and maintains a **Förderverein**.[scraped content]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a **Gymnasium** (grades 7–12/13, leading to **Abitur**), it offers a standard canon including Deutsch, Mathematik, Naturwissenschaften, Gesellschaftswissenschaften, and Fremdsprachen, with **bilingual Deutsch-Englisch** from grade 7 (extra English, Geografie in English from grade 8) and options for 3rd languages: Französisch, Latein, or **Polnisch** (one of only two Berlin gymnasiums offering it through Abitur).[1][4][5][6] **Leistungskurse** (advanced courses) cover Bildende Kunst, Biologie, Chemie, Deutsch, Englisch, Erdkunde, Französisch, Geschichte, Informatik, Mathematik, Musik, Physik, and more.[1][6] Special programs include **MINT** emphasis, digital/iPad integration, **Begabtes Berlin** since 2020, **BvBO** for career orientation, and regional gifted groups.[4][5][6][8] Strong Abitur outcomes: averages 2.45–2.70 (2013–2017), success rates 97–100%.[1] No mention of MSA or BBR pathways, as it is a full Gymnasium.

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars emphasize **sports** (477 Sportabzeichen in 2025 for grades 7–10; 3rd fastest school in Reinickendorf sprints; athletics successes), **arts/music** (Adventskonzert with 140 participants, ensemble music, Band/Chor/Orchester, English Drama Group), and projects (Make@thon on Circular Economy, fake news workshops, Christmas cards for hospital patients).[1][3][5][6][scraped content] Exchanges include **Poland** (Europäische Begegnungsschule), Paris, and La Réunion; other activities: EYP, e-Twinning, Debating Society, Schach, Schülerzeitung "Dickes B", Hospiz-Ag, Schulsanitätsdienst.[4][5][6][scraped content] Events like Tag der offenen Tür, Projekttage, and Exkursionen enrich experiences.[scraped content]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
- **Digital education**: Gütesiegel confirmed for third time in 2026 for fostering digital competencies.[scraped content]
- **MINT**: "MINT-freundliche Schule" signet awarded three times, latest November 2024.[1][8][scraped content]
- **Abitur**: Excellent results (e.g., 2.45 average, 100% success in 2016/17).[1]
- **Sports**: 477 Sportabzeichen (2025, >60% of grades 7–10); 3rd fastest in Reinickendorf; multiple podium finishes in sprints/jumps.[3][scraped content]
- **Community**: €2000 hospice donation (2025); strong Open Day interest.[scraped content]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **tuition-free public Gymnasium**, admission for grade 7 requires a **Gymnasialempfehlung** from primary school; in oversubscription, prioritized by **Förderprognose average grade**.[6][7] For 2026/27, six grade 7 classes; online booking from **16 February 2026**, in-person March 2026 (past year: five classes, booking from 17 February 2025).[scraped content][7] Phone appointments for special cases (030-210052460); FAQ, forms available online. No tests mentioned except per FAQ.[7]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Gebrüder-Montgolfier-Gymnasium positions itself as a place of **learning, creativity, and collaborative shaping**, emphasizing the development of individual strengths through a broad spectrum of subjects while fostering curiosity, initiative, and responsibility.[5] Its core values include **respect** in interactions, **curiosity** toward knowledge and diversity, **responsibility** for actions, and **courage** to explore new paths, inspired by the Montgolfier brothers' inventive spirit.[3][5] The school's Leitbild (educational guiding principle) is a lived attitude rather than a static text, centered on a strong **school community** involving teachers, students, parents, and staff to create a vibrant, modern, and equitable learning environment.[5]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in **1983** as a 10-class polytechnic high school "Wilhelm Pieck" in Berlin-Lichtenberg, the school evolved post-reunification into a comprehensive school with a gymnasial upper level in the 1990s and became an independent **Gymnasium in 2000**, adopting its current name to symbolize research, innovation, creativity, and technical vision.[3] Located in Berlin's **Treptow-Köpenick district** (Johannisthal neighborhood) at **Ellernweg 20-22, 12487 Berlin**, it serves approximately **917 students** with **76 teachers** as of 2023/24.[1][3] Facilities include modernized classrooms, open learning zones, a library with research stations, three computer rooms, a language lab with 11 workstations, accessibility features (parking for disabled, wheelchair elevator, accessible WC), and a **30 kWp solar installation** covering 37% of energy needs and reducing CO2 emissions.[1][3][4][6] The school holds titles as a **"MINT-friendly school"** (STEM-focused) and **"Digital School"**.[3]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a **Gymnasium** starting from **7th grade** (ages 12/13), it offers a musisch-sprachbetontes Profil (music- and language-emphasized) with pathways to **Abitur** (university entrance qualification); no mentions of MSA or BBR.[1][2][4] Subjects span languages (**Deutsch, Englisch, Französisch**; S1: Englisch/Französisch, S5: Französisch/Englisch, 3rd/4th: Latein/Russisch), social sciences (**Geschichte, Politische Bildung, Geografie, Psychologie, Ethik**), **MINT** (Mathematik, Informatik, Biologie, Chemie, Physik), arts/music (**Bildende Kunst, Musik, Darstellendes Spiel**), and sports.[1][5] Leistungskurse (advanced courses) include Bildende Kunst, Biologie, Chemie, Deutsch, Englisch, Erdkunde, Französisch, Geschichte, Mathematik, Physik, Politische Bildung.[1][4] Features teilgebundener Ganztagsbetrieb (part-time all-day program), Wahlpflichtkurse (elective compulsory courses), and interdisciplinary projects.[4][5]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars emphasize arts (orchestra and guitar classes with semi-annual concerts, theater, choir, dance, band, ceramics), languages (DELF/Cambridge prep), STEM (LEGO-Mindstorms), and sports (volleyball, basketball, broad movement offerings, teams, fitness).[1][4][5] Events include Projektwoche, Tag der offenen Tür, Fremdsprachenfest, Big Challenge, Kennenlernfahrt for 7th grade, Spendenlauf charity run, and Elternabend.[5] Special focuses on creative projects, exhibitions, concerts, performances; partnerships with Musikschule Treptow-Köpenick, GHOST e.V., Studienkreis, and a school in Jonava, Lithuania.[4]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
**Abitur averages** (2014-2024) range from **2.18 to 2.52** (excellent on German 1.0-6.0 scale), with success rates of **91-100%**.[1] Recognitions include **"MINT-freundliche Schule"**, **"Digitale Schule"**, and **"Schule ohne Rassismus - Schule mit Courage"** (School without Racism - School with Courage).[3][4] Enrollment grew from 847 (2017/18) to 949 (2022/23), stabilizing at 917 (2023/24).[1]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **public, tuition-free** school, admission is open; key process includes **Anmeldungszeitraum Kl. 7** (March 5-12) and **Tag der offenen Tür** (e.g., Jan 24, 10:00-13:00).[5] 7th-grade Kennenlernfahrt supports new student integration.[5]

description:
# Georg-Büchner-Gymnasium Berlin - School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

The Georg-Büchner-Gymnasium is guided by a humanistic mission rooted in a quote from Georg Büchner's novella *Lenz*: "One must love humanity to penetrate the unique essence of each individual; no one should seem too insignificant, too ugly—only then can one understand them."[5] This philosophy emphasizes deep understanding of individual students and inclusive community values.

The school identifies itself as a **"Schule ohne Rassismus – Schule mit Courage"** (School Without Racism – School with Courage)[1], reflecting its commitment to anti-racism education and social responsibility. The school's educational approach prioritizes **community spirit** (*Gemeinschaftsgedanke*), which is embedded in various school events and activities.[1]

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Founding and Development:**
The school was established on August 19, 1974, initially as a branch of the Ulrich-von-Hutten-Gymnasium (UvH). It became an independent gymnasium in the 1976/77 school year under the leadership of Dr. Segner.[1] The school was officially named **Georg-Büchner-Oberschule (GBO)** on December 16, 1978, after the German writer and philosopher Georg Büchner.[1]

**Location and Facilities:**
The school is located in **Lichtenrade**, a district in the **Tempelhof-Schöneberg** borough of Berlin.[1] The campus includes sports facilities, with outdoor areas that were upgraded in 2024 with modern furniture installations.[8] The school features a **spectacular building** that houses over 300 interactive science and technology exhibits (referenced through student visits to the Phaeno museum, suggesting similar hands-on learning spaces).[2]

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**Bilingual Program (German-English):**
The school's defining feature is its **bilingual profile** (Deutsch-Englisch), established since the 1995/96 school year.[1] In 2010, this expanded from a specialized track to a comprehensive bilingual profile affecting all seventh-grade classes.[1]

The bilingual curriculum includes:
- **Grades 7-8:** Enhanced English instruction with increased weekly hours and partial small-group teaching[1]
- **Grade 8 (second semester onwards):** Geography taught in English[1]
- **Grade 9 onwards:** History taught in English[1]
- **Upper secondary (Oberstufe):** Political science available in English; English advanced course no longer mandatory[1]
- **Certification:** Students receive a "bilingual certificate" upon completion of Grade 10 or with their Abitur, documenting which subject courses were taught in English[1]

**Specialized Programs:**
- **Mathematics-Natural Sciences (MatNat) Classes:** Specialized tracks with enhanced STEM focus[2]
- **Media Track (Medienzweig):** Dedicated program for media literacy and production[2]
- **Arts and Music Emphasis (musisch-künstlerischer Schwerpunkt):** Strong focus on creative and performing arts[2]
- **International Class:** Support for students with diverse linguistic backgrounds[1]

**Additional Academic Support:**
- Methodology training (*Methodentraining*)[1]
- Career and university guidance (*Studien- und Berufsberatung*)[1]
- Digital learning initiatives, significantly expanded during the COVID-19 pandemic (2020-2021)[1]

**Graduation Pathways:**
As a gymnasium, the school prepares students for the **Abitur** (German university entrance qualification). The school also offers support for students pursuing alternative pathways through its comprehensive guidance programs.

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**Extracurricular Activities:**
The school offers diverse **working groups (AGs)** that remain open to new members throughout the year, including:
- **Music:** School bands, instrumental courses, and jazz ensembles[2]
- **Technology:** Technical support AG for school events[2]
- **Creative workshops:** "Glimmer & Shine" jewelry design workshop led by student Zeinab from Grade 9[2]
- **Sustainability:** Sustainability AG conducting urban tours in Stuttgart[4]
- **Film:** Documentary filmmaking workshops exploring school topics[1]

**Sports:**
- Annual Christmas volleyball tournament featuring Grade 10 classes, upper secondary students, and faculty[2]
- Competitive handball teams qualifying for regional finals[2]
- Fair play and teamwork emphasized in all sporting activities[2]

**Cultural and Social Events:**
- Christmas Jam concert featuring school bands and instrumental performances[2]
- Christmas festival organized by student volunteers[2]
- Prevention-focused parent evenings addressing topics like eating disorders[1]
- Field trips and excursions (e.g., visits to the Phaeno science museum in Wolfsburg)[2]

**International Exchange Programs:**
The school maintains active **sister school partnerships**, including exchanges with Australian schools (Fort Street High School in Sydney), where students spend two weeks with host families experiencing both German and Australian cultures.[6]

**Pastoral Care and Support:**
- **School social work** (*Schulsozialarbeit*) and open all-day programs (*Offener Ganztag*)[1]
- Mental health and wellness support, including partnerships with organizations like Dick & Dünn e.V. for eating disorder prevention and counseling[1]
- Peer support and conflict resolution services[1]

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

**Recognition and Certifications:**
- **"School Without Racism – School with Courage"** designation, reflecting institutional commitment to anti-racism education[1]
- **Bilingual certification program** recognized across German educational institutions[1]
- **Solar energy initiative:** Participation in "Solar for Schools" program providing sustainability education[7]

**Athletic Achievements:**
- Handball team qualified as group leader for regional finals (as of March 2024)[2]
- Consistent participation and success in inter-school sports competitions[2]

**School Culture Recognition:**
The school maintains an **alumni culture** (*Alumnikultur*) and has developed a school hymn ("Die Schule, die am Stadtrand steht" / "The School at the City's Edge") reflecting strong institutional identity.[1]

## 6. ADMISSIONS

**Enrollment Process for Incoming Grade 7 Students:**

| Timeline | Event |
|----------|-------|
| January 19, 2026 | Information evening on school profile (18:00-20:00) |
| January 24, 2026 | Open house day (10:00-13:00) |
| February 19-27, 2026 | Registration period for classroom visits (Hospitationen) |
| March 5-12, 2026 | Official enrollment period |

**Classroom Visits (Hospitationen):**
The school offers prospective students the opportunity to observe classes in Grades 7 and 8 to gain insight into instruction and school culture. Registration for visits opens January 19, 2026.[1]

**Public School Status:**
As a public gymnasium in Berlin, the Georg-Büchner-Gymnasium is **tuition-free**. There are no enrollment fees or tuition charges. The school is funded through the Berlin public education system.[1]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Georg-Friedrich-Händel-Gymnasium is a music-focused public Gymnasium emphasizing musical education alongside comprehensive general education, producing an Abitur that qualifies graduates for any university.[1][6][7] Every student must join a school ensemble (choir, orchestra, or band), commit to instrumental or vocal training, and participate in mandatory music elective courses from grade 9 and as a Leistungskurs (advanced course) in the upper secondary phase, targeting musically gifted students from grade 5.[1][2][3][7]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 1906 as part of a higher girls' school (Lyzeum) in Berlin-Friedrichshain, the school moved to its current building at Frankfurter Allee 36/37 (now 6A) erected in 1907/1908; the gymnasium section merged with another school in 1934 before reestablishing post-1954.[1][6] Located in Berlin's Friedrichshain-Kreuzberg district near U-Bahnhof Frankfurter Tor, it serves ~531 students with 47 teachers in a protected historic building featuring a solar installation since 2019 for energy supply.[1][5][8] Facilities include an aula (auditorium) with ongoing construction documentation, supporting ensembles and events.[6]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
This grundständiges (from grade 5) Gymnasium offers grades 5-12/13 (G8/G9 Abitur pathways), with two main tracks from grade 5 and an additional track from grade 7; open all-day programs (OGB), social practicum in grade 10, and Zweiter Bildungsweg (second educational path).[1][2][5] Languages: English (S1 from grade 5), French (from grade 7), optional Latin (from grade 8); Leistungskurse in Biology, German, English, Mathematics, Music (mandatory).[2][3][7] Core subjects include extensive music (4 weekly ensemble hours, voice/instrumental training), art projects, biology/chemistry experiments, interactive math tools, physics demos, and electives like Wahlpflichtunterricht; Cambridge certificate available.[1][2][6][7]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars center on music with choirs (Händelkinderchor, Canta Chiara, be:one, Die Primaner, Voca), bands (Shikamini, Shikamana), orchestras (Kleines Orchester, Jugendorchester, Jugendsinfonieorchester), and ensemble rehearsals led by named teachers; international trips (Taiwan 2019, Italy/Romfahrt/Florenz 2018, France 2018, England, China/Peking 2025, Austria).[1][2][6] Other activities: theater (10th-grade productions at Theater Strahl, Musical Krauskopf), sports (festivals, ski courses), art projects (architecture, mimicry, sculptures), math competitions (Känguru, Olympiade), geography partnerships, Rome trips for Latin, school radio/robotics AGs, Förderverein events (summer festival), career counseling, and schulsozialarbeit.[2][3][6]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
The school consistently ranks among Berlin's top Gymnasien in Abitur results.[6] Recent successes include gold medals for choirs Canta Chiara and be:one at the 2025 "Musica Eterna Roma" international competition, cultural exchanges (Peking 2025), theater premieres, and strong performances in math competitions (Känguru, Olympiade, VERA 8).[3][6] Schulinspektion 2019 noted positive quality.[6]

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a tuition-free public school, enrollment starts in grade 5 for musically gifted students with prior instrumental or choral experience; applications cover timeframe, conditions, documents, and decisions, with Tag der offenen Tür (open house).[6][7][10] Quereinsteiger (lateral entry) possible if spots available from grade 7; new IServ server requires updated access data.[1][7]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Georg-Herwegh-Gymnasium is a state-funded general academic Gymnasium emphasizing well-equipped classrooms, specialized profiles in **MINT (mathematics, informatics, natural sciences, technology)** with a digital concept and **music**, alongside support for new students via remedial courses in math, English, and German for 7th graders[7][1]. It fosters a constructive, positive learning atmosphere where students interact cooperatively, teachers exhibit approachable behavior, and lessons prioritize subject content with high student motivation and performance orientation, as noted in the 2019 school inspection[6]. The school values broad extracurricular offerings from beekeeping to robot building and skiing, preparing students for academic and professional paths[7].

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in its current form in **1962** by merging a boys' and girls' higher school, the Gymnasium is located in Berlin's Reinickendorf district, Hermsdorf neighborhood, at Fellbacher Str. 18-19, 13467 Berlin, with a branch at Fichtestr. 14[2][1][3]. It serves approximately **911-938 students** (2025/26 school year) with **82-90 teachers**[7][1]. Facilities include a main building for grades 9-12 with free lockers, a new building for grades 7-8 with rentable lockers, a cafeteria (Mensa), 10 classrooms in the 2011 extension, and accessible features like ramps and elevators[2][Curriculum Page]. The community is leistungsorientiert (performance-oriented), with strong local ties evident in events like district Christmas celebrations[Main Page].

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a Gymnasium, it covers grades **7-12** (Sekundarstufe I and II), leading to **Abitur** after G8/G9 pathways, with no MSA or BBR mentioned as primary options[4][1]. Core languages: **English** (L1), **French or Latin** (L2); **Italian** as L3 from grade 9[1][7][3]. Special programs include one **MINT/natural sciences class** per grade (enhanced math, informatics, sciences; partnerships with TU Berlin, Berlin Zoo; projects like Chem-kids, Junior Science Olympiad) and one **music class** per grade[1][7][2]. Leistungskurse (advanced courses) in biology, chemistry, math, physics, music, English, French, German, history, geography, arts, politics[1][3][4]. Certifications: CAE (Cambridge Advanced English), DELF (French); method training, diving courses[7][3][4]. Vocational orientation is excellent, with structured programs across grades[5][6].

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars (AGs) span robotics, beekeeping, surfing, cross-country skiing, journalism, book clubs, debating, Italian, rock band, big band (GHG Jazz Owls), orchestras, choirs, theater workshops[7][Main Page][4]. Sports achievements include 8th consecutive strong performance in Berlin secondary schools' swimming competition, handball honors from Handball-Verband Berlin, hall handball regional, indoor soccer[Main Page][8]. Arts: Christmas concerts, chamber music evenings, big band double concerts, RBB TV appearance of rock band, Berliner Ensemble workshop[Main Page]. International exchanges with partner schools in Morges (Switzerland), Chicago (USA), Istanbul (Turkey), Falkenberg (Sweden)[3].

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
- **Schulinspektion 2019**: Positive atmosphere, motivated students, Abitur results at or above Berlin average (math above district average), strong intermediate exams[6].
- **Career orientation**: "Qualitätssiegel Berlin für exzellente berufliche Orientierung" (2016, 2021; 5-year validity each); 2024 "Botschafterschule" (lighthouse school) by Netzwerk Siegelschule[5][6][9].
- Sports: Handball ehrung, top swimming team (8x participation)[Main Page].
- Competitions: Mathe-Olympiade, RoboCup-Junior, Fremdsprachenwettbewerbe, Juvenes Translatores[8][4].

### 6. ADMISSIONS
As a **tuition-free public Gymnasium**, enrollment for 7th grade (2026) involves online or in-person booking of appointments via the website, followed by personal registration March 5-12 at the secretariat[Main Page]. Open house (**Tag der offenen Tür**) on January 16, 2026 (15:00-18:00); guided tours available to meet teachers and see facilities[Main Page][About Page]. No specific requirements beyond district assignment; preparatory courses ease entry[7].
